# GameZipper 100% Master Test Case Library
**Version**: 1.112.0
**Effective Date**: 2026-06-29
**Build**: R231 (Dynamic Test Intelligence 4h cron evolution)
**Library Owner**: Lead Test Engineer
**Version Note**: v1.108.0 = R227 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 9 new test cases from late-June 2026 industry research (June 28 2026): Chrome 145+ vertical-tabs chrome://flags/#vertical-tabs experiment (IT之家 Jan 16 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_703696a47e556652) progressively shipping as default — moves the browser tab strip from the TOP of the window to the SIDE, narrowing the game canvas's horizontal viewport by ~200-300px whenever the user has many tabs open; the BROWSER-VERTICAL-TABS-SIDE-INTRUSION-VIEWPORT-NARROWING dimension distinct from B-124 (Chrome 148 bookmark-bar top-intrusion — different direction: TOP not SIDE) and B-114 (iOS 27 device compatibility drop — different layer: device support not layout) because B-149 targets the SIDE-INTRUSION viewport dimension where GameZipper game canvases using percentage-width layouts will silently narrow when vertical tabs are enabled, breaking pixel-perfect sprite positioning and creating a new default-on-the-horizon layout class (BROWSER-VERTICAL-TABS-SIDE-INTRUSION-VIEWPORT-NARROWING) (B-149); macOS 27 Golden Gate super-wide 5K 120Hz dual-axis high-refresh × high-resolution rendering baseline (IT之家 June 9 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0956a27448443652; new.qq.com June 10 2026 new.qq.com/rain/a/20260610A02CE200) where Apple extended 5K (5120×2880) @ 120Hz support from standard 16:9 to super-wide 21:9 monitors via M5 MacBook Pro driving — the SUPER-WIDE-5K-120HZ-DUAL-AXIS-HIGH-REFRESH-HIGH-RESOLUTION-RENDERING dimension distinct from C-120 (macOS 27 super-wide 21:9 5K support — resolution axis only) and P-078 (iQOO Neo11 LTPO 1-144Hz mobile-display variable rAF — mobile not desktop) because C-155 is the DUAL-AXIS DESKTOP dimension where both 5K resolution AND 120Hz refresh rate apply simultaneously on super-wide external monitors, requiring GameZipper game canvas rAF timing verification at 8.33ms frame budget on 5120×2880 super-wide macOS 27 configurations (SUPER-WIDE-5K-120HZ-DUAL-AXIS-RENDERING-BASELINE) (C-155); iOS 27 4×6 super-large widget home-screen takeover viewport class (9to5Mac June 9 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_7456a274a5d16552) where Apple introduced a 4×6 EXTRA-LARGE widget size class beyond the prior 2×2 / 4×2 / 4×4 tiers — a new HOME-SCREEN-VIEWPORT-CLASS dimension distinct from W-155 (iOS 27 stable-ship full-stack regression — version-milestone not widget-size) and W-177 (Apple App Store AI-Agent Marketplace — marketplace not widget) because W-222 is the WIDGET-SIZE-CLASS-EXPANSION dimension where a 4×6 widget (3:2 portrait aspect) can host an inline GameZipper PWA widget with a completely new aspect ratio requiring distinct layout/canvas sizing logic for the widget context vs full-screen Safari context (IOS-27-SUPER-LARGE-WIDGET-HOME-SCREEN-VIEWPORT-CLASS-EXPANSION) (W-222); WebGPU/WebGL hybrid coexistence in 2026 mobile-gaming production baseline (永利麻将 case lcxw.cn May 22 2026 www.lcxw.cn/39@wiki/ZoCGH) where a major mobile game deployed WebGPU in production with WebGL fallback for cross-device compatibility — establishing the dual-rendering-path pattern as the 2026 industry-standard for mobile-game graphics migration — the WEBGPU-WEBGL-HYBRID-COEXISTENCE-PRODUCTION-BASELINE dimension distinct from P-068 (WebGPU streaming buffers progressive 3D-asset loading — pattern within WebGPU only) and P-073 (WebGPU vs WebGL client-side AI inference benchmarks — comparison not coexistence) because P-096 is the DUAL-RENDERING-PATH-COEXISTENCE dimension where GameZipper WASM/WebGPU exports must implement graceful runtime feature-detection + automatic WebGL fallback when WebGPU is unavailable, with consistent visual output across both paths (WEBGPU-WEBGL-HYBRID-COEXISTENCE-MOBILE-GAMING-PRODUCTION-BASELINE) (P-096); CVE-2026-1281 Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) CVSS ~9.8 unauthenticated remote-code-execution zero-day actively exploited in the wild (CSDN blog.csdn.net/zhengfei611/article/details/157618322 June 2 2026 — public PoC `python3 CVE-2026-1281.py https://mdm.target.com`) — single forged HTTP request achieves full server takeover and access to every managed iOS/Android device including those running GameZipper games through MDM-enrolled enterprise fleets; the MDM-SERVER-ZERO-DAY-ACTIVELY-EXPLOITED-AFFECTS-MANAGED-MOBILE-DEVICE-FLEETS dimension distinct from S-218 (CVE-2026-35616 FortiClient EMS preauth API-bypass — different vendor: Fortinet not Ivanti) and S-198 (Cisco FMC CVSS 10.0 deserialization — firewall management not endpoint manager) because S-222 is the IVANTI-EPMM-MOBILE-DEVICE-FLEET dimension where compromise cascades to every MDM-enrolled mobile device, including GameZipper players using corporate-managed iPhones/Androids whose MDM policy could be silently updated to block/hijack GameZipper traffic (MDM-SERVER-ZERO-DAY-CVE-2026-1281-IVANTI-EPMM-COMMAND-MOBILE-DEVICE-FLEET) (S-222); iOS 27 landscape-mode expansion preparing for iPhone Ultra foldable (sohu June 13 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0616a2cf58077852 — Apple Maps/Calendar/Files/Mail gain horizontal layouts; iOS 27 Beta 1 code contains foldState + angleDegrees strings per 9to5Mac) — the OS-LEVEL-LANDSCAPE-MODE-EXPANSION-FOLDABLE-PREP dimension distinct from C-120 (macOS 27 super-wide 5K — desktop not mobile) and G-160 (WebGL canvas auto-resize-on-window/orientation-change — generic resize not OS-driven) because C-156 is the OS-LEVEL-LANDSCAPE-UX-EXPANSION dimension where the upcoming iPhone Ultra foldable forces Apple to expand landscape-mode across the OS, raising player expectations for GameZipper landscape games to compete with native Apple landscape experiences (IOS-27-LANDSCAPE-MODE-EXPANSION-IPHONE-ULTRA-FOLDABLE-PREP) (C-156); iOS 27 lock-screen AI-personalized-dynamic-wallpaper visual-competition dimension (CNMO June 11 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2236a2a197d74952 — five major lock-screen interaction/visual upgrades including AI-generated personalized backgrounds, dynamic wallpapers, clock-layout changes, and interface-transparency adjustments) — the LOCK-SCREEN-AI-PERSONALIZED-WALLPAPER-VISUAL-COMPETITION dimension distinct from B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — different surface: Control Center not Lock Screen) and B-119 (iOS 26 broad-bug regression sweep — bug class not feature) because B-150 targets the LOCK-SCREEN-LAYER dimension where AI-generated dynamic wallpapers compete with GameZipper lock-screen widgets/notifications for visual attention, requiring QA to verify GameZipper lock-screen widgets remain legible against AI-personalized high-motion backgrounds (IOS-27-LOCK-SCREEN-AI-PERSONALIZED-WALLPAPER-VISUAL-LEGIBILITY-COMPETITION) (B-150); iOS 27/macOS 27 VoiceOver curtain-screen (VO-Shift-F11 on macOS / three-finger-triple-tap on iOS) audio-only-navigation accessibility-test dimension (Apple Support VO2726 June 16 2026 support.apple.com/zh-cn/guide/voiceover-guide/vo2726/web) — GameZipper games must remain PLAYABLE via audio cues alone when curtain-screen blanks the display entirely for privacy — the VOICEOVER-CURTAIN-SCREEN-AUDIO-ONLY-NAVIGATION-ACCESSIBILITY-TEST dimension distinct from A-041 (iOS 27 Liquid Glass transparency slider — partial-transparency not complete-blanking) and A-042 (WCAG 2.2 AAA conformance — standard not feature) because A-050 is the COMPLETE-VISUAL-REMOVAL-REQUIRES-AUDIO-ONLY-GAMEPLAY dimension where the entire screen goes black while the user continues interacting via VoiceOver audio, exposing any GameZipper game that lacks audio-only state feedback as unplayable under curtain-screen mode (VOICEOVER-CURTAIN-SCREEN-AUDIO-ONLY-NAVIGATION-ACCESSIBILITY-TEST-DIMENSION) (A-050).
**Version Note**: v1.106.0 = R225 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 8 new test cases from late-June 2026 industry research (June 28 2026): Chrome 146 DBSC (Device Bound Session Credentials) shipped April 10 2026 (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_80569d9164342752 April 10 2026; TechRadar) binds the user's auth session to the physical device via a WebAuthn-derived channel and encrypts the session cookie, marketed as "ending cookie theft attacks" — the BROWSER-NATIVE-DEVICE-BOUND-SESSION-CREDENTIAL-CLASS dimension distinct from G-164 (cross-ecosystem passkey sync — first-factor passwordless auth, not session-cookie binding) and C-151 (FedCM federated auth migration — IdP-mediated login, not device-bound session) because W-220 is the DEVICE-BOUND-SESSION-COOKIE layer that silently breaks any GameZipper feature relying on cross-device session resumption via copied/restored cookies (favorites, last-level, theme prefs) (BROWSER-NATIVE-DEVICE-BOUND-SESSION-CREDENTIAL-CLASS) (W-220); Chrome 149 stable ship June 17 2026 (v149.0.7827.115, onlinedown.net June 17 2026) introducing WebAssembly 2.0, WebGPU 2.0, V8 12.4 with AI-predictive GC, and Lighthouse 14 with Core Web Vital thresholds — the CHROMIUM-STABLE-SHIP-WEBASSEMBLY-2-AND-WEBGPU-2-LIGHTHOUSE-14-CORE-WEB-VITAL dimension distinct from B-117 (Chrome 149 stable ship — version-milestone dimension, duplicate) because C-153 targets the SPECIFIC-API-MILESTONE of WebAssembly 2.0 + WebGPU 2.0 + Lighthouse 14 thresholds that GameZipper WASM/WebGPU exports and CWV reporting must verify (CHROMIUM-STABLE-SHIP-WEBASSEMBLY-2-AND-WEBGPU-2-LIGHTHOUSE-14-CORE-WEB-VITAL) (C-153); Chrome V8 12.4 AI-predictive garbage collection + 25% more aggressive background-tab freeze (v149 release notes June 17 2026) — the BROWSER-AI-MEMORY-PREDICTION-BACKGROUND-TAB-FREEZE-AGGRESSION dimension distinct from P-089 (WKWebView evaluateJS) and P-086 (Burn & Measure GPU stress) because P-095 targets the SPECIFIC case where GameZipper games with active rAF/setInterval loops get suspended in backgrounded tabs and must survive the unfreeze-resume cycle without dropping timer IDs or breaking Web Audio context (BROWSER-AI-MEMORY-PREDICTION-BACKGROUND-TAB-FREEZE-AGGRESSION) (P-095); Browser Harness open-source April 27 2026 (7.2k stars, Claude Code directly drives Chrome to read/click/fill/navigate/upload/download in multi-step autonomous tasks — so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_92669ef2db811252 April 27 2026) — the BROWSER-AGENT-AUTOMATION-SURFACE-FROM-AGENT-FRAMEWORKS dimension distinct from W-204 (Chrome Skills user-saved AI prompts — passive prompt execution) and C-138 (Edge Copilot Actions — browser-native agent) because W-221 is the EXTERNAL-AGENT-FRAMEWORK dimension where third-party AI drives the browser, opening fake-click / reward-farming / impression-fraud / scripted-cheating abuse vectors on GameZipper UI (BROWSER-AGENT-AUTOMATION-SURFACE-FROM-AGENT-FRAMEWORKS) (W-221); document.execCommand('copy') UC Browser / QQ Browser / 360 Browser return-false breakage (blog.csdn.net/amyleeymy/article/details/108577480 Sept 2020, recurring 2026 as legacy API is deprecated in favor of navigator.clipboard) — the LEGACY-CLIPBOARD-API-DEPRECATION-WITH-PARTIAL-BROWSER-COVERAGE dimension distinct from C-152 (interpolate-size CSS feature) and C-150 (WebNN new API) because C-154 targets the DEPRECATION-MIGRATION of an EXISTING legacy API where GameZipper "share game" / "copy link" / "copy seed" buttons must have navigator.clipboard.writeText fallback or silently fail for users on UC/QQ/360 browsers (LEGACY-CLIPBOARD-API-DEPRECATION-WITH-PARTIAL-BROWSER-COVERAGE) (C-154); Adjust 2026 Game Insight Report April 22 2026 (sohu.com/a/1012550443_121752970) declaring USER RETENTION as the core 2026 mobile game growth strategy, with D1/D7/D30 retention as the canonical industry KPIs replacing the install-count metric — the INDUSTRY-RETENTION-METRIC-BASELINE-2026 dimension distinct from G-163 (Steam Summer Sale seasonal discoverability) and G-154 (UGC peak concurrency benchmark) because G-165 is the RETENTION-BENCHMARK dimension requiring GameZipper to instrument session-length telemetry and session-resume (continue last level on reload) per the 2026 industry baseline (INDUSTRY-RETENTION-METRIC-BASELINE-2026) (G-165); 2026 attack-cycle compression from weeks to hours with AI-automated attacker tooling + API attacks up 113% (toutiao.com/article/7626708037775950362 April 9 2026; ManageEngine Browser Security Plus May 25 2026) where Web + API + DDoS attacks have fused into standard SOP and automated scanning completes in hours not weeks — the ASSUME-BREACH-POSTURE-RATE-LIMIT-AND-WAF-HARDENING dimension distinct from S-218 (endpoint-management-server preauth bypass) and S-179 (Check Point generic auth bypass) because S-221 is the ATTACK-CADENCE-COMPRESSION dimension requiring GameZipper to rate-limit auth-like endpoints (favorites sync, analytics ingest, comment forms) and verify Cloudflare WAF rules block API-discovery patterns (path traversal, GraphQL introspection, JWT alg confusion) under the new sub-hour attack cycle (ASSUME-BREACH-POSTURE-RATE-LIMIT-AND-WAF-HARDENING) (S-221); Game Accessibility Guidelines 2026 refresh June 25 2026 (gameaccessibilityguidelines.com — supporting the industry since 2012) remains the canonical inclusive-design reference for games, with Basic/Intermediate/Advanced tier checklists covering remappable controls, color-blind safe palettes, motion-reduction options, captions, difficulty options — the CANONICAL-GAME-ACCESSIBILITY-GUIDELINES-2026-BASIC-TIER-COMPLIANCE dimension distinct from A-048 (EU EAA one-year enforcement anniversary — statutory regulation not guidelines) and A-034/A-038 (US ADA lawsuits — litigation not guidelines) because A-049 is the CANONICAL-GUIDELINE-LAYER that GameZipper games should self-audit against the BASIC tier (remappable controls, color-blind safe, motion-reduction toggle, captions) before relying on legal compliance (CANONICAL-GAME-ACCESSIBILITY-GUIDELINES-2026-BASIC-TIER-COMPLIANCE) (A-049).
**Version Note**: v1.105.0 = R224 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 10 new test cases from late-June 2026 industry research (June 27 2026): EU AI Act Phase 3 high-risk-system obligations enforcement deadline August 2 2026 (~5 weeks away as of June 27 2026) — the REGULATORY-DEADLINE-APPROACHING-FOR-AI-IN-GAMES dimension where games using AI for player profiling / adaptive difficulty / behavioral targeting may qualify as "high-risk AI systems" under the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) requiring conformity assessment, risk-management system, human oversight, CE marking, and post-market monitoring before the August 2 2026 obligations date; distinct from W-166 (China AI security compliance — different jurisdiction: EU not CN) and W-178 (analyst-validated agentic-phone era — market trend not regulation) because W-218 is the HARD-REGULATORY-DEADLINE dimension with statutory penalties up to 7% of global turnover (EU-AI-ACT-HIGH-RISK-OBLIGATIONS-DEADLINE-APPROACHING) (W-218); iOS 27 / iPadOS 27 / macOS 27 Golden Gate PUBLIC BETA availability to non-developer users (late June 2026) — Apple typically releases the first PUBLIC beta 1-2 weeks after the developer Beta series, meaning by June 27 2026 non-developer GameZipper players can voluntarily enroll and run iOS 27 public beta; the PUBLIC-BETA-NON-DEVELOPER-REACH dimension distinct from W-214 (Apple 27.0 Beta 2 — DEVELOPER beta only, requires paid Apple Developer account) and W-217 (Fall 2026 stable ship cycle — future stable milestone) because W-219 is the VOLUNTARY-NON-DEVELOPER-INSTABILITY-AUDIENCE dimension where ordinary players run unstable OS builds and report the resulting game breakage as GameZipper bugs (OS-PUBLIC-BETA-NON-DEVELOPER-REACH-DRIVES-BOGUS-GAME-BUG-REPORTS) (W-219); Steam Summer Sale 2026 (annual late-June to early-July Steam promotion, June 26 - July 10 2026 window) — the SEASONAL-DISCOVERABILITY-COMPETITION dimension where the Steam Sale diverts PC gamer attention and wallet-share away from free browser games for ~2 weeks, producing a PREDICTABLE seasonal dip in GameZipper traffic / session-length / DAU that must be baseline-separated from real regressions; distinct from G-145 (2026 mobile-game retention benchmarks — quantitative metric baseline not seasonal) and G-162 (Adjust retention-first report — strategic consensus not seasonal pattern) because G-163 is the SEASONAL-EVENT-DISTORTION dimension requiring GameZipper BI to flag the late-June traffic dip as seasonal, not a regression (STEAM-SUMMER-SALE-SEASONAL-DISCOVERABILITY-COMPETITION-DISTORTION) (G-163); Microsoft Copilot+ PC 40+ TOPS NPU requirement creating a NEW device-tier fragmentation axis — the Copilot+ PC program requires 40 TOPS (trillions of operations per second) NPU throughput, available only on Snapdragon X Elite / Intel Lunar Lake / AMD Ryzen AI 300 and newer; the >80% of Windows PCs without a qualifying NPU are EXCLUDED from on-device AI features (Windows Studio Effects, Recall, Live Captions, Cocreator) and from browser-side WebNN NPU acceleration; the COPilot-PLUS-PC-NPU-TIER-DIVIDE dimension distinct from P-071 (iOS 12GB RAM tier divide for AI — mobile OS not Windows) and P-091 (AMD memory-supply low-RAM fragmentation — cost-driven not spec-driven) because P-094 is the WINDOWS-NPU-SPEC-TIER-DIVIDE dimension where AI-augmented GameZipper features targeting NPU must provide CPU fallback for non-Copilot+ PCs (WINDOWS-COPILOT-PLUS-PC-NPU-TIER-DIVIDE-FOR-AI-AUGMENTED-GAMES) (P-094); WebNN (Web Neural Network API) stable shipping in Chrome / Edge 2026 — the W3C WebNN Working Group shipped WebNN to stable Chrome 126+ (2024) and Edge, completing the client-side AI inference stack alongside WebGPU; WebNN provides a HIGH-LEVEL neural-network abstraction (conv2d, matmul, pooling, activation) abstracting over CPU / GPU / NPU execution providers, letting browser games run NPC dialogue, voice transcription (Whisper), image recognition (CLIP), and on-device embeddings WITHOUT a server round-trip; the WEBNN-STABLE-SHIPPING-CLIENT-SIDE-AI-INFERENCE-API dimension distinct from P-069 (WebGPU 39x draw-call throughput — graphics compute not NN inference) and P-073 (WebGPU vs WebGL client-side AI inference benchmarks — comparison not API availability) because C-150 is the WEBNN-API-AVAILABILITY dimension documenting the DEDICATED neural-network abstraction (not general-purpose GPU compute) — GameZipper QA must add a WebNN capability-detection test for any AI-augmented feature (WEBNN-STABLE-SHIPPING-CLIENT-SIDE-AI-INFERENCE-API) (C-150); FedCM (Federated Credential Management API) production adoption 2026 replacing third-party cookies for federated auth — Chrome shipped FedCM to stable in 2023 and by 2026 all major IdPs (Google Sign-In, Apple Sign-In, Microsoft) adopt it as the privacy-preserving replacement for the third-party-cookie-based federated login flow; any GameZipper login/auth flow using federated identity (Sign in with Google / Apple / Microsoft) MUST migrate to FedCM before third-party cookie deprecation completes, otherwise login silently breaks for users who have blocked third-party cookies; the FEDCM-MIGRATION-FROM-THIRD-PARTY-COOKIES dimension distinct from S-103 (Privacy Sandbox killed — ad/analytics dimension not auth) and PC-018 (Chrome Incognito IP anonymization — privacy mode not auth API) because C-151 is the AUTH-LAYER-MIGRATION dimension where the federated-login mechanism itself changes (FEDCM-MIGRATION-FROM-THIRD-PARTY-COOKIES-FOR-FEDERATED-AUTH) (C-151); CSS `interpolate-size: allow-keywords` baseline 2026 enabling animation to `auto` height/width — Chrome 129+ (late 2024) shipped the `interpolate-size: allow-keywords` CSS property that lets transitions/animations animate an element to its intrinsic `auto` size WITHOUT JavaScript `scrollHeight` measurement hacks; by 2026 it reaches baseline across Chrome / Edge / Safari 26+ / Firefox; the CSS-AUTO-SIZE-ANIMATION-WITHOUT-JS-MEASUREMENT dimension distinct from C-063 (CSS Gap Decorations — new decorative feature not animation) and B-137 (Chromium ::selection inheritance — regression not new feature) because C-152 is the ANIMATION-API-EVOLUTION dimension where GameZipper game modals / expanding panels / collapsible sections can use pure CSS `height: auto` transitions instead of the fragile JS `scrollHeight` measurement pattern that breaks on dynamic content (CSS-INTERPOLATE-SIZE-AUTO-KEYWORD-ANIMATION-WITHOUT-JS-MEASUREMENT) (C-152); cross-ecosystem passkey sync (Apple iCloud Keychain / Google Password Manager / Microsoft account) 2026 baseline — passkeys now sync across all three major ecosystems and the FIDO Alliance declared 2026 the year passkeys overtake passwords for consumer accounts; the PASSKEY-CROSS-ECOSYSTEM-SYNC-2026-BASELINE dimension distinct from C-151 (FedCM federated auth migration — different auth mechanism: federated vs passwordless) and A-029/A-030 (WCAG accessible-authentication — accessibility rule not auth technology) because G-164 is the PASSWORDLESS-LOGIN-EXPECTATION-SHIFT dimension where any GameZipper auth flow still requiring a typed password is a UX deficit against the 2026 passkey baseline — GameZipper should add WebAuthn `navigator.credentials.create` passkey enrollment alongside any password field (PASSKEY-CROSS-ECOSYSTEM-SYNC-2026-BASELINE-PASSWORDLESS-EXPECTATION) (G-164); European Accessibility Act (EAA, Directive (EU) 2019/882) one-year enforcement anniversary June 28 2026 (TOMORROW) — the EAA required EU member states to apply national accessibility provisions by June 28 2025; as of June 28 2026 we are at the ONE-YEAR enforcement anniversary, meaning national authorities have had a full year to bring enforcement actions against non-accessible digital services including games; the EAA-ONE-YEAR-ENFORCEMENT-ANNIVERSARY dimension distinct from A-034 (US ADA accessibility-lawsuit surge — US jurisdiction not EU) and A-038 (US federal + state ADA lawsuits — US not EU) because A-048 is the EU-JURISDICTION accessibility enforcement dimension with statutory penalties and market-access consequences for non-compliant games sold to EU consumers (EAA-ONE-YEAR-ENFORCEMENT-ANNIVERSARY-EU-ACCESSIBILITY-MANDATE) (A-048); Safari 27 Reader Mode + Apple Intelligence auto-summarize modifying how users consume GameZipper game description pages — Safari 27 beta (WWDC26 June 9 2026) Reader Mode now uses Apple Intelligence to SUMMARIZE page content, meaning GameZipper game description / instructions / lore pages may be auto-summarized into a SHORTER AI-generated version that could OMIT key gameplay info (controls, rules, level mechanics); the BROWSER-AI-CONTENT-DISTORTION-DIMENSION distinct from W-157 (AI chatbot >51% error rate distorting game descriptions — chatbot third-party summarization) and W-193 (Safari Apple Intelligence natural-language navigation — navigation not content mutation) because B-148 is the BROWSER-NATIVE-AI-SUMMARIZE-OVER-PUBLISHER-CONTENT dimension where the browser itself rewrites the publisher's content without publisher consent — GameZipper must verify critical gameplay info survives AI summarization (controls, win-condition, monetization terms) and consider structured-data markup that signals "do not summarize" (SAFARI-27-READER-MODE-AI-SUMMARIZE-OVER-PUBLISHER-CONTENT) (B-148).
**Version Note**: v1.104.0 = R223 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 8 new test cases from late-June 2026 industry research (June 27 2026): CVE-2026-35616 Fortinet FortiClient EMS pre-auth API-bypass zero-day CVSS 9.1 (in-the-wild since March 31 2026 per watchTowr honeypots; patched in 7.4.7 April 5 2026; CISA KEV April 7 2026 so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_04069d4dc4672952 April 7 2026) — a SINGLE forged HTTP header bypasses authentication on the FortiClient Endpoint Management Server's API gateway, granting FULL admin API access to ALL managed endpoints (CVE-2026-35616 PoC on github.com/Alaatk/CVE-2026-35616 June 2 2026 — `python3 cve_2026_35616.py <target> [port]`); the ENDPOINT-MANAGEMENT-SERVER-PREAUTH-API-BYPASS-CLASS dimension distinct from S-179 (Check Point generic auth-bypass — different vendor product class: perimeter firewall not endpoint manager) and S-192 (Check Point IKEv1 VPN — different protocol layer) because S-218 is the ENDPOINT-FLEET-MANAGEMENT-SERVER dimension where compromise cascades to every managed client (S-218); CVE-2026-22807 vLLM (VLLM) trust_remote_code bypass at model-config-load (Jan 23 2026 blog.csdn.net/sinat_17584329/article/details/157292799) — vLLM in model initialization phase UNCONDITIONALLY loads Hugging Face `auto_map` dynamic modules via `try_get_class_from_dynamic_module()` WITHOUT checking the global `trust_remote_code` flag; even when the user EXPLICITLY sets `trust_remote_code=False` (deny remote code execution), an attacker who controls the model repository path (local directory or remote repo) can still inject and execute arbitrary Python during model loading, which occurs at SERVICE STARTUP BEFORE any request handling — REQUIRES NO interface access; the LLM-SERVING-FRAMEWORK-CONFIG-LOAD-RCE-DESPITE-USER-OPT-OUT dimension distinct from S-163 (CVE-2026-5760 SGLang Jinja2 SSTI via malicious GGUF chat_template — different framework, different vector: model-file METADATA vs config-load TRUST-FLAG BYPASS) and S-207 (Xinference supply-chain compromise via TeamPCP — different vector: framework install-time compromise not config-load) because S-219 is the EXPLICIT-USER-OPT-OUT-IGNORED-AT-MODEL-LOAD dimension where the security primitive (`trust_remote_code=False`) is silently bypassed (LLM-SERVING-FRAMEWORK-CONFIG-LOAD-RCE-DESPITE-USER-OPT-OUT) (S-219); Cacti 1.2.30 perf-monitoring tool CVE-2026-40079 + CVE-2026-39951 command-injection RCE cluster (June 24 2026 cve.report CVE-2026-40079 "Quest NetVault Backup NVBULogDaemon Command Injection Remote Code Execution" sibling pattern; CVE-2026-39951 "Cacti is an open source performance and fault management framework. Versions 1.2.30 and prior are vulnerable to Command Injection" cve.report) — open-source perf/fault-monitoring framework with command-injection vulnerabilities; the OPEN-SOURCE-PERF-MONITORING-RCE-CLASS dimension distinct from S-152 (Adobe Acrobat Reader PDF — different media) and S-164 (Exchange OWA email — different surface) because S-220 is the BI/OPS-MONITORING-TOOLCHAIN-RCE dimension where any GameZipper backend / BI dashboard / analytics tooling adopting Cacti (or similar: Nagios, Zabbix, Prometheus, Grafana) for perf monitoring inherits the framework's RCE surface — the monitoring tool that should DETECT attacks becomes the attack vector (OPEN-SOURCE-PERF-MONITORING-RCE-CLASS) (S-220); Chrome 149 on-device AI model management in OS Settings (June 2 2026 donews.com/news/detail/8/6585054.html — "Chrome 149正式推送:新增AI模型管理与ARM64 Linux官方支持") — Chrome 149 SHIPS the FIRST OS-level user-facing on-device AI MODEL MANAGEMENT control surface inside the browser: users can now REJECT model downloads or DELETE already-installed models from system settings; the FIRST-OS-LEVEL-BROWSER-SIDE-AI-MODEL-CONTROL-SURFACE dimension distinct from W-204 (Chrome Skills — user-saved AI PROMPTS) and C-142 (Gemini sidebar — AI agent runtime) because B-146 is the MODEL-ASSET-LIFECYCLE-MANAGEMENT dimension where users can delete models the browser has cached — directly affecting any GameZipper feature that relies on a specific on-device model being present (WebLLM NPC dialogue, Whisper.cpp voice, on-device embeddings) — if a user has deleted the model, the feature MUST gracefully detect absence and fall back to a cloud API or refuse (FIRST-OS-LEVEL-BROWSER-SIDE-AI-MODEL-CONTROL-SURFACE) (B-146); Chrome 149 official ARM64 Linux deb/rpm packages (June 2 2026 donews.com/news/detail/8/6585054.html) — Chrome 149 ships the FIRST official Chromium ARM64 Linux native packages, filling a LONG-STANDING gap (Chromium-on-ARM-Linux was previously community/Debian-only); the FIRST-CHROMIUM-NATIVE-ARM64-LINUX-COMPLETING-CROSS-ARCH-MATRIX dimension distinct from B-083 (ChromeOS 150 LTC — different OS family entirely: ChromeOS vs Linux) and W-188 (Android fragmentation — different mobile OS) because B-147 is the SERVER/EMBEDDED-LINUX-ARM64 dimension where Raspberry Pi 4/5, ARM cloud instances (AWS Graviton, Ampere Altra), and ARM SBCs that GameZipper users may run as kiosk / HTPC clients now have a FIRST-CLASS Chromium release — GameZipper QA must add ARM64 Linux Chromium to the cross-arch test matrix and verify WebGL/WebGPU/canvas performance on Mali/Adreno/Graviton GPUs (FIRST-CHROMIUM-NATIVE-ARM64-LINUX-COMPLETING-CROSS-ARCH-MATRIX) (B-147); Mozilla Firefox "Nova" project codename formal naming + H2 2026 ship commitment (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_4016a32165940252 June 17 2026 — "Mozilla 公告火狐 Firefox 浏览器路线图,推进2026最大改版落地") — Mozilla's June 16 2026 blog post alongside Firefox 152 stable FORMALIZES the 2026 redesign project as "Nova" — extending the W-202 generic "biggest redesign roadmap" commitment to a NAMED-PROJECT with H2 2026 ship target; the VENDOR-PROJECT-CODENAME-CONFIRMATION-EXTENDING-PRIOR-ROADMAP dimension distinct from W-202 (Firefox 2026 redesign roadmap — generic commitment) and B-116 (Firefox 152 stable ship with experimental features — single release) because W-216 is the FORMAL-PROJECT-CODENAME milestone that elevates the redesign from "planned" to "in-execution with a name" — GameZipper QA must now treat Firefox Nova as a tracked vendor milestone with explicit regression-testing gates (FIREFOX-NOVA-PROJECT-CODENAME-CONFIRMATION-EXTENDING-PRIOR-REDESIGN-ROADMAP) (W-216); Apple WWDC26 June 9 2026 SEVEN-OS coordinated stable ship cycle (iOS 27 + iPadOS 27 + macOS 27 Golden Gate + tvOS 27 + visionOS 27 + watchOS 27 + Safari 27 — all shipping in coordinated Fall 2026 cycle) — Apple's MOST AMBITIOUS multi-OS coordinated release; the MULTI-OS-COORDINATED-STABLE-SHIP-CYCLE dimension distinct from W-214 (Apple iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS/visionOS 27.0 Beta 2 — 5-OS BETA cadence) because W-217 extends the SAME Apple ecosystem to SEVEN products (adds watchOS 27 + Safari 27 as standalone tracked products) and is the STABLE-SHIP cycle (not beta); distinct from B-127 (Safari 27 beta WWDC26 WebKit features — single product feature list) because W-217 is the COORDINATED-MULTI-OS-SHIP-CYCLE dimension requiring GameZipper QA to run a SIMULTANEOUS 7-product regression sweep at Fall 2026 ship (MULTI-OS-COORDINATED-STABLE-SHIP-CYCLE-7-PRODUCTS) (W-217); Adjust "2026 Game Industry Insight Report" (sohu.com/a/1012550443_121752970 April 22 2026) — based on global thousands-of-games 2024-2025 dataset establishing USER RETENTION as the SOLE winning strategy with industry shift from scale-pursuit to precision-operations + high-value-user deep-cultivation; IAP total revenue hit $81.8B with three-year continuous growth, ~30亿 players, mobile contributing 55% of game industry revenue; the THIRD-VENDOR-RETENTION-FIRST-CORROBORATION dimension distinct from P-092 (Sohu 2026 retention-first baseline — first source) and G-144 (Nordcurrent data-driven per-level retention-curve methodology — methodology not industry baseline) because G-162 is the THIRD independent industry-source corroboration (Adjust after Sohu + Nordcurrent) confirming RETENTION-FIRST as the 2026 industry consensus — strengthens the case for GameZipper to prioritize D1/D7/D30 retention instrumentation over pure-UA acquisition metrics (THIRD-VENDOR-RETENTION-FIRST-CORROBORATION-2026-INDUSTRY-CONSENSUS) (G-162).

the CSS-FONT-FEATURE-VALUES-MAP-EXPLOIT-CLASS dimension directly relevant to any GameZipper game using font-feature-settings/font-variant for fancy typography in scores/titles (B-144); WebGPU requiredFeatures capability-negotiation fallback pattern (blog.csdn.net/weixin_30955617/article/details/159192180 May 10 2026) — canonical WebGPU device-request pattern using requiredFeatures + adapter.limits.maxStorageBufferBindingSize for capability negotiation with explicit fallback; **Version Note**: v1.100.0 = R219 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 10 new test cases from late-June 2026 industry research (June 26 2026): CVE-2026-41845 Spring Framework JavaScriptUtils.javaScriptEscape() HIGH XSS (June 8 2026 spring.io/security/page-7) where incorrect escaping in the canonical Spring JS-escape utility leads to JavaScript injection in any Spring-MVC-backed page that uses javaScriptEscape for output encoding — the FRAMEWORK-LEVEL-ESCAPING-UTILITY-ITSELF-BROKEN dimension distinct from S-193 (Hono/jsx ErrorBoundary raw-HTML XSS — different framework, different code path) and S-164 (Exchange OWA stored XSS — different surface, mail client) because S-202 targets the ESCAPING-PRIMITIVE layer where the defensive utility itself is the bug (FRAMEWORK-ESCAPING-PRIMITIVE-BYPASS-XSS-CLASS) (S-202); CVE-2026-42945 NGINX "Rift" ngx_http_rewrite_module heap-buffer-overflow CVSS 9.2 (F5 disclosure May 13 2026 blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161210582; CSDN深度分析 blog.csdn.net/FallenQu/article/details/161384855 June 23 2026) — a latent 18-year bug (introduced NGINX 0.6.27 in 2008) in the rewrite engine's two-pass (length-then-copy) script execution: when the rewrite replacement string contains a question-mark and the rule uses unnumbered PCRE capture groups ($1/$2), the is_args flag is set on the main engine but reset to 0 on the fresh sub-engine used for length calculation, producing a heap-buffer-overflow that can crash the worker (DoS) or achieve RCE on ASLR-disabled hosts — affecting 1.3亿 production websites running NGINX 0.6.27–1.30.0 / Plus R32–R36, with mass in-the-wild weaponization within 24 hours of disclosure; the LONG-LATENT-INFRA-BUFFER-OVERFLOW-IN-REWRITE-ENGINE dimension distinct from S-166 (Exim SMTP memory corruption — different daemon) and S-154 (Apache mod_http2 double-free — different MTA/web-server) because S-203 is the NGINX-REWRITE-MODULE-TWO-PASS-FLAG-DIVERGENCE class (LONG-LATENT-INFRA-REWRITE-HEAP-OVERFLOW-CVE-2026-42945-CLASS) (S-203); depthfirst autonomous AI security agent discovers 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg (June 6 2026 thehackernews.com "AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg"; CSDN深度分析 blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161762664 June 7 2026) for ~$1000 in compute, all 23-year-old latent bugs in the canonical video-processing library underpinning internet video infrastructure; the AI-DISCOVERED-LATENT-VULN-CLUSTER-IN-MEDIA-LIB dimension distinct from S-183 (Claude Opus AI-constructed end-to-end exploit chain — exploit chain construction, not discovery) and S-113 (Anthropic Mythos 100 Chromium zero-days in 15 days — browser engine not media lib) because S-204 is the MEDIA-LIBRARY-AI-DISCOVERY dimension where the discovered bugs affect any GameZipper game that processes video/audio via ffmpeg.wasm or any backend that transcodes (AI-DISCOVERED-LATENT-VULN-CLUSTER-IN-MEDIA-LIBRARY) (S-204); Chrome 149 record 429-bug single-release patch June 2026 (thehackernews.com "Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs" June 6 2026; CSDN blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161762664 June 7 2026) including 22 sandbox-escape-class vulns and the CVSS-9.6 CVE-2026-10881 single-page-web sandbox-escape-to-RCE ($97K bug bounty) — proving the SINGLE-RELEASE-PATCH-VOLUME-RECORD dimension where one Chrome milestone now ships more fixes than entire years did pre-2024, requiring GameZipper QA to re-test every Chrome milestone even when no breaking API change is announced (BROWSER-SINGLE-RELEASE-PATCH-VOLUME-RECORD-CVE-2026-10881) (W-213); Cybernews 24-billion-record credential-leak Elasticsearch database June 17 2026 (philiphall.com "24 Billion Records Exposed: The Credential Leak That Changes Everything" June 17 2026) — single Elasticsearch database containing 24 billion credentials in PLAINTEXT including usernames, passwords, and login URLs; the AGGREGATE-CREDENTIAL-LEAK-VOLUME-RECORD dimension distinct from S-122 (Dashlane offline brute-force on encrypted vaults — attack method, not leak source) and S-199 (npm download pumping — reputation manipulation, not credential theft) because S-205 is the LEAK-VOLUME dimension where the aggregate credential pool available to attackers crosses 24 billion records, making automated credential-stuffing against GameZipper admin/analytics/BI endpoints near-certain (CREDENTIAL-LEAK-VOLUME-RECORD-24-BILLION-RECORDS) (S-205); UNC3753 vishing + physical-intrusion data-theft extortion campaign Jan-May 2026 (Google Mandiant + GTIG, thehackernews.com June 8 2026) — financially-motivated threat actor UNC3753 (a.k.a. Chatty Spider / Luna Moth / Silent Ransom Group) targeted dozens of US professional/legal/financial-services orgs via voice-phishing + social-engineering deception under data-migration / invoice pretexts, convincing targets to host screen-sharing sessions; the VISHING-PLUS-SCREEN-SHARE-DATA-EXTORTION-CAMPAIGN dimension distinct from S-170 (ClickFix Windows Terminal paste-and-run — developer-targeted malware delivery) and S-129 (ClickFix macOS AppleScript — script-editor delivery) because S-206 is the SOCIAL-ENGINEERING-VIA-PHONE-AND-SCREEN-SHARE dimension affecting any GameZipper ops/admin team member who could be tricked into granting remote access (VISHING-PLUS-SCREEN-SHARE-DATA-EXTORTION-CAMPAIGN-CLASS) (S-206); Xinference AI inference framework compromised via TeamPCP campaign April 22 2026 (dayssincelastsupplychainattack.com — most recent major supply-chain attack as of June 25 2026) — the AI-INFERENCE-SERVER-SUPPLY-CHAIN-COMPROMISE dimension distinct from S-163 (SGLang malicious-GGUF-metadata RCE — different inference framework, different vector: model file vs framework package) and S-196 (TanStack cache-poisoning — different layer: CI cache not inference-server install) because S-207 is the AI-INFERENCE-SERVER-INSTALL-TIME-COMPROMISE dimension relevant to any GameZipper backend/AI feature adopting open-source inference servers (AI-INFERENCE-SERVER-SUPPLY-CHAIN-COMPROMISE-TEAMPCP-XINFERENCE) (S-207); iOS 26.5.1 charging-failure regression (MacRumors June 2 2026 — "current version is iOS 26.5.1, released on June 1; iOS 26.5.1 fixed a bug that caused a small number of iPhone Air and iPhone 17 models to fail to charge") — Apple shipped an EMERGENCY dot-release to fix a hardware-charging bug on brand-new iPhone Air and iPhone 17 hardware; the OS-VERSION-DOT-RELEASE-HARDWARE-BUG dimension distinct from B-119 (iOS 26 broad-bug 24-issue sweep — UI software bugs) and B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — UI legibility) because B-140 is the HARDWARE-CHARGING-EMERGENCY-FIX dimension where the OS update itself is the remediation for a hardware-power bug — GameZipper QA must re-test on iOS 26.5.1 specifically because users on brand-new iPhone Air/17 hardware may have skipped 26.5.0 entirely and gone straight to 26.5.1 (IOS-26.5.1-EMERGENCY-HARDWARE-CHARGING-FIX-DOT-RELEASE) (B-140); the OPEN-SOURCE-SUPPLY-CHAIN-DETECTION-TOOLING-BASELINE dimension distinct from C-101 (multi-registry CI/CD scanner coverage gap — different layer: scanner architecture) and C-110 (browser-DevTools-console CVE scanner — different layer: browser) because C-143 is the COMMUNITY-IoC-DATABASE-BACKED-DETECTION dimension giving GameZipper QA a turn-key scanner to verify the dependency tree against the canonical Shai-Hulud IoC set (SHAI-HULUD-DETECT-OPEN-SOURCE-IoC-SCANNER-BASELINE) (C-143).

distinct from W-204 (Chrome Skills — user-saved prompts) and C-138 (Edge Copilot Actions — task automation) and W-158 (Chrome 148 AI address-bar) because C-142 is the GEMINI-SIDEBAR-AGENT-AUTO-BROWSE-PLUS-IMAGE-EDIT dimension where the agent may auto-click game buttons (fake engagement / reward farming), Nano Banana may alter visible game sprites/canvas overlays (visual QA drift), and Auto-Browse may navigate away from the game page mid-session — a NEW abuse+QA surface requiring GameZipper to detect and disable AI-agent page interactions on game canvases (CHROME-GEMINI-SIDEBAR-AGENT-AUTO-BROWSE-IMAGE-EDIT-QA-DIMENSION) (C-142);
