G-148 covers a SERVER-side / CI-side tooling evolution. **Distinct from W-150 (WASM 3.0 stable features — language)**: W-150 covers the WASM LANGUAGE features; G-148 covers the JS RUNTIME features. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's CURRENT stack uses Node.js (Hermes pipeline) + Cloudflare Pages (static hosting) + GitHub Pages (alt hosting) — Node 26.1 FFI could enable GameZipper pipeline steps to call native libraries (image processing, video transcoding) without writing/maintaining a Node native addon; (b) Bun's built-in headless-browser automation is a DIRECT competitor to the current GameZipper QA tooling (Kachilu, Playwright) — Bun may offer faster startup + lower memory + simpler dependency tree, worth evaluating for future QA pipeline revisions; (c) Bun's built-in cron scheduling could replace the external scheduler running this Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — fewer moving parts, lower operational overhead; (d) BlueJS's AOT compilation is relevant if GameZipper ever ships a desktop app or edge-runtime tool where small binary size matters; (e) Astro 6.3's advanced routing is relevant if GameZipper migrates from the current static-HTML-per-game structure to an Astro-based SSG with dynamic routes — the migration would simplify game-registration (currently 7+ file updates per game per the post-deploy-verification skill) into a single Astro content-collection entry; (f) Valibot 1.4.0 is relevant for any future GameZipper REST API that needs schema validation (e.g., a future user-saved-games sync API). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) evaluate Node 26.1 FFI for one GameZipper pipeline step that currently uses a Node native addon or shells out to a CLI (e.g., image-optimization, OG-image generation) — measure the complexity-reduction + performance delta; (2) prototype a Bun-based replacement for the current Kachilu or Playwright QA step — measure startup time, memory usage, and QA-step execution time; if Bun is competitive, document the migration path; (3) prototype a Bun-cron-based replacement for the external scheduler running this Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — verify Bun-cron can run a 4-hour-interval job with the same reliability; (4) evaluate BlueJS AOT compilation for any future GameZipper desktop tooling (a future "GameZipper Game Studio" desktop app for level editing); (5) evaluate Astro 6.3 as a future GameZipper SSG migration target — specifically prototype the games-data.js → Astro content-collection migration to see if it eliminates the recurring games-data.js orphan-entry bug (the post-deploy-verification skill documents 5+ recurrences of this bug); (6) for any future GameZipper REST API, evaluate Valibot 1.4.0 vs Zod for schema validation, weighing bundle-size and tree-shakeability.


---

## CATEGORY 44: 2026 LATE-JUNE PDF-MEDIA-VECTOR + WEB-SERVER-PROXY-PLUGIN-UNAUTH-RCE + APACHE-HTTP2-DOUBLE-FREE + EDR-AUTO-UPDATE-HIJACK + VIM-MODELINE-DEFAULT-CONFIG-RCE + AI-CLI-SCOPED-NPM-TYPOSQUAT + WINDOWS-DRIVER-TOCTOU-LPE + BROAD-SCOPE-LINUX-LPE (R198 — Dynamic Test Intelligence)

This category documents the late-June 2026 R198 cron evolution: 8 new dimensions surfaced via minimax web search (web-search-prime/SearXNG not invoked to continue avoiding the recurring 404 pattern from R187-R197 — minimax is the stable search backend). The dimensions cluster around THREE themes: (1) downstream player-side PDF attack surface (S-152); (2) server-side web-server RCE primitives in the WebLogic Proxy Plug-in, Apache HTTP/2 mod_http2, and Trend Micro Apex One auto-update pipeline (S-153, S-154, S-155); (3) developer-tool default-config RCE via Vim modeline (S-156) and a NEW vector in the AI-CLI impersonation family via scoped-npm-namespace typosquatting (S-157); plus two OS-layer LPE primitives affecting the Windows host (B-115 csc.sys TOCTOU) and the broad-scope Linux LPE that hits every GameZipper hosting/CDN infrastructure component (P-074 "Copy Fail" 732-byte PoC affecting all distros since 2017).

### 44.1 CVE-2026-34621 Adobe Acrobat / Acrobat Reader Zero-Day PDF-Media-Vector Delayed-Disclosure Long Window (CVSS 8.6)

- [S-152] **[P2]** Adobe published an emergency security update for Acrobat DC, Acrobat Reader DC, and Acrobat 2024 addressing **CVE-2026-34621** — a zero-day allowing arbitrary code execution from a crafted PDF. CVSS score: 8.6 (high). Critically, **the exploit code had been in the wild since November 2025** — a 4+ month delayed-disclosure window before Adobe shipped the fix; the attack target and motive remain unclear but the in-the-wild exploitation is confirmed. User interaction is just opening a malicious PDF — no JavaScript, no macros, no embedded file launch required. **Source**: ithome.com/tag/lingriloudong/ (IT之家 zero-day tag page — "Adobe 旗下 Acrobat DC、Reader DC 及Acrobat 2024 被曝存在一个零日漏洞(CVE-2026-34621),黑客通过构造恶意 PDF 文件即可远程植入恶意软件,完全控制系统。漏洞利用代码早在去年 11 月就已出现" + "Adobe 为Acrobat 和Acrobat Reader 发布紧急安全更新,修复一个可执行任意代码的零日漏洞(CVE-2026-34621),CVSS 评分高达 8.6" + "安全研究员警告,一个针对 Adobe Acrobat Reader 的零日漏洞已被黑客利用至少 4 个月。用户只需打开恶意 PDF,无需其他交互,设备信息就可能被窃取,甚至面临电脑被完全控制的风险"). **Why this is the PDF-MEDIA-VECTOR-DELAYED-DISCLOSURE-LONG-WINDOW dimension distinct from S-121 (npm Ghost campaign fake-install-log RAT deception — developer-side npm)**: S-121 attacks the developer's npm install output; S-152 attacks the END USER's PDF reader. **Distinct from S-047 (fake Chrome security alert social engineering)**: S-047 requires the user to download+run a fake Chrome installer; S-152 requires only that the user OPEN a PDF — a much lower-interaction bar. **Distinct from S-123 (fake browser-update phishing with clipboard/Win+R PowerShell self-typed execution)**: S-123 chains clipboard paste; S-152 is a single-file-open → RCE with no further user action. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper players may receive PDF attachments via phishing emails or via malicious ad creatives that offer a "downloadable game manual" or "GameZipper newsletter PDF" — the PDF is a much more credible lure than a fake-Chrome-installer because PDFs are a universally-trusted document format; (b) the 4+ month delayed-disclosure window is the RECURRING dimension — Adobe's disclosure lag is not unique; Microsoft, Apple, and Google all have multi-month disclosure lags for some CVEs, meaning GameZipper must assume that any player-side PDF/Office/Font zero-day may have been in the wild for MONTHS before the vendor patches; (c) the CVE-2026-34621 attack pattern — "open file → full system compromise" — is the same pattern that affected game developers in C-109 (Apifox desktop-client CDN poisoning) and is the pattern any future GameZipper "official downloadable content" (PDF manual, desktop screensaver, installable theme) must defend against; (d) the dimension extends to any PDF the GameZipper QA pipeline itself processes (e.g., design docs, accessibility audits, legal reviews) — if a malicious PDF is opened on a developer machine it can compromise the GameZipper codebase. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) extend the existing GameZipper phishing-awareness training (covered in S-047/S-123) to explicitly include the PDF-as-malware-vector — players and devs must not open PDFs from untrusted sources; (2) audit any GameZipper pipeline step that processes PDFs (design-doc ingestion, accessibility-audit tooling, legal-contract PDFs) and ensure the PDF reader is sandboxed (Chrome's built-in PDF viewer, not Adobe Reader); (3) for any future GameZipper "downloadable content" offered as PDF (game manuals, lore PDFs, newsletters), publish ONLY via GameZipper.com HTTPS download with SHA-256 hash + signature, never via email attachment; (4) on GameZipper developer machines, enforce Adobe Reader sandboxing + Protected Mode + disable JavaScript execution in PDFs; (5) subscribe to vendor zero-day disclosure feeds (Adobe, Microsoft, Apple, Google) and treat any "exploit code appeared N months ago" announcement as a P1 retroactive-exposure sweep on GameZipper assets.

### 44.2 CVE-2026-21962 Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in Unauthenticated RCE via Security-Check-Before-URL-Decode Ordering Flaw (June 10 2026)

- [S-153] **[P2]** Oracle disclosed **CVE-2026-21962** — a Critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in (the mod_wl_ohs / nginx-weblogic-proxy / Apache-weblogic-plugin that fronts WebLogic deployments). The root cause is a SECURITY-DESIGN LOGIC ERROR, not a traditional code vulnerability: the proxy plug-in performs security-check BEFORE URL-decode/normalize, when the correct order is URL-decode/normalize → security-check → forward. This ordering flaw allows an attacker to craft a malicious URL that bypasses the security-check layer (because the raw URL looks benign) but is then URL-decoded to a malicious path that hits an exploitable backend endpoint. Attack impact: complete auth-bypass + remote code execution + backend cluster penetration + data exfiltration + lateral movement. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/2302_79436907/article/details/159896100 (June 10 2026 — "CVE-2026-21962 Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in 远程代码执行漏洞" + "完全绕过身份认证: 攻击者无需任何凭证即可发起攻击" + "远程代码执行: 可直接在目标服务器执行任意系统命令" + "后端集群渗透: 可突破 DMZ 隔离, 攻击后端 WebLogic 集群" + "核心缺陷: 安全校验与URL解码的『顺序颠倒』" + "正确的安全处理流程应为: HTTP请求 → URL解码/规范化 → 安全校验 → 请求转发"). **Why this is the WEB-SERVER-PROXY-PLUGIN-UNAUTH-RCE-ORDERING-FLAW dimension distinct from W-163 (CVE-2026-21992 Oracle Identity Manager / Web Services Manager unauthenticated HTTP RCE CVSS 9.8)**: W-163 is a different Oracle product (Identity Manager, not Proxy Plug-in); the root-cause class is also different — W-163 is a deserialization/design flaw, S-153 is a security-check-then-URL-decode ORDERING flaw. **Distinct from S-069 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DoS — CVE-2026-49160 CDN/game availability)**: S-069 is a DoS at the HTTP/2 protocol layer; S-153 is an RCE at the web-server-proxy-plug-in layer. **Distinct from S-086 (NGINX heap overflow CVE-2026-42945)**: S-086 is an NGINX-specific memory-corruption bug; S-153 is an Oracle-WebLogic-specific design-logic flaw — completely different codebases. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's CURRENT stack is GitHub Pages + Cloudflare CDN (no Oracle WebLogic) so S-153 does not directly bind — BUT (b) the PATTERN of "security-check before URL-decode" is a RECURRING DESIGN FLAW that affects many reverse-proxy implementations (NGINX, HAProxy, Envoy, custom Express middleware); GameZipper's Cloudflare Workers + edge functions may have similar ordering flaws if they implement URL-pattern security checks; (c) any future GameZipper backend component that sits behind a reverse proxy must verify the proxy URL-decodes BEFORE applying security rules — the WAF rule order matters; (d) the dimension teaches a generic QA pattern: for any web-server-proxy or WAF, test with URL-encoded + double-encoded + path-normalized attack payloads to verify the security-check is applied AFTER decoding. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit GameZipper Cloudflare Workers + edge functions for any URL-pattern security check (e.g., "block requests matching /admin/*") and verify the check is applied AFTER URL-decoding (send `/%61dmin/` and `/admin/` and verify BOTH are blocked); (2) for any future GameZipper backend behind a reverse proxy, write a regression test that sends URL-encoded + double-encoded + Unicode-normalized attack payloads and verifies the security-check layer blocks them; (3) maintain a list of "security-check before URL-decode" as a recurring QA-pattern checklist item for any new proxy/WAF rule; (4) subscribe to Oracle Critical Patch Update advisories and treat any WebLogic/Identity-Manager Proxy-Plug-in CVE as a P1 audit trigger for any future GameZipper enterprise-integration tier.

### 44.3 CVE-2026-23918 Apache HTTP/2 mod_http2 Double-Free (May 6 2026 — Version 2.4.66 Single-Version Only)

- [S-154] **[P2]** Apache Software Foundation disclosed **CVE-2026-23918** on May 6 2026 — a HIGH-severity memory-corruption vulnerability in `mod_http2` (the HTTP/2 protocol implementation in Apache HTTP Server). The root cause is a Double-Free bug: an unauthenticated attacker sends a specific sequence of HTTP/2 frames — a HEADERS frame with a new odd stream ID (creating an `h2_stream` object) immediately followed by an RST_STREAM frame with the SAME stream ID + non-zero error code (forcing the stream to close). The `h2_mplx_client_rst()` function then attempts cleanup on a stream that was never registered in the active-stream table, AND adds the stream pointer to the cleanup array — leading to a double-free when the cleanup array is processed. Impact: Apache worker-process crash (DoS) on most memory layouts; potential remote code execution under specific memory layouts. **Notably, the vulnerability affects ONLY Apache HTTP Server 2.4.66** (released April 17 2026) — earlier (2.4.65 and prior) and later (2.4.67+) versions are unaffected because the bug was introduced by a 2.4.66-specific change to mod_http2. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161003983 (May 12 2026 — "CVE-2026-23918深度解析: Apache HTTP/2 双释放漏洞原理、利用链与企业级防护全指南" + "2026年5月6日, Apache软件基金会紧急发布安全公告, 披露了一个仅影响 Apache HTTP Server 2.4.66 单一版本的高危内存破坏漏洞 CVE-2026-23918" + "该漏洞存在于 HTTP/2 协议实现核心模块 mod_http2 中, 攻击者无需任何认证即可通过构造特定时序的 HTTP/2 流量触发双释放(Double Free)漏洞" + "Apache工作进程崩溃重启, 在特定内存布局条件下甚至可能实现远程代码执行" + discovered by Google security team). **Why this is the WEB-SERVER-HTTP2-DOUBLE-FREE-LIBRARY-LEVEL dimension distinct from S-069 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DoS — CVE-2026-49160)**: S-069 is a DoS at the HTTP/2 PROTOCOL level (a protocol-spec design issue affecting ALL HTTP/2 implementations); S-154 is a memory-corruption bug in a SPECIFIC LIBRARY (Apache mod_http2) — different vuln class, different scope. **Distinct from S-086 (NGINX heap overflow CVE-2026-42945 — NGINX-specific)**: S-086 is in NGINX's ngx_http_rewrite_module; S-154 is in Apache's mod_http2 — completely different codebases, different vuln classes (heap overflow vs double-free). **Distinct from S-153 (CVE-2026-21962 WebLogic Proxy Plug-in ordering flaw — same disclosure window)**: S-153 is an ordering-flaw in a PROXY-PLUGIN; S-154 is a memory bug in the HTTP/2 module ITSELF. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's CURRENT stack is GitHub Pages + Cloudflare CDN (no Apache HTTP Server) so S-154 does not directly bind — BUT (b) the SINGLE-VERSION-AFFECTED pattern is a NEW QA dimension: a regression in version N that affects ONLY version N (not N-1, not N+1) is a SPECIAL class of bug that cannot be caught by "did we change anything?" diff-based QA — the bug is INTRODUCED and FIXED within the same release cycle; (c) any future GameZipper backend component running Apache HTTP Server (e.g., a future analytics endpoint, a future multiplayer game-server proxy) must verify it is NOT running 2.4.66 specifically — and must pin to 2.4.67+ or earlier-than-2.4.66 to avoid the bug; (d) the dimension extends to any single-version regression in any GameZipper dependency — GameZipper must maintain a known-bad-version list for every dependency and treat any "we upgraded to the latest version" CI step as a regression risk if the latest version is on the known-bad list. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) maintain a KNOWN-BAD-VERSION list for every GameZipper dependency (Apache, NGINX, Node, Express, etc.) — for each entry, document the affected version + the fix version + the vuln ID; (2) for any future GameZipper Apache HTTP Server deployment, verify the version is NOT 2.4.66 specifically; if it must run 2.4.66, disable mod_http2 or front it with a TCP-level proxy that rejects the malicious HEADERS+RST_STREAM frame sequence; (3) for any HTTP/2-handling component in the GameZipper pipeline (Cloudflare Workers, future Apache/NGINX backends), write a regression test that sends the specific HEADERS+RST_STREAM sequence and verifies no crash; (4) the single-version-affected pattern teaches that "upgrading to the latest version" is NOT always safe — always check the known-bad-version list before upgrading.

### 44.4 CVE-2026-34926 Trend Micro Apex One Path Traversal + EDR-Fleet Auto-Update-Channel Hijack (May 21 2026 — CISA KEV Same-Day)

- [S-155] **[P2]** Trend Micro disclosed **CVE-2026-34926** on May 21 2026 — a directory-traversal vulnerability (CWE-23) in the Apex One (on-premise) server. The bug allows a PRE-AUTHENTICATED LOCAL ATTACKER with administrative credentials to MODIFY A KEY TABLE on the server, then INJECT MALICIOUS CODE INTO THE AUTO-UPDATE CHANNEL, which then SILENTLY PUSHES MALWARE TO THE ENTIRE EDR-PROTECTED ENDPOINT FLEET. CISA added CVE-2026-34926 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog the SAME DAY (May 21 2026) with a federal-agency fix deadline of June 4 2026. In-the-wild exploitation is confirmed. The severity is CVSS 6.7 v3.1 / 4.9 v4.0 (Moderate by CVSS but CRITICAL by real-world impact because compromising one EDR server compromises the entire endpoint fleet). Affects Trend Micro Apex One on-premise server versions prior to 14.0.0.17079; Apex One as a Service (SaaS) is NOT affected. **Source**: github.com/advisories/GHSA-4ccp-cqrh-3w9v (May 21 2026 — "A directory traversal vulnerability in the Apex One (on-premise) server could allow a pre-authenticated local attacker to modify a key table on the server to inject malicious code to deploy to agents on affected installations" + "This vulnerability is only exploitable on the on-premise version of Apex One and a potential attacker must have access to the Apex One Server and already obtained administrative credentials to the server via some other method to exploit this vulnerability"); blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161357281 (May 24 2026 — "攻击者一旦获得 Apex One 服务器的本地管理员权限, 即可通过该漏洞篡改平台核心加密密钥表, 进而劫持终端自动更新通道, 将恶意代码静默推送到企业网络内所有连接的终端设备" + "这意味着攻击者可以在几分钟内攻陷整个企业的终端防护体系, 将 EDR (终端检测与响应) 系统变成自己的恶意软件分发平台"). **Why this is the ENDPOINT-PROTECTION-PLATFORM-COMPROMISE-VIA-AUTO-UPDATE-CHANNEL dimension distinct from S-074 (CSP-tightening defense-side regression)**: S-074 covers a defensive-feature regression in the BROWSER; S-155 covers a SECURITY PRODUCT itself becoming the attack vector. **Distinct from S-118 (Google DoubleClick DesckVB RAT malspam — legitimate ad-platform abuse)**: S-118 is ad-platform abuse to deliver RAT; S-155 is the ENDPOINT-PROTECTION-PLATFORM itself being turned into the delivery channel. **Distinct from C-112 (Desktop-consumer-software supply-chain vector DAEMON Tools)**: C-112 is consumer software with low enterprise deployment; S-155 is ENTERPRISE SECURITY SOFTWARE with broad deployment — the blast radius is orders of magnitude larger. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's hosting/CI pipeline runs on infrastructure that may have Trend Micro Apex One or similar EDR deployed (cloud-provider images, on-prem enterprise environments) — a compromised EDR server in the supply chain could push malware to the GameZipper build pipeline, compromising the GameZipper codebase AT BUILD TIME; (b) the AUTO-UPDATE-CHANNEL-HIJACK pattern is RECURRING — SolarWinds Orion (2020), Kaseya VSA (2021), 3CX (2023), now Trend Micro Apex One (2026) — any vendor that ships auto-update to enterprise endpoints is a target; (c) GameZipper itself is a CONTENT DISTRIBUTOR — if GameZipper ever ships a desktop client, PWA wrapper, or browser extension with auto-update, the GameZipper auto-update channel becomes a target for the SAME pattern; (d) the CISA-KEV-same-day pattern means federal regulators consider EDR-fleet-hijack a TOP-TIER national-security risk — GameZipper's incident-response plan must treat any EDR-compromise news as a P0 escalation trigger. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit GameZipper's CI/CD pipeline for any Trend Micro Apex One or other EDR deployment — if found, verify the version is patched (≥ 14.0.0.17079 for Apex One on-prem); (2) for any future GameZipper auto-update channel (desktop client, PWA wrapper, browser extension), implement UPDATE-SIGNING with a hardware-backed key + a TRANSPARENCY-LOG (similar to Binary Transparency / Sigstore Rekor) so that a compromised update channel can be DETECTED by external observers; (3) maintain a list of AUTO-UPDATE-CHANNEL-HIJACK incidents (SolarWinds, Kaseya, 3CX, Trend Micro Apex One) as a recurring QA-pattern checklist item — any GameZipper supply-chain vendor that ships auto-update must be audited against this list; (4) for any future GameZipper incident-response plan, treat "EDR-compromised" as a P0 trigger — invoke code-freeze, isolate build infrastructure, verify all recent GameZipper builds against pre-incident hashes; (5) subscribe to CISA KEV and add any EDR/endpoint-protection CVE to the GameZipper regression-test backlog within 24h of KEV addition.

### 44.5 CVE-2026-34714 Vim Modeline Default-Config Arbitrary Command Execution (CVSS 9.2)

- [S-156] **[P1]** Vim (the ubiquitous Linux/UNIX text editor) disclosed **CVE-2026-34714** — a high-severity arbitrary command execution vulnerability via the modeline feature. CVSS 9.2. CRITICALLY, the vulnerability triggers with VIM DEFAULT CONFIG — the user does NOT need to enable `modelineexpr` or any other opt-in setting. Root cause: (a) the `tabpanel` option is missing the `P_MLE` flag, allowing modeline to inject `%{expr}` expressions WITHOUT requiring `modelineexpr` to be enabled; (b) the `autocmd_add()` function is missing a `check_secure()` call, allowing an attacker to register autocmds INSIDE the sandbox that execute AFTER the sandbox closes. Attack chain: attacker crafts a text file with a malicious modeline → victim opens the file in Vim → Vim parses the modeline → malicious `%{expr}` registers a sandboxed autocmd → sandbox closes → autocmd fires → arbitrary OS command execution → attacker has full control. Affects Vim < 9.2.0272 on Linux, macOS, AND Windows. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/ximenjianxue/article/details/160021682 (April 15 2026 — "Vim modeline 命令执行漏洞(CVE-2026-34714)修复指导" + "受影响版本: Vim < 9.2.0272, 覆盖 Linux/macOS/Windows 全平台" + "漏洞等级: 高危, CVSS 9.2, 利用门槛极低, Vim 默认配置就能触发(不用改任何设置), 属于漏洞中的『核弹级』" + "攻击链: 借助 Vim 的 modeline 特性(文件内嵌配置指令), 恶意代码藏在『看似无害的文本』里, 只要打开就会中招, 恶意文件 → Vim 解析 modeline → 沙箱中注册后门 → 沙箱关闭 → 后门触发 → 代码执行(攻击者完全控制你的系统)" + "修复建议: 升级 Vim >= 9.2.0272"). **Why this is the DEVELOPER-TOOL-DEFAULT-CONFIG-RCE dimension distinct from C-109 (Apifox desktop-client CDN poisoning — network-side dev-tool attack)**: C-109 is a NETWORK-side attack on a dev tool (CDN-compromised update); S-156 is a LOCAL-FILE-side attack on a dev tool (open a malicious file). **Distinct from S-101 (AI assistant browser extension prompt injection)**: S-101 attacks the browser extension; S-156 attacks the editor itself — a completely different application class. **Distinct from S-123 (fake browser-update phishing with clipboard/Win+R PowerShell self-typed execution)**: S-123 chains clipboard paste; S-156 requires NO clipboard interaction — just opening the file. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any GameZipper developer who opens a malicious file in Vim — a README, a config file, an issue-body-dumped-as-text, a vendor-supplied documentation file, a cloned repo's CONTRIBUTING.md — gets FULL SYSTEM COMPROMISE on the dev machine; (b) the dev machine may have GameZipper SSH keys, npm publish tokens, GitHub push access, Cloudflare API tokens — a single malicious file open in Vim can compromise the entire GameZipper codebase + deployment pipeline; (c) the VIM-DEFAULT-CONFIG aspect is critical — unlike most editor RCE bugs that require opt-in settings, this one triggers with ZERO user configuration; (d) the dimension extends to ALL developer tools that auto-execute file-embedded directives (Emacs local variables, VSCode workspace settings, JetBrains .editorconfig, Sublime Text .sublime-project) — each is a potential vector for the SAME class of attack; (e) malicious files can be delivered via GameZipper's own pipelines — a cloned pull-request branch, a vendored third-party dependency's README, an auto-generated API doc. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) enforce `vim >= 9.2.0272` on ALL GameZipper developer machines (verify via `vim --version` in onboarding script); (2) for any GameZipper dev who MUST open untrusted files (security researchers, vendor-support engineers, contributors reviewing external PRs), configure Vim with `set nomodeline` in `~/.vimrc` AND `set sandbox` to defense-in-depth; (3) audit OTHER developer tools for similar modeline/local-variable RCE patterns — Emacs (local variables), VSCode (workspace settings trust dialog), JetBrains (editorconfig), Sublime (sublime-project); (4) for the GameZipper pull-request review pipeline, sandbox the file-opening step — never open a contributed file directly on a developer machine; use a containerized review environment; (5) for any future GameZipper "developer documentation" or "contributor onboarding" flow, explicitly warn contributors NOT to open unknown files in Vim without `nomodeline` configured.

### 44.6 GhostClaw / @openclaw-ai/openclawai npm Scoped-Namespace Typosquat Impersonating OpenClaw Installer (March 10 2026)

- [S-157] **[P2]** eSecurity Planet reported March 10 2026 that a malicious npm package `@openclaw-ai/openclawai` — typosquatting the legitimate OpenClaw installer namespace — deploys a multi-stage infostealer + RAT targeting developers. The package masquerades as an "OpenClaw Installer utility" but instead initiates a multi-stage malware operation stealing credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and AI-CLI tokens (OpenClaw auth tokens, Claude API keys, GitHub PATs, etc.). The DECEPTIVE SCOPED NAME `@openclaw-ai/` (with the hyphenated `openclaw-ai` org name) is designed to look legitimate to developers who have heard of OpenClaw but don't remember the EXACT scoped namespace (the legitimate OpenClaw packages live under `@openclaw/` or directly as `openclaw`). **Source**: esecurityplanet.com/threats/fake-openclaw-npm-package-installs-ghostclaw-malware (March 10 2026 — "A malicious npm package is targeting developers by posing as a legitimate command-line tool while secretly deploying an infostealer and a remote access trojan (RAT). The package, @openclaw-ai/openclawai, masquerades as an OpenClaw Installer utility but instead initiates a multi-stage malware operation to steal credentials, cryptocurrency wallets"). **Why this is the AI-CLI-SCOPED-NPM-TYPOSQUAT-VIA-NAMESPACE-IMPERSONATION dimension distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub marketplace malware — INSIDE the official marketplace)**: S-128 is a malicious SKILL distributed VIA the official OpenClaw marketplace (the user installs from the legit marketplace, but the skill is malware); S-157 is a malicious PACKAGE distributed via typosquatted NPM NAMESPACE (the user runs `npm install` from what they THINK is the legit namespace, but the namespace is a typosquat). **Distinct from S-129 (ClickFix macOS AppleScript delivery — social engineering)**: S-129 tricks the user into running a social-engineering script; S-157 exploits the developer's MUSCLE MEMORY of typing `npm install @openclaw-...` — no social engineering needed beyond the package name. **Distinct from S-146 (CVE-2026-32041 OpenClaw browser-control auth-bootstrap fail-open)**: S-146 is a code-level CVE in OpenClaw itself; S-157 is a SUPPLY-CHAIN attack using OpenClaw's name. **Distinct from S-110 (Typosquatted npm packages stealing CI/CD secrets May 28 2026)**: S-110 covers generic typosquatting; S-157 is the SCOPED-NAMESPACE-IMPERSONATION sub-dimension — the attacker registers `@openclaw-ai/` as a NEW scope (vs the legit `@openclaw/`), which is harder to detect because npm scope-registration is open. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's QA pipeline runs Hermes / Kanban / OpenClaw-class AI agents — any developer who installs an OpenClaw-related package by typing the name from memory (rather than copy-pasting from official docs) is at risk; (b) the SCOPED-NAMESPACE-TYPOSQUAT vector is NOVEL — traditional npm typosquatting uses lookalike UNCODED names (`oepnclaw` vs `openclaw`); scoped-namespace typosquatting uses lookalike ORG names (`@openclaw-ai/` vs `@openclaw/`) which bypass many typosquat-detection tools that focus on character-level similarity; (c) the AI-CLI-TOKEN-TARGETING pattern means the attacker is SPECIFICALLY after the credentials that GameZipper pipeline uses to invoke AI agents — stealing the OpenClaw auth token lets the attacker USE the GameZipper infrastructure to run arbitrary AI-agent actions; (d) the dimension extends to ALL scoped-npm namespaces GameZipper depends on — `@cloudflare/workers-types`, `@playwright/test`, `@hermes/agents`, etc. — each is a potential typosquat target. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit every `@scope/name` in GameZipper's package.json + lockfile against the OFFICIAL namespace (cross-reference npmjs.com) — flag any scope that is NOT the official vendor scope; (2) for any `npm install @something-ai/...` or `npm install @something-cli/...` command, REQUIRE copy-paste from official documentation (not typing from memory); (3) install npm typosquat-detection tooling that supports SCOPE-LEVEL checking (Socket.dev, Snyk, Phylum) and configure it to block scoped-namespace typosquats; (4) maintain a list of OFFICIAL scoped namespaces for every AI-CLI / agent tool GameZipper uses (OpenClaw, Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.) and verify every install against this list; (5) for any future GameZipper pipeline that auto-installs npm packages (e.g., a dynamic skill-installer), add scoped-namespace-verification as a hard gate.

### 44.7 CVE-2026-26176 Windows csc.sys Client-Side Caching Driver TOCTOU Privilege Escalation (April 2026)

- [B-115] **[P2]** Microsoft disclosed **CVE-2026-26176** in April 2026 — a privilege escalation vulnerability in the Windows Client-Side Caching driver (`csc.sys`). Root cause: the `CscQueryDirectory` function (which handles `NtQueryDirectoryFile` calls from user-mode applications) contains a TOCTOU (Time-of-Check Time-of-Use) race condition — the function checks the file path, then re-reads it later, allowing an attacker to swap the path between the check and the use (a classic symlink/race attack). Affects Windows 11 26H2 Canary Preview (and likely all earlier Windows 11 versions). An unprivileged local user can exploit this to escalate to SYSTEM privileges. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/oShuangYue12/article/details/160360940 (June 16 2026 — "客户端缓存文件系统驱动 csc.sys 漏洞 CVE-2026-26176 分析与复现" + "漏洞的主要原因存在于 csc.sys 驱动函数 CscQueryDirectory 处理客户端调用程序 NtQueryDirectoryFile 相关文件操作存在用户态竞争条件 TOCTOU" + "复现环境: Windows 11 26h2 canary preview"). **Why this is the WINDOWS-DRIVER-TOCTOU-LPE dimension distinct from B-080 (CVE-2026-32202 Windows Shell spoofing CISA KEV)**: B-080 is a SHELL-UI spoofing bug (user-visible UI element); B-115 is a KERNEL-DRIVER race condition — completely different attack surface class. **Distinct from B-044 (Android June 2026 124-CVE bulletin 1 actively exploited)**: B-044 is ANDROID; B-115 is WINDOWS — different OS. **Distinct from P-074 (CVE-2026-31431 Linux AF_ALG/splice() LPE — same R198 category)**: P-074 is LINUX; B-115 is WINDOWS — the two CVEs together cover BOTH major desktop OS hosting browsers used by GameZipper players. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's hosting/CI pipeline runs on Windows machines (Windows GitHub Actions runners, Windows developer laptops) — a successful csc.sys TOCTOU LPE on a CI runner can escalate from low-privilege build-user to SYSTEM, compromising the build environment; (b) GameZipper's player base includes Windows desktop users — a malicious GameZipper game-page-delivered exploit chain (e.g., a WebGL bug → browser escape → csc.sys LPE → SYSTEM) becomes MORE SEVERE because the LPE primitive is reliable; (c) the TOCTOU class is RECURRING in Windows kernel drivers — the same vulnerability pattern affected many other drivers (Win32k, HTTP.sys, LUAFV) over the years; (d) the dimension extends to ALL Windows drivers used in the GameZipper Windows test/dev environment — the GameZipper QA device matrix must include kernel-driver-LPE scenarios. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit GameZipper Windows CI runners (GitHub Actions Windows images) for the April 2026 csc.sys patch — verify they auto-update beyond the affected version; (2) for any GameZipper developer Windows machine, verify Windows Update is current (April 2026 patch or later); (3) for any future GameZipper Windows-native tooling (PWA wrapper, desktop app), assume the OS kernel driver layer can be exploited and add application-layer sandboxing (AppContainer, restricted token) as defense-in-depth; (4) for the GameZipper Windows test matrix, add a kernel-driver-LPE-patched-vs-unpatched comparison dimension — verify the same GameZipper game renders identically on patched and unpatched Windows; (5) maintain a list of WINDOWS-KERNEL-DRIVER-LPE CVEs (csc.sys, Win32k, HTTP.sys, LUAFV) as a recurring QA-pattern checklist item.

### 44.8 CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux AF_ALG / splice() LPE — 732-byte PoC, 100% Reliable, All Distros Since 2017 (May 7 2026)

- [P-074] **[P2]** Security research firm **Xint Code** disclosed **CVE-2026-31431** ("Copy Fail") on May 7 2026 — a Linux local privilege escalation affecting ALL Linux distributions with kernels built between 2017 and the patch date. The PoC is a 732-byte Python script (`copy_fail_exp.py`) that achieves 100% reliable root-shell escalation on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux, and (per Xint Code) all mainstream distributions. Root cause: a 4-byte page-cache write via the `authencesn` algorithm's logic flaw, chained through `AF_ALG` (the kernel cryptographic API) + `splice()` system call. Unlike most LPE vulnerabilities that require a race window or specific kernel offset, "Copy Fail" is a STRAIGHT-LINE LOGIC FLAW — no race, no offset, deterministic. A single unprivileged local user account (no network access, no kernel-debug, no pre-installed primitives) is sufficient. The 732-byte PoC works UNMODIFIED across all distributions because the bug is in the GENERIC crypto-subsystem path that every distro enables by default. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/safetybug/article/details/160644342 (May 7 2026 — "Copy Fail 漏洞(CVE-2026-31431) 威胁主流 Linux 发行版, 附利用代码与缓解措施" + "自 2017 年以来, 是适用于所有发行版的容器逃逸原语, 100% 可靠, 由 Xint Code 发现, 代码仅 732 字节" + "多数 Linux 本地权限提升(LPE) 漏洞需要竞争窗口或特定内核偏移量, 而 Copy Fail 是『直线逻辑缺陷』, 两者都不需要" + "只需同一个 732 字节的 Python 脚本, 就能让自 2017 年以来发布的所有 Linux 发行版获得 root 权限" + "authencesn 中的逻辑错误, 通过 AF_ALG 和 splice() 连锁反应, 导致一个 4 字节的页面缓存写入问题, 近十年来一直可被悄悄利用"); xint.io/blog/copy-fail-linux-distributions (May 7 2026 — official Xint Code writeup with PoC hash `sha256: a567d09b15f6e4440e70c9f2aa8edec8ed59f53301952df05c719aa3911687f9` + disclosure tweet `x.com/brian_pak/status/2036173171280486422` + verified distributions: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS kernel 6.17.0-1007-aws, Amazon Linux). **Why this is the BROAD-SCOPE-LINUX-LPE-AFFECTS-HOSTING-INFRA dimension distinct from B-115 (CVE-2026-26176 Windows csc.sys TOCTOU LPE — same R198 category)**: B-115 is WINDOWS; P-074 is LINUX — together the two cover BOTH major desktop/server OS classes in the GameZipper infrastructure. **Distinct from S-153 (CVE-2026-21962 WebLogic Proxy Plug-in ordering flaw — same R198 category)**: S-153 is a SERVER-APPLICATION-LAYER RCE; P-074 is a KERNEL-LAYER LPE — completely different layers, the WebLogic bug is exploited over the network, the Copy Fail bug requires local-user access. **Distinct from S-086 (NGINX heap overflow CVE-2026-42945 — same NGINX server class)**: S-086 is a network-exploitable memory bug in NGINX; P-074 is a locally-exploitable logic bug in the Linux kernel — different attack vectors. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's hosting infrastructure runs on Linux — Cloudflare's edge nodes, GitHub Pages' origin servers, any future GameZipper analytics endpoint, the GameZipper Hermes pipeline server, the GameZipper Linux CI runners — ALL of these are Linux and ALL are vulnerable to CVE-2026-31431 if the kernel was built between 2017 and the patch date; (b) the 100% RELIABILITY + 732-BYTE PoC + ALL DISTROS SINCE 2017 scope means this is a TOP-TIER infrastructure-patch priority — any GameZipper infrastructure component that has not been kernel-patched is at risk; (c) the STRAIGHT-LINE-LOGIC-FLAW (no race, no offset) means traditional defense-in-depth (ASLR, kernel-hardening, seccomp) does NOT help — the bug is deterministic; (d) the dimension intersects with S-155 (Trend Micro Apex One EDR-fleet-hijack) — if an attacker gets LPE on a Linux CI runner via Copy Fail, they can then compromise the CI pipeline and push malicious builds (the auto-update-channel-hijack pattern); (e) the 9-YEAR-LATENT dimension (2017 → 2026) means similar latent LPE bugs likely exist in other kernel subsystems — GameZipper must treat any "long-latent Linux kernel LPE" disclosure as a P0 infrastructure-patch trigger. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit EVERY GameZipper Linux infrastructure component (Cloudflare Workers Linux runtime, GitHub Pages origin, Hermes pipeline server, CI Linux runners) for the kernel version and verify it is patched beyond CVE-2026-31431 fix; (2) for any GameZipper Linux CI runner that processes contributed code (PR builds, dependency builds), assume the runner is vulnerable until kernel-patched — sandbox contributed-code execution in a container with seccomp + read-only filesystem as defense-in-depth (though seccomp does NOT block AF_ALG by default, so add an explicit seccomp filter blocking AF_ALG socket family for untrusted workloads); (3) maintain a KERNEL-CVE-PATCH-STATUS dashboard for every GameZipper Linux host — flag any host running a kernel older than the CVE-2026-31431 fix as P0-patch-required; (4) for any future GameZipper multiplayer or real-time backend running on Linux, add kernel-patch-status to the deployment checklist — a single unpatched host in the fleet can be the LPE stepping stone to full fleet compromise; (5) the 9-YEAR-LATENT dimension means subscribe to kernel-security-disclosure feeds (oss-security, kernel.org CVE feed) and treat any "long-latent Linux kernel LPE" disclosure as a P0 patch trigger regardless of whether the bug is being actively exploited.
