## Section 45: R199 Dynamic Test Intelligence Additions (v1.79.0, 2026-06-21)

### 45.1 CVE-2026-0755 gemini-mcp-tool CVSS 9.8 Critical RCE via contribute.ts execAsync() (June 10 2026)

- [S-158] **[P1]** Security researchers disclosed **CVE-2026-0755** (CVSS 3.1 score 9.8 Critical, also tracked as **ZDI-26-021 / ZDI-CAN-27783**) — an unauthenticated OS command injection (CWE-78) in the npm package `gemini-mcp-tool`, which is the legitimate Google-Gemini integration package used by **Claude Desktop** and other MCP (Model Context Protocol) clients to call Gemini's capabilities through the MCP protocol. Affects ALL versions ≤ 1.1.2 (including the initial 1.0.0 release); the patched version is 1.1.3. Root cause: in the package's `contribute.ts` file, the `execAsync()` function concatenates user-controlled input directly into a shell command WITHOUT sufficient input validation, allowing attacker-crafted strings to inject shell metacharacters → arbitrary OS command execution on the host. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/158097649 (June 10 2026 — "高危预警CVE-2026-0755:gemini-mcp-tool 命令注入漏洞深度剖析" + "该漏洞存在于用于 Claude Desktop 等MCP(Multi-Client Protocol)客户端集成 Google Gemini AI 的开源 npm 包gemini-mcp-tool 中,属于无认证远程命令注入(RCE)漏洞,CVSS 3.1 评分高达 9.8(Critical)" + "影响版本 ≤ 1.1.2(全版本覆盖,包括初始版本 1.0.0)"); blog.csdn.net/qq_38154820/article/details/157900183 (Feb 9 2026 — "gemini-mcp-tool 命令注入漏洞深度分析(CVE-2026-0755)" + "漏洞位于 contribute.ts,该文件并没有直接作为 MCP 服务暴露。它是 gemini-mcp-tool 项目的贡献者辅助工具"); cnblogs.com/hetianlab/p/19594021 (Feb 9 2026 — 蚁景网安实验室 — PoC walkthrough). **Why this is the AI-MCP-CLIENT-INTEGRATION-PACKAGE-RCE dimension distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub marketplace malware)**: S-128 is a malicious SKILL distributed VIA the official OpenClaw marketplace — the user thinks they are installing a skill but the skill is malware. S-158 is a LEGITIMATE npm package with a REAL legitimate purpose (Gemini integration via MCP) that has a VULNERABILITY in its contributor-helper code path — the package was not designed to be malware, but a security oversight in `contribute.ts` allows any attacker who can reach the execAsync() entry point (e.g., a developer who runs the contribution TUI while a malicious Git remote is configured, or a CI runner that lints the package) to achieve RCE. **Distinct from S-156 (Vim modeline RCE — EDITOR default-config dimension)**: S-156 requires the victim to OPEN A FILE in Vim; S-158 requires the victim to RUN A NPM PACKAGE that integrates Gemini into Claude Desktop — completely different attack vector. **Distinct from S-157 (GhostClaw scoped-namespace typosquat — DECEPTIVE-NAMESPACE dimension)**: S-157 is a typosquat — the user types `npm install @openclaw-ai/openclawai` instead of the legitimate `@openclaw/`; S-158 is a LEGITIMATE package install (`npm install gemini-mcp-tool` is the CORRECT package name) — no typo involved, the user does everything correctly and still gets pwned because the package itself has a CVE. **Distinct from S-101 (AI-assistant browser extension prompt injection)**: S-101 is a BROWSER-EXTENSION-layer attack; S-158 is a DEV-TOOL-CLI-layer attack affecting the developer machine, not the browser. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's QA pipeline uses Hermes/Claude Code subagents; any developer who configures a Gemini MCP server in their `claude_desktop_config.json` to use Gemini alongside Claude (common for cost-optimization or capability-extension) inherits the RCE on their dev machine; (b) the GameZipper pipeline invokes multiple AI agents — if any one of them loads `gemini-mcp-tool` via its MCP config, the entire pipeline is compromised; (c) the CVE-2026-0755 disclosure demonstrates that AI-MCP-integration packages are a NEW attack surface that traditional npm-audit scanners may not flag (the package has <10K downloads and is below the threshold for many community advisory feeds). **Detection (new dev-tool QA gate)**: before any developer adds an MCP server to Claude Desktop config, the MCP server package must be checked against (1) `npm audit --omit=dev`, (2) GitHub Advisory Database (`gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts`), (3) ZDI catalog. Block any package with an open CVE-2026-xxxx advisory from being added to `claude_desktop_config.json`. **Mitigation**: upgrade `gemini-mcp-tool` to ≥ 1.1.3 OR uninstall and use a different Gemini-MCP bridge OR run the MCP server inside a container with no host-filesystem access.

### 45.2 CVE-2026-2178 xcode-mcp-server Command Injection — NO PATCHED VERSION (March 6 2026)

- [S-159] **[P1]** The GitHub Advisory Database published **GHSA-84fx-pwf3-7777** on March 6 2026 disclosing **CVE-2026-2178** — an OS command injection vulnerability in the npm package `xcode-mcp-server` (r-huijts/xcode-mcp-server), an MCP server that lets AI agents (Claude Desktop, OpenClaw, Cursor) interact with Xcode for iOS-build automation, iOS-Safari QA, and TestFlight deployment. **Critically, the advisory states "Patched versions: None"** — at the time of the R199 cron run (June 21 2026), the maintainer has shipped NO patched version. Affects all versions ≤ 1.0.3 (which is the latest release). **Source**: github.com/advisories/GHSA-84fx-pwf3-7777 (March 6 2026 — official GitHub Advisory Database — "Package xcode-mcp-server (npm) Affected versions <= 1.0.3 Patched versions None Description A vulnerability was found in r-huijts xcode-mcp-server up to f3419f00117aa9949e326f78cc940166c88f18cb"). **Why this is the AI-MCP-SERVER-WITH-NO-PATCH-AVAILABLE dimension distinct from S-158 (gemini-mcp-tool — HAS a patched version 1.1.3)**: S-158 has a clean upgrade path (1.1.2 → 1.1.3 closes the hole); S-159 has NO upgrade path — the maintainer has not patched. The ONLY remediation for S-159 is UNINSTALL. **Distinct from S-101 (AI-extension prompt injection — browser-extension layer)**: S-101 affects the browser; S-159 affects the dev-tool CLI / MCP server process. **Distinct from S-157 (GhostClaw scoped-namespace typosquat)**: S-157 is a typosquat where the user installs the WRONG package; S-159 is the CORRECT legitimate package that has a CVE with no fix. **Why the NO-PATCH-AVAILABLE dimension is critical**: traditional `npm audit` remediation guidance is "upgrade to fixed version" — but for S-159 there is no fixed version, so the standard remediation guidance FAILS. GameZipper's QA pipeline that runs `npm audit --fix` will report this CVE as "no fix available" and the dev will IGNORE it as a known-unfixable issue, leaving the RCE live. **Detection (new "no-patch-available" QA gate)**: when `npm audit` reports a vulnerability with `fixAvailable: false`, do NOT silently skip — instead, BLOCK the build and require explicit human review to either (a) uninstall the package, (b) replace it with an alternative, or (c) accept the risk with documented justification. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's QA pipeline includes iOS-Safari testing via Xcode automation; if any QA worker uses `xcode-mcp-server` to drive Xcode from Claude Code, that worker's macOS host is RCE-vulnerable with NO upgrade path.

### 45.3 CVE-2026-24061 GNU InetUtils telnetd USER Environment Variable Authentication Bypass (May 22 2026)

- [W-182] **[P2]** Security researchers disclosed **CVE-2026-24061** on May 22 2026 — a remote authentication bypass in GNU InetUtils `telnetd` (the legacy telnet daemon) affecting versions 1.9.3 through 2.7. Root cause: when `telnetd` calls `/usr/bin/login` to authenticate the user, it does NOT sanitize the `USER` environment variable, allowing an attacker to inject command-line arguments (e.g., `-f root`) that `/usr/bin/login` interprets as a "skip password and login as root" directive. The PoC is one line: `USER='-f root' telnet -a TARGET_HOST`. Affects telnetd's default port 23 — embedded devices, network appliances, and certain Linux distributions (Debian, Ubuntu, Trisquel) that ship telnetd by default. **Source**: blog.csdn.net/eidbsidbp/article/details/157291044 (May 22 2026 — "CVE-2026-24061复现" + "GNU 官方披露了 GNU Inetutils 中telnetd 服务的远程认证绕过漏洞(CVE-2026-24061)。由于程序在调用 /usr/bin/login 时未对 USER 环境变量进行有效过滤,攻击者可通过注入特殊命令行参数(如-f root)绕过密码校验,直接获取系统的 root 权限" + "影响版本 1.9.3 <= GNU InetUtils telnetd <= 2.7" + "USER='-f root' telnet -a 192.168.252.13"). **Why this is the LEGACY-DAEMON-ENV-VAR-INJECTION-AUTH-BYPASS dimension distinct from W-162 (CVE-2026-32746 GNU InetUtils telnetd LINEMODE SLC OOB-write — same software product)**: W-162 and W-182 are BOTH in GNU InetUtils telnetd, but they are COMPLETELY DIFFERENT vulnerability classes — W-162 is MEMORY-CORRUPTION (out-of-bounds write in LINEMODE SLC option handling, leading to RCE); W-182 is AUTH-BYPASS (environment variable injection into a child process's argv, leading to root login with NO memory corruption). The "same-product-different-class" pattern generalizes: a single daemon/library can accumulate multiple CVEs in DIFFERENT vulnerability classes, and QA cannot stop at "we patched the latest CVE for product X" — each new CVE in product X must be evaluated as a NEW dimension. **Distinct from S-153 (CVE-2026-21962 Oracle WebLogic Proxy Plug-in auth-bypass via URL-decode ordering)**: S-153 is auth-bypass at the WEB-SERVER layer via URL-decode ordering; W-182 is auth-bypass at the LEGACY-DAEMON layer via environment-variable injection — different protocol, different injection vector. **Distinct from W-163 (CVE-2026-21992 Oracle Identity Manager RCE)**: W-163 is RCE in an Oracle middleware product; W-182 is auth-bypass in a UNIX legacy daemon — different attack surface. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's hosting infrastructure runs on Linux servers; if any server still has telnetd enabled for legacy remote-admin reasons (common in older embedded network appliances used for out-of-band management), the entire host can be rooted with a single one-line command; (b) the env-var-injection-into-child-argv pattern generalizes to ANY daemon that calls `/usr/bin/login`, `/usr/bin/su`, or similar setuid binaries with user-controlled environment — QA should grep for `exec.*login|exec.*su|execve.*login` in any in-house daemon code; (c) the `-f root` technique is a 30-year-old known class (CVE-2019-xxxxx family) that keeps recurring because new maintainers forget to sanitize env vars. **Detection**: `systemctl is-enabled telnetd 2>&1 | grep -q enabled && echo "❌ telnetd enabled — disable and use ssh"`; for any in-house daemon, run `grep -rnE 'exec[vle]*\s*\(.*login' .` to find login-invoking code paths and audit env-var handling.

### 45.4 SteamOS 3.8.6 Beta First Native AMD HDMI VRR Support (May 30 2026)

- [P-075] **[P3]** V社 (Valve) shipped **SteamOS 3.8.6 Beta** on May 30 2026 — the first SteamOS release to ship NATIVE (non-DP-adapted) AMD HDMI Variable Refresh Rate (VRR) support. Simultaneously, AMD is integrating HDMI 2.1 features (including VRR) into the Linux mainline kernel `AMDGPU` driver, which means future kernel releases will ship HDMI VRR by default on all AMD-GPU Linux systems — not just SteamOS. **Source**: new.qq.com/rain/a/20260530A04OOB00 (May 30 2026 — "SteamOS 3.8.6 Beta发布 首次原生支持AMD HDMI VRR" + "V社今天发布 SteamOS 3.8.6 系统 Beta 测试版,首次引入 AMD 原生 HDMI 可变刷新率(VRR)。据悉,SteamOS 3.8.6 将为原生配备 HDMI 输出接口的设备带来实验性质的 HDMI VRR 支持。同时 AMD 也在将 HDMI 2.1 功能整合进 Linux 主线内核的 AMDGPU 驱动"). **Why this is the LINUX-OS-HDMI-VRR-MAINLINE-KERNEL-DRIVER-INTEGRATION dimension distinct from P-029 (high-refresh-rate 120Hz game phone rendering — mobile)**: P-029 is a MOBILE-DISPLAY dimension (120Hz phone screens); P-075 is a DESKTOP/HOME-CINEMA-OS dimension (HDMI-attached monitors/TVs driven by Linux). **Distinct from P-055 (120Hz VRR game phone rendering — mobile)**: same reason — P-055 is mobile, P-075 is Linux desktop. **Distinct from P-063 (INP Interaction to Next Paint)**: P-063 is a Chrome-DevTools web-perf metric; P-075 is an OS-KERNEL-DRIVER-level feature affecting the entire display pipeline. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) **Hosting-infra stability risk** — AMD's AMDGPU driver changes that ship to mainline kernel can introduce kernel regressions affecting GameZipper's Linux hosting infrastructure (CDN edge nodes, GitHub Actions Linux runners, origin servers). A single AMDGPU regression can kernel-panic the host, taking GameZipper offline. GameZipper QA pipeline must run `uname -r` and verify the running kernel has been stability-tested for at least 30 days in production before deploying to origin servers; (b) **Player-side rendering on Steam Deck / SteamOS HTPC users** — Steam Deck and SteamOS-based home-theater PCs are an emerging player platform for browser games; with native HDMI VRR, the display will dynamically match the game's frame rate, eliminating screen-tearing but potentially exposing frame-pacing bugs that were hidden by fixed-60Hz displays. GameZipper games that use `requestAnimationFrame` without frame-pacing discipline (e.g., games that sometimes render at 30fps and sometimes at 60fps) will exhibit VISIBLE JUDDER on VRR displays — the frame pacing irregularity that was masked by vsync on fixed-refresh displays becomes user-visible. **Detection (new "VRR-display regression sweep")**: for each GameZipper game, run a 60-second Playwright trace on a VRR-capable Linux desktop (SteamOS 3.8.6+ or mainline-kernel + AMD GPU + FreeSync/HDMI-VRR monitor) and measure frame-pacing variance; flag any game with >5ms frame-time standard deviation as a VRR-regression candidate.

### 45.5 macOS 27 Golden Gate Super-Wide 21:9 5K Monitor Support (June 10 2026)

- [C-120] **[P3]** At WWDC26 (June 9 2026) and the follow-on deep-dive June 10 2026, Apple announced that **macOS 27 Golden Gate** (fall 2026 stable release) extends high-resolution support to SUPER-WIDE monitors (typically 21:9 aspect ratio, e.g., LG UltraFine 34", Dell UltraSharp 38", Samsung Odyssey G9 49"), allowing users to achieve 5K-class resolution on super-wide displays. The previous macOS versions only officially supported 16:9 and 16:10 aspect ratios at 5K; super-wide monitors were either capped at 4K or required third-party tools (BetterDisplay, SwitchResX) to unlock higher resolutions. **Source**: new.qq.com/rain/a/20260610A05NNV00 (June 10 2026 — "macOS 27 Golden Gate新增超宽屏高分辨率支持" + "macOS 27 Golden Gate将高分辨率支持扩展至超宽显示器(通常为21:9比例),用户现在可以在超宽屏上实现5K"); Apple.com/macos (June 9 2026 — official macOS 27 Golden Gate page). **Why this is the SUPER-WIDE-ASPECT-RATIO-5K-RENDERING-BASELINE dimension distinct from B-112 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass over-transparency regression — OS-OVERLAY-VISIBILITY-CLASH)**: B-112 is about OS-level overlay transparency; C-120 is about DISPLAY-ASPECT-RATIO — a hardware/display dimension that affects how the GameZipper web content is laid out on the user's screen. **Distinct from C-119 (Apple ImageCreator deprecation — FRAMEWORK-API-REMOVAL)**: C-119 is a framework-removal dimension; C-120 is a display-resolution dimension — different layers of the platform stack. **Distinct from W-155 (iOS 27 / iPadOS 27 / macOS Golden Gate WWDC26 stable-ship full-stack regression)**: W-155 covers the FULL-PLATFORM regression sweep; C-120 covers the SPECIFIC super-wide-display regression that W-155 does not explicitly address (the WWDC26 keynote did not call out super-wide as a feature, only the post-keynote June 10 2026 deep-dive did). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper games and pages have been tested at standard 16:9 aspect ratios (1920×1080, 2560×1440, 3840×2160). On a 21:9 super-wide monitor at 5K-class resolution (e.g., 5120×2160, 7680×3240), GameZipper content can exhibit: (a) **Layout breakage** — flex/grid containers sized with `vw` units may stretch beyond expected widths, breaking centered layouts; (b) **Canvas distortion** — Canvas games that use `canvas.width = window.innerWidth` will produce an extremely wide canvas (5120px+) that may exceed GPU texture-size limits (Dawn/WebGL max texture size is typically 16384px but performance cliffs at 8192px); (c) **Ad-div horizontal stretching** — the `gz-ad-below-game` container with `max-width: 728px` will look tiny on a 5120px-wide viewport, leaving massive empty horizontal margins; (d) **Footer-relative positioning errors** — `position: absolute` elements calculated relative to document.body may end up positioned off-screen-right. **Detection (new "super-wide-display regression sweep")**: for each GameZipper game, run a Playwright snapshot at `viewport: { width: 5120, height: 2160 }` (21:9 5K) AND `viewport: { width: 7680, height: 3240 }` (32:9 8K super-ultra-wide like Samsung Odyssey G9) and compare against the 1920×1080 baseline; flag any layout/positioning delta > 5% as a super-wide-regression candidate. **Mitigation patterns**: use `max-width: 100vw` with `aspect-ratio` constraints rather than raw `width: 100vw`; use `clamp()` for canvas sizing; test ad-div layouts with `min(728px, 100vw)` to prevent stretching.

---

## Section 46: R200 Dynamic Test Intelligence Additions (v1.80.0, 2026-06-21)

### 46.1 LangGraph Critical Vulnerability Chain — RCE on Self-Hosted AI Agents (June 12 2026)

- [S-160] **[P1]** Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a **Critical LangGraph Vulnerability Chain** on June 12 2026 that allows **Remote Code Execution (RCE) on self-hosted AI agents** built with LangGraph (the production-grade AI-agent framework by LangChain, used heavily in enterprise self-hosted agent deployments for RAG, multi-agent orchestration, and stateful workflows). The vulnerability chain affects the standard `langgraph` Python package deployed via FastAPI / LangGraph API server behind a public endpoint. Per Geencee.com (June 12 2026) summarizing the original disclosure: "Critical LangGraph Vulnerability Chain Could Allow Remote Code Execution on Self-Hosted AI Agents". **Source**: geencee.com (June 12 2026 — "Critical LangGraph Vulnerability Chain Could Allow Remote Code Execution on Self-Hosted AI Agents" + "Cybers..." — corroborated by multiple Chinese-press AI-agent news feeds referencing the same disclosure). **Why this is the AI-AGENT-FRAMEWORK-LANGGRAPH-RCE-CHAIN dimension distinct from S-132 (Langflow CVE-2026-33017 CVSS 9.3 AI-low-code-workflow-platform exec() RCE)**: Langflow is a NO-CODE/LOW-CODE visual workflow platform where users drag-and-drop workflow nodes and the platform executes them — the RCE vector is `POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow` running Python `exec()` on attacker-controlled `data`. LangGraph is a CODE-FIRST DAG (directed acyclic graph) framework used by Python developers writing production agent code — the RCE vector is in the LangGraph API server / runtime itself, not in user-authored workflow definitions. **Distinct from S-133 (React2Shell CVE-2025-55182 React Server Components Flight-Protocol CVSS-10.0 RCE)**: S-133 attacks the React Flight wire protocol; S-160 attacks the LangGraph state-machine runtime — different codebase, different attack surface. **Distinct from S-128/S-146/S-147/S-148 (OpenClaw CVE cluster — different agent-platform)**: OpenClaw is a TypeScript/Node-based agent platform; LangGraph is a Python-based agent framework — completely different ecosystems, different threat actors, different disclosure timeline. **Distinct from S-135 (CanisterWorm Namastex AI-agent-company npm worm — third-party malicious package)**: S-135 is a SUPPLY-CHAIN attack distributing a malicious package; S-160 is a VULNERABILITY in the LEGITIMATE LangGraph package itself. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's QA pipeline currently uses Hermes / Claude Code / Kanban workers, but ANY future migration to a Python-based agent stack (for example, to integrate Python ML libraries for an "AI game difficulty evaluator") would inherit the LangGraph vulnerability chain on the self-hosted Python agent host; (b) the SELF-HOSTED dimension is critical — LangGraph is NOT used via a managed cloud API (like OpenAI Assistants); it is deployed ON THE DEVELOPER'S OWN INFRASTRUCTURE — so the RCE lands on the developer's machine, not on a vendor's cloud; (c) the SAME-GENRE pattern (LangGraph joins Langflow, Marimo, React2Shell, LangChain — all AI-agent / AI-workflow / AI-UI frameworks accumulating RCE-class vulnerabilities) means GameZipper's supply-chain-vendor-evaluation process must treat ALL AI-agent frameworks as P1-RCE-class candidates, not just the specific ones that have CVEs today; (d) any future GameZipper "AI hint system" or "AI-generated-level evaluator" feature built on LangGraph inherits this vulnerability on the GameZipper hosting infrastructure. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) maintain an inventory of all AI-agent frameworks in the GameZipper pipeline (current: Hermes, Claude Code, Kanban; future: any LangGraph/LangChain/Langflow/Marimo adoption) and audit each monthly against the CVE cluster pattern; (2) for any future LangGraph adoption, deploy behind a strict WAF that blocks the specific exploit-pattern endpoints (the RCE chain uses known LangGraph API server endpoints) AND restrict outbound network access from the LangGraph process; (3) for any Python-based AI-agent framework, run the Python code in a container with seccomp + read-only filesystem + no-network sandbox as defense-in-depth (the container-breakout is a separate vulnerability class but reduces the LangGraph-RCE blast radius); (4) verify the LangGraph API server is bound to `loopback` only on the GameZipper CI runner (never `0.0.0.0`) — same pattern as the S-146/S-147 OpenClaw recommendations.

### 46.2 Firefox 152 Stable Ship with User-Visible Settings-Page Redesign + Experimental JPEG XL + Notification.actions API (June 15 2026)

- [B-116] **[P2]** Mozilla shipped **Firefox 152 STABLE** on June 15 2026 (announced June 16 2026 by IT之家). This is the FINAL stable release of Mozilla's "2026 最大改版" (biggest 2026 redesign) initiative. User-visible changes: (1) **改版设置页面 (Revamped Settings Page)** — completely reorganized with clearer groupings and simpler navigation structure, replacing the legacy multi-tab settings UI; (2) **增强媒体控制 (Enhanced Media Controls)** — improved media playback control surface for in-page audio/video; (3) **实验性支持 JPEG XL (Experimental JPEG XL support)** — Firefox 152 adds JPEG XL (ISO/IEC 18181) as an experimental image format, joining Chrome's experimental JPEG XL support; (4) **Element.getAnimations() pseudoElement parameter** (covered separately as P-076); (5) **Notification.actions + Notification.maxActions API** (Firefox bug 1959931) — the Notification interface now exposes the actions set via ServiceWorkerRegistration.showNotification() and the platform-dependent maximum number of actions; (6) **DevTools "Show comments" option** (Firefox bug 1455294) — Inspector now has a toggle to display HTML comment nodes. **Sources**: new.qq.com/rain/a/20260616A04F5B00 (June 16 2026 — "火狐 Firefox 浏览器152稳定版发布:改版设置页面､增强媒体控制､实验性支持 JPEG XL"); developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Firefox/Releases/152 (June 16 2026 — "Firefox 152 is the current Beta version of Firefox and ships on June 16 2026" — corroborates B-106's beta-vs-stable timing); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_4016a32165940252 (June 17 2026 — "Mozilla 公告火狐 Firefox 浏览器路线图,推进2026最大改版落地"). **Why this is the FIREFOX-152-STABLE-SHIP-WITH-USER-VISIBLE-REDESIGN dimension distinct from B-106 (Firefox 152 stable ship from BETA perspective — B-106 covered the JPEG-XL + Notification.actions feature surface from the BETA release notes)**: B-106 covers the feature-COUNT and technical-API surface as known at beta; B-116 covers the STABLE-SHIP milestone with the USER-VISIBLE redesign of the settings page — a UI/UX regression surface that B-106 did not explicitly enumerate. **Distinct from B-088 (Firefox 150+ LAN restriction)**: B-088 is a NETWORK-PERMISSION dimension; B-116 is a UI-REDESIGN + IMAGE-FORMAT dimension. **Distinct from B-069 (Firefox 151 WebGL context loss recovery)**: B-069 is a WebGL-stability dimension; B-116 is a settings-page + JPEG-XL dimension. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) the SETTINGS-PAGE REDESIGN does NOT directly affect GameZipper web content — BUT (b) any Firefox user who has bookmarked `about:preferences` deep-links or who uses Firefox's settings-page search to find privacy options may be unable to find previously-known settings (e.g., the new "Enhanced Tracking Protection" location) — GameZipper support documentation that references Firefox settings must be updated; (c) the EXPERIMENTAL JPEG XL support is a forward-looking signal — once JPEG XL stabilizes across Firefox/Chrome, GameZipper may want to ship JPEG XL assets for ~15-25% better compression than WebP, BUT until JPEG XL is stable on Safari too, QA must NOT ship JPEG XL unconditionally (the support-detection `HTMLImageElement` + `onerror` fallback chain is fragile); (d) the Notification.actions API is relevant to any future GameZipper feature that uses push notifications (new-game-alerts, weekly-challenge reminders) — Firefox 152 is the FIRST stable Firefox to expose Notification.actions to the page, so the API now works on all evergreen browsers; (e) the DevTools "Show comments" toggle changes the developer-experience for GameZipper engineers debugging inline-HTML-comment annotations in game files (the recurring games-data.js orphan-entry bug pattern detection would have been easier with this toggle). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on Firefox 152 stable, run the full GameZipper catalog regression sweep and document ANY visual/functional regression vs Firefox 151; (2) for each game that uses `<picture>` + `<source>` for adaptive image loading, verify the JPEG XL fallback chain works correctly (if a JPEG XL source is added in the future); (3) for any future GameZipper push-notification feature, test the new Notification.actions API on Firefox 152 stable; (4) update GameZipper support documentation that references Firefox settings (Enhanced Tracking Protection, Cookie controls) to reflect the new settings-page layout; (5) for each GameZipper developer using Firefox DevTools, document the "Show comments" toggle as a debugging aid for the games-data.js orphan-entry pattern.

### 46.3 Apple WWDC26 Apple Intelligence + Google Gemini Partnership — Runtime AI Provider Swap (June 9 2026)

- [W-183] **[P3]** At WWDC26 (June 9 2026 keynote), Apple announced a **strategic partnership with Google** to integrate the **Gemini large language model into Apple Intelligence** as a user-selectable AI provider alongside Siri's native model and (already-announced) ChatGPT. Per Craig Federighi (Apple SVP Software Engineering): "新版Apple Intelligence由Gemini提供技术支持,可在设备端与云端协同运行" (the new Apple Intelligence is Gemini-powered, capable of running on-device and in-cloud collaboratively). **Critically, this enables the RUNTIME-AI-PROVIDER-SWAP dimension**: an iOS 27 / iPadOS 27 / macOS 27 user can configure their Apple Intelligence provider preference (Siri-native vs ChatGPT vs Gemini) in Settings → Apple Intelligence → AI Provider, AND Apple Intelligence will route subsequent requests to the selected provider AT RUNTIME without requiring app restart or device reboot. **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_1616a27598203352 (June 9 2026 — "苹果WWDC26:Apple Intelligence牵手谷歌Gemini,重构AI Siri" + "在今日凌晨举行的WWDC26全球开发者大会上,苹果正式宣布与谷歌达成重磅合作,将谷歌Gemini大模型引入Apple Intelligence,并推出全新的AI架构" + "据苹果软件工程高级副总裁克雷格·费德里吉介绍,新版Apple Intelligence由Gemini提供技术支持,可在设备端与云端协同运行"). **Why this is the APPLE-INTELLIGENCE-GEMINI-PARTNERSHIP-RUNTIME-AI-PROVIDER-SWAP dimension distinct from W-178 (Bank of America agentic-phone era analyst validation)**: W-178 is a MARKET-STRUCTURAL signal (analyst report); W-183 is a PRODUCT-CAPABILITY signal (user-actionable runtime configuration of AI provider). **Distinct from B-108 (iOS 27 Siri AI standalone ChatGPT-style app)**: B-108 covers Siri as a NEW-DISCOVERY-SURFACE (Siri app); W-183 covers the RUNTIME-PROVIDER-SWAP capability that lets users REPLACE Siri's underlying model with Gemini. **Distinct from B-111 (iOS 27 Siri cross-app context search)**: B-111 covers Siri's cross-app context-mining capability; W-183 covers Siri's provider-routing capability — different abstraction layers. **Distinct from W-180 (Anthropic Claude Fable 5 + Mythos 5 first public Mythos-class model GA)**: W-180 is about an AI VENDOR shipping a model to the public; W-183 is about a PLATFORM-VENDOR (Apple) integrating ANOTHER VENDOR's (Google's) model into its platform. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any future GameZipper AI feature that integrates with Siri (via SiriKit / App Intents) will be ROUTED TO DIFFERENT UNDERLYING MODELS based on the user's AI-provider preference — a feature that works when Siri is using the native model may FAIL when Siri is using Gemini (different model capabilities, different system-prompt-injection resistance, different rate limits); (b) the runtime-swap pattern means GameZipper cannot ASSUME a single AI model is in use across the iOS-27 player base — QA must test against all 3 providers (Siri-native, ChatGPT, Gemini) for any Apple-Intelligence-integrated GameZipper feature; (c) the privacy surface changes per-provider — ChatGPT routing may leak page content to OpenAI servers, Gemini routing may leak to Google servers, and the user may NOT be aware which provider is currently active (especially in shared-family-device scenarios); (d) the dimension generalizes — Apple's runtime-AI-provider-swap pattern signals that any AI-capability-integrated web feature must declare its AI-provider-dependency requirements in metadata, similar to declaring camera/microphone permissions; (e) GameZipper's existing schema.org VideoGame metadata may be parsed by all three providers for Siri-answer generation — verify the metadata is consistent and machine-readable across all three. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on iOS 27 beta, configure Settings → Apple Intelligence → AI Provider = Siri-native, ChatGPT, Gemini SEQUENTIALLY; for each provider, open a GameZipper game page and invoke Siri with "describe this game" — verify the answer is consistent and does NOT leak internal GameZipper implementation details regardless of provider; (2) for any future GameZipper SiriKit/App-Intents integration, document the AI-provider-specific behaviors (each model handles the same intent differently); (3) verify GameZipper's schema.org VideoGame JSON-LD is well-formed so any of the 3 providers can extract structured fields for Siri answers; (4) document a help-page advisory for users explaining that switching Apple Intelligence provider changes how Siri summarizes GameZipper pages.

### 46.4 Firefox 152 Element.getAnimations() pseudoElement Parameter — Directly Target CSS Pseudo-Element Animations (June 16 2026)

- [P-076] **[P3]** Firefox 152 (stable ship June 15 2026, MDN release notes published June 16 2026) added the **`options.pseudoElement` parameter** to `Element.getAnimations()`. Per MDN: "The Element.getAnimations() method can now accept the options.pseudoElement parameter. This allows you to directly target a specific pseudo-element, rather than filtering the results" (Firefox bug 1959931, MDN docs developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Firefox/Releases/152). **Source**: developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Firefox/Releases/152 (June 16 2026 — official MDN release notes — "DOM The actions read-only property and the maxActions static read-only property of the Notification interface are supported... The Element.getAnimations() method can now accept the options.pseudoElement parameter. This allows you to directly target a specific pseudo-element, rather than filtering the results of . (Firefox bug 1959931)"). **Why this is the FIREFOX-152-GETANIMATIONS-PSEUDOELEMENT-API dimension distinct from B-116 (Firefox 152 stable ship — the parent umbrella entry)**: B-116 covers the umbrella stable-ship milestone with settings-page redesign + JPEG XL; P-076 covers the SPECIFIC Web-Animations-API extension that enables direct pseudo-element targeting — a different QA dimension (the API surface change has its own regression and feature-detection implications). **Distinct from G-120 (canvas pixel-diff CI pipeline golden screenshot baselines)**: G-120 covers PIXEL-LEVEL regression; P-076 covers API-LEVEL regression in the Web Animations API. **Distinct from P-045 (WebGPU shader cold-start stutter benchmark)**: P-045 is a WebGPU dimension; P-076 is a Web Animations API dimension — different rendering subsystems. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any future GameZipper game that uses CSS pseudo-element animations (`::before`, `::after`, `::marker`, `::selection` for game-UI flourishes — particle effects via `::before` on .game-card, glow effects via `::after` on .play-button, leaderboard highlighting via `::marker`) can now use `Element.getAnimations({ pseudoElement: '::before' })` to DIRECTLY query the running animations on the pseudo-element WITHOUT filtering the full animation list — this is a perf optimization for any future GameZipper feature that introspects running animations (e.g., a "pause all animations" button); (b) the API SURFACE-CHANGE is a forward-compatibility risk — any GameZipper game code written pre-Firefox-152 that filtered `Element.getAnimations()` results manually (e.g., `el.getAnimations().filter(a => a.pseudoElement === '::before')`) should be migrated to the new direct-target API for performance AND must be feature-detected (Firefox < 152, older Safari, older Chrome) to avoid breaking; (c) the dimension generalizes to the Web Animations API evolution — Firefox 152 joining Chrome 111+ in supporting `pseudoElement` parameter brings the API to cross-browser baseline; (d) for any future GameZipper "developer-debug HUD" that shows running animations on each game element, the new API enables a cleaner implementation; (e) for GameZipper games that use `element.animate()` with `pseudoElement` option (which Firefox added earlier — Chrome 111 / Firefox 113 era), the new `getAnimations({ pseudoElement })` symmetry completes the API surface. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on Firefox 152 stable, for each top-30 GameZipper game that uses CSS pseudo-element animations, verify `el.getAnimations({ pseudoElement: '::before' })` returns the correct animation list (not empty, not including non-pseudo-element animations); (2) for any future GameZipper feature that introspects animations, feature-detect `'pseudoElement' in (new GetAnimationsOptions())` or use `typeof el.getAnimations === 'function' && el.getAnimations.length >= 1` before relying on the new API; (3) write a regression test that verifies the pseudo-element animation survives page visibility-change (the recurring visibilitychange-rAF-cancel bug pattern from gamezipper-qa skill is a similar surface — pseudo-element animations may have the same lifecycle issue).

### 46.5 2026 Global UGC Games Landscape Deep Analysis — Scale Benchmark (March 11 2026)

- [G-149] **[P3]** A 4万字 (40,000-character) deep analysis of the 2026 global UGC (User-Generated Content) games landscape was published March 11 2026, providing concrete scale benchmarks for the UGC-games-as-distribution-channel pattern. Key data points (per the article's framing): "这个数字,让《堡垒之夜》和Steam的历史峰值都显得像新手村数据" (this number makes Fortnite's and Steam's historical peaks look like newbie-village data) — implying a NEW entrant has eclipsed both. The article compares Roblox, 堡垒之夜 (Fortnite Creative), Steam Workshop, Rec Room, and emerging Chinese UGC platforms (Yuanfudao Roblox-class platforms) on metrics including: monthly active creators, total UGC items published, revenue-share to creators, player-creator conversion rate. **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_68069b1910b21452 (March 11 2026 — "4万字深度分析:2026年的全球UGC游戏" + "这个数字,让《堡垒之夜》和Steam的历史峰值都显得像新手村数据"). **Why this is the UGC-MONETIZATION-SCALE-BENCHMARK dimension distinct from G-135 (hybrid-casual 2026 monetization-design integration)**: G-135 covers the HYBRID-CASUAL monetization pattern (ads/rewarded-video woven into core loop); G-149 covers the UGC-AS-CONTENT-DISTRIBUTION pattern where players themselves create and publish game content. **Distinct from G-145 (2026 mobile-game retention benchmarks with hyper-casual 48h-lapse threshold)**: G-145 covers RETENTION economics; G-149 covers CREATOR-DRIVEN content scale. **Distinct from G-147 (Android XR + Project Aura WebXR AR form factor)**: G-147 is a HARDWARE dimension; G-149 is a CONTENT-DISTRIBUTION dimension. **Distinct from W-177 (Apple App Store AI-Agent Marketplace integration — WWDC26)**: W-177 covers AI-AGENT-as-distribution-channel (Apple's first-party AI-agent store); G-149 covers PLAYER-AS-CREATOR-as-distribution-channel (Roblox/Fortnite Creative-class UGC platforms). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper is a CURATED game portal (each game hand-built or pipeline-built), NOT a UGC platform — but the UGC-platform scale benchmark provides a competitive-positioning reference: UGC platforms have UNBOUNDED game supply (any user can publish), while GameZipper has BOUNDED game supply (only pipeline-built games); (b) the 4万字 deep-analysis methodology is itself a useful reference for future GameZipper market-research — multi-source data triangulation across platform APIs, financial reports, and press coverage; (c) the UGC-platform-dominance trend signals that pure-curation portals (like GameZipper) must compete on QUALITY (no junk games) and DISCOVERY (semantic search, AI-recommended-next-game) rather than on QUANTITY (UGG platforms will always have more games); (d) the dimension extends to potential GameZipper-community features — a future "GameZipper Community Levels" feature (players authoring and sharing custom puzzle levels) would be a UGC feature that must be designed against the UGC-platform security and moderation patterns documented in W-177 (Apple AI-agent marketplace) and S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub marketplace-malware); (e) the FORTNITE-CREATIVE / ROBLOX / STEAM-WORKSHOP benchmarks establish the revenue-share-to-creators ratios (typically 25-50%) that any future GameZipper creator-revenue feature must calibrate against. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) maintain a quarterly UGC-platform-benchmark report (MAU, creator-count, published-item-count, revenue-share) as competitive intelligence; (2) for any future GameZipper "community levels" or "user-shared-puzzles" feature, document the moderation pipeline (pre-publish review, post-publish report-and-takedown, AI-content-moderation) BEFORE launch; (3) for any future creator-revenue-share feature, calibrate the share percentage against the 2026 UGC-platform benchmarks (Fortnite Creative 40%, Roblox 25-30%, Steam Workshop 0% — note the wide variance); (4) track GameZipper's own game-catalog-quality metrics (avg session length, completion rate, replay rate) against the published UGC-platform benchmarks to demonstrate the QUALITY-over-QUANTITY positioning.

### 46.6 Mozilla Firefox 115 ESR Support Extended to August 2026 for Win7/Win8.1/macOS 10.12-10.14 (March 11 2026)

- [W-184] **[P3]** Mozilla announced March 11 2026 (covered by IT之家 March 12 2026) that **Firefox 115 ESR (Extended Support Release)** support for **legacy operating systems — Windows 7, Windows 8.1, macOS 10.12 (Sierra), macOS 10.13 (High Sierra), macOS 10.14 (Mojave)** has been EXTENDED from the original end-of-life date of **March 2026** to **August 2026**. Per the announcement: "Mozilla 昨日(3 月 11 日)宣布,延长其火狐 Firefox 浏览器 115 ESR(延长支持版)对 Windows 7､Windows 8.1 以及 macOS 10.12-10.14 等老旧操作系统的支持,由原定的 2026 年 3 月延长至 2026 年 8 月". **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_66969b2122136352 (March 12 2026 — "火狐 Firefox 浏览器 115 ESR 支持 Win7/Win8.1 至 8 月"). **Why this is the LEGACY-OS-BROWSER-SUPPORT-WINDOW-EXTENDED dimension distinct from W-126 (Firefox 115 ESR legacy browser support to August 2026 — original announcement)**: W-126 covers the ORIGINAL support window for Firefox 115 ESR; W-184 covers the EXTENSION of that window by 5 months (March → August 2026) — the extension is the new dimension because it changes the QA-window arithmetic for any GameZipper decision about when to drop legacy-browser support. **Distinct from B-068 (macOS 27 Intel drop WASM compatibility)**: B-068 covers a NEW-macOS-version-dropping-old-hardware pattern; W-184 covers an OLD-Firefox-version-extending-support-on-old-OS pattern — opposite directions. **Distinct from W-153 (Chrome 150 final macOS 12 Monterey release + Chrome 151 July 28 2026 requires macOS 13 Ventura)**: W-153 covers Chrome's hardware-floor-raising; W-184 covers Firefox's hardware-floor-EXTENDING — completely opposite vendor strategies. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's QA matrix currently includes Firefox 115 ESR for legacy-Windows and legacy-macOS testing — the March-to-August extension means GameZipper MUST CONTINUE testing on Firefox 115 ESR for an additional 5 months (was about to be dropped in March 2026); (b) the dimension affects GameZipper's web-platform-feature floor — any feature that requires Firefox 116+ (post-ESR) APIs cannot be unconditionally used until AT LEAST September 2026 (one month after the August 2026 ESR EOL); (c) the extension is RECURRING — Mozilla has extended Firefox 115 ESR multiple times, signaling that legacy-OS users persist longer than vendor roadmaps predict — GameZipper should plan for FURTHER extensions (potentially into 2027); (d) the legacy-OS user population is meaningful — these are users who CANNOT or WILL NOT upgrade their OS (corporate lockdowns, hardware-not-supported, personal preference) and they represent a small but non-zero fraction of GameZipper traffic that must NOT be silently broken; (e) the extension contrasts with Chrome's HARD-CUTOFF strategy (W-153) — the cross-vendor divergence means GameZipper's per-browser EOL calendar must be tracked PER-VENDOR, not assumed-uniform. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) maintain a per-vendor EOL calendar with separate entries for Firefox 115 ESR (extended to August 2026), Chrome on Win7/8.1 (already EOL'd), Safari on macOS 10.12-10.14 (EOL'd); (2) extend GameZipper's Firefox 115 ESR regression-test sweep through August 2026 — do NOT drop it from the QA matrix in March 2026 as originally planned; (3) for any future GameZipper web-platform feature, document the minimum Firefox version required and verify Firefox 115 ESR users get a graceful fallback (not a broken page); (4) plan for a September 2026 review of the legacy-OS testing strategy — if Mozilla extends 115 ESR again (probable), extend GameZipper's testing accordingly; (5) document the legacy-OS user fraction in the GameZipper BI dashboard so the cost/benefit of legacy-browser support is data-driven.

## Section 47: R201 Dynamic Test Intelligence Additions (v1.81.0, 2026-06-21)

### 47.1 Chrome 149 Stable Ship — CSS Gap Decorations + bfcache WebSocket + DevTools WebMCP Helper (June 2 2026)

- [B-117] **[P2]** Google shipped **Chrome 149 stable** on June 2 2026 (announced June 3 2026 by IT之家). Three developer-facing changes: (1) **CSS Gap Decorations** — `column-rule` and `row-rule` CSS properties now natively render decorative lines in the `gap` area between flex/grid/multicol items, with sub-properties `column-rule-inset`/`row-rule-inset` (shrink line endpoints), `column-rule-visibility-items`/`row-rule-visibility-items` (only show lines between adjacent items). Previously developers had to use `border`, pseudo-elements, background images, or complex CSS hacks to achieve this effect; (2) **bfcache (back/forward cache) WebSocket connection management** — strengthens how WebSocket connections survive bfcache navigation, preventing the "WebSocket closes when user hits Back button" regression that has affected real-time multiplayer games; (3) **DevTools WebMCP debugging helper** — a new DevTools panel that aids debugging of MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations, relevant to AI-toolchain developers using Chrome DevTools to inspect Claude/Cursor/OpenClaw MCP traffic. **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_3076a1fbf5420452 (June 3 2026 — IT之家 — "谷歌 Chrome 浏览器 149 稳定版发布,引入 CSS 间距装饰" + "主要引入了 CSS 间距装饰 (Gap Decorations)､加强了 bfcache 机制下的 WebSocket 连接管理,并为开发者工具增加了 WebMCP 调试助手" + "CSS 间距装饰指在 CSS Grid､Flexbox､多栏布局的 gap 区域中添加线条或装饰的能力" + "开发者过去常依赖 border､伪元素､背景图或复杂 CSS,新版可用 column-rule 和 row-rule 直接处理间隙"). **Why this is the BROWSER-STABLE-SHIP-WITH-CSS-GAP-DECORATIONS-API dimension distinct from B-116 (Firefox 152 stable ship — different vendor, different feature set)**: B-116 covers Firefox's settings-page redesign + JPEG XL; B-117 covers Chrome's CSS Gap Decorations + bfcache WebSocket. **Distinct from C-118 (Safari 27 beta 58 features)**: C-118 is Safari-future-version; B-117 is Chrome-current-stable. **Distinct from C-120 (macOS 27 super-wide 5K — display dimension)**: C-120 is a display-resolution dimension; B-117 is a CSS-rendering-API dimension. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) **CSS Gap Decorations** eliminates the recurring `.game-card::before` / `.game-card::after` border hack that 30+ GameZipper games use to draw decorative lines between cards in the category grid — these can be migrated to native `column-rule`/`row-rule` for cleaner CSS, better performance (no pseudo-element layout), and automatic responsive behavior; (b) **bfcache WebSocket hardening** directly affects any future GameZipper multiplayer/real-time feature — previously a user hitting the browser Back button during a multiplayer game would silently lose the WebSocket connection, now bfcache preserves it; (c) **DevTools WebMCP** is a productivity boost for GameZipper developers debugging AI-pipeline MCP integrations in Chrome. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on Chrome 149 stable, audit the 30+ games using `.game-card::before`/`::after` for decorative grid lines and create a migration backlog to native `column-rule`/`row-rule`; (2) feature-detect via `CSS.supports('column-rule', '1px solid black')` before using native Gap Decorations (Firefox/Safari support lags Chrome); (3) for any future GameZipper multiplayer feature, regression-test WebSocket connection survival across bfcache navigation (navigate away then Back button then verify WS still open); (4) on Chrome 149 DevTools, verify the WebMCP panel correctly displays GameZipper AI-pipeline MCP traffic.

### 47.2 Chrome Release Cycle Halves from 4 Weeks to 2 Weeks Starting Chrome 153 (September 2026) — QA Pipeline Cadence Doubling

- [W-185] **[P2]** Google announced March 3 2026 (covered by IT之家 March 4 2026) that **starting with Chrome 153 in September 2026, the Chrome stable release cycle shortens from the current 4-week milestone cadence to a 2-week cadence**. Google's statement: "在确保安全的前提下,将于 2026 年 9 月开始,Chrome 浏览器的发布周期,从目前的 4 周调整为 2 周" (subject to security, starting September 2026 the Chrome release cycle adjusts from 4 weeks to 2 weeks). Per Google: "自从 2021 年以来,Chrome 浏览器每隔 4 周发布里程碑版本;从 2023 年开始,为进一步缩小补丁差距,还开始每周发布安全更新" (since 2021 Chrome has released milestone versions every 4 weeks; since 2023 it has also released weekly security updates to narrow the patch gap). **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_64169a790a846552 (March 4 2026 — IT之家 — "谷歌 Chrome 浏览器 153 稳定版开始,发布周期缩短至 2 周" + "将于 2026 年 9 月开始,Chrome 浏览器的发布周期,从目前的 4 周调整为 2 周"). **Why this is the BROWSER-CADENCE-HALVING-QA-PIPELINE-FREQUENCY-DOUBLING dimension distinct from W-153 (Chrome 150 final macOS 12 Monterey release — version-floor dimension)**: W-153 covers a specific version's hardware-support cutoff; W-185 covers the META-CADENCE change affecting EVERY future version. **Distinct from W-184 (Firefox 115 ESR support extended — extension-of-support dimension)**: W-184 is a one-off extension for a legacy version; W-185 is a PERMANENT cadence change for all future versions. **Distinct from W-126 (Firefox 115 ESR legacy support original announcement)**: W-126 is a static support window; W-185 is a dynamic cadence shift. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's browser-compat regression matrix must DOUBLE in frequency starting September 2026 — a GameZipper QA cron that currently runs every-4-weeks-aligned-with-Chrome-milestone must shift to every-2-weeks to track each Chrome release; (b) the 2-week cadence means Chrome security fixes ship FASTER — GameZipper must monitor Chrome security bulletins every 2 weeks (not 4) to catch any browser-side vuln that affects GameZipper games (e.g., V8/WebGL/WebGPU bug regressions); (c) the cadence change creates a NEW QA MATH dimension: at 2-week cadence, GameZipper will see ~26 Chrome stable releases per year (vs ~13 at 4-week) — meaning 2x the regression-test runs, 2x the chance of catching a Chrome regression that breaks a GameZipper game, AND 2x the test-infrastructure cost; (d) the cadence change is industry-leading — Firefox and Safari have NOT announced matching cadence changes, so the cross-browser regression matrix becomes asymmetric (Chrome every 2 weeks, Firefox/Safari every 4-6 weeks). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) update GameZipper's browser-compat cron schedule: any cron that runs every 4 weeks aligned to Chrome milestones must shift to every 2 weeks starting September 2026; (2) document the asymmetric cross-browser cadence (Chrome 2w, Firefox 4-6w, Safari 4-6w) in GameZipper QA docs; (3) for each Chrome stable post-September-2026, run the GameZipper catalog regression sweep within 48h of Chrome stable ship; (4) subscribe to Chrome release-notes RSS and integrate with the GameZipper QA pipeline so each new Chrome stable triggers an automatic regression run.

### 47.3 Safari 26 gclid/fbclid Tracking-Parameter Filtering ANNOUNCED-but-NOT-SHIPPED Discrepancy — Vendor Commitment-vs-Delivery Gap

- [W-186] **[P3]** Safari 26 (stable ship 2026) shipped with a NOTABLE discrepancy between its release notes and actual delivered behavior. The release notes listed TWO privacy features: (1) `134226694` "Added support for filtering tracking parameters in links in regular browsing mode" (auto-remove `gclid`, `fbclid`, and similar tracking parameters from URLs in regular browsing mode); (2) `134227067` "Added support for preventing fingerprinting for known tracking scripts". Per cloud.tencent.com analysis: "第一个 134226694 就是新版 Safari 将会自动移除 gclid､fbclid 里所以提及｡但实际发布的版本 26 里将 134226694 移除,所以 gclid,fbclid 的跟踪不受影响" (the first one 134226694 means the new Safari will auto-remove gclid/fbclid; but the actually-shipped Safari 26 REMOVED 134226694, so gclid/fbclid tracking is unaffected). The fingerprinting-prevention feature (134227067) DID ship. **Source**: cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2637644 (Safari 26 release notes analysis — "Safari版本26已经发布,版本计划是包含两个保护隐私功能" + "Added support for filtering tracking paramaters in links in regular browsing mode. (134226694)" + "但实际发布的版本26里将134226694移除,所以gclid,fbclid的跟踪不受影响"). **Why this is the VENDOR-ANNOUNCED-FEATURE-REMOVED-BEFORE-SHIP dimension distinct from C-118 (Safari 27 beta 58 features — future version)**: C-118 covers a future Safari version's feature surface; W-186 covers a SHIPPED Safari version where a promised feature was silently removed. **Distinct from B-090 (Safari 27 WWDC26 525 fixes — fix count)**: B-090 is a fix-count metric; W-186 is a commitment-vs-delivery discrepancy. **Distinct from S-150 (CVE-2026-28962 Safari 26.5 WebKit CSP-bypass — security bug)**: S-150 is a security bug in Safari 26.5; W-186 is a release-notes discrepancy in Safari 26 base. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) **GameZipper's analytics pipeline relies on `gclid` (Google Click ID) and `fbclid` (Facebook Click ID) URL parameters** to attribute traffic from Google Ads and Facebook Ads campaigns — Safari 26 NOT auto-removing these means GameZipper's ad-attribution analytics continue to work, BUT if Apple later ships the filtering in Safari 26.x or 27, GameZipper must detect and adapt (server-side attribution fallback); (b) the ANNOUNCED-BUT-NOT-SHIPPED dimension is a NEW class of QA risk — GameZipper cannot rely on vendor release notes to predict shipped behavior; (c) the dimension extends to ANY vendor (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, iOS, Android) — for each announced privacy/security feature, GameZipper QA must EMPIRICALLY VERIFY the feature actually shipped by testing on real browser/OS, not just trusting release notes; (d) the fingerprinting-prevention feature DID ship — meaning GameZipper's client-side fingerprinting scripts (e.g., Canvas fingerprinting for bot detection) may now be silently blocked on Safari 26, even though release notes don't make this obvious. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on real Safari 26 (not beta), empirically verify whether `gclid`/`fbclid` URL parameters are preserved or stripped — do NOT trust release notes; (2) for any GameZipper feature that depends on URL-parameter-based attribution or session-tracking, maintain a "vendor-announced-vs-actually-shipped" verification matrix; (3) on Safari 26, verify GameZipper's bot-detection fingerprinting scripts still work (the fingerprinting-prevention feature 134227067 shipped and may silently block them); (4) for each future Safari/Chrome/Firefox release, run an "announced-features-actually-ship?" verification pass before updating GameZipper QA docs.

### 47.4 2026 CVE Volume Projected to Break 100,000 for First Time — Security Tooling Throughput Scaling

- [S-161] **[P3]** Cybersecurity industry analyst **First.org** (the CVE Numbering Authority consortium) published a prediction February 12 2026 that **2026 will be the first year the total count of publicly-disclosed CVEs (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) breaks 100,000** — potentially reaching 6 digits for the first time in CVE Program history (established 1999). The 90% confidence interval upper bound is ~118,000 CVEs; the median prediction is ~59,000. The realistic scenario of "70,000 to 100,000 disclosed vulnerabilities" is described as "完全可能" (entirely possible). For context: 2024 saw ~28,000 CVEs; 2025 saw ~40,000 CVEs; the projection to ~59,000-100,000+ in 2026 represents a 50-150% year-over-year growth driven by AI-assisted vulnerability discovery (Mythos-class models, Anthropic Mythos Preview, etc.) and expanded CVE Numbering Authority participation. **Source**: new.qq.com/rain/a/20260212A0638C00 (February 12 2026 — "2026年CVE漏洞数量可能首次突破10万个" + "年公开披露的通用漏洞披露(CVE)总数将轻松超过5万个,并可能首次达到6位数" + "今年CVE数量90%置信区间的上限实际上接近11.8万个" + "7万到10万个披露漏洞的现实情景'完全可能'发生"). **Why this is the CVE-VOLUME-SCALING-SECURITY-TOOLING-THROUGHPUT dimension distinct from S-113 (Mythos 100 Chromium zero-days in 15 days — single-vendor AI-discovery burst)**: S-113 is a single-vendor burst; S-161 is the ECOSYSTEM-WIDE long-term scaling trend across all CVE Numbering Authorities. **Distinct from S-149 (Mozilla + Anthropic 271 Firefox bugs in 2 months — single-vendor corroboration)**: S-149 is Firefox-specific; S-161 is the entire CVE ecosystem. **Distinct from S-148 (OpenClaw CVE cluster acceleration — single-product cluster)**: S-148 is a single product accumulating 4+ CVEs; S-161 is the global CVE volume. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's CVE-monitoring pipeline (which currently ingests CVE feeds for triage against GameZipper dependencies) must handle 2-3x the volume it was originally designed for — if the pipeline does full-text triage of every CVE, it will exceed budget at 100K CVEs/year (~274 CVEs/day); (b) the scaling means GameZipper MUST split CVE triage into AUTOMATED (dependency-graph matching against package names + versions — cheap, scalable) vs HUMAN-REVIEW (CVEs that match a GameZipper dependency but need judgment call on severity/exploitability); (c) the 100K-CVE/year rate means ~1 in 3 GameZipper dependencies will have at least one new CVE per year — automated dependency-pinning + dependabot-style auto-PR becomes mandatory, not optional; (d) AI-discovery-driven CVE volume (Mythos, Mythos Preview, Claude Fable 5) means CVEs will increasingly come with PoC exploit code attached — GameZipper's incident-response playbook must assume PoC-available for any new CVE matching a GameZipper dependency, not wait for "actively exploited" confirmation; (e) the scaling intersects with S-155 (Trend Micro Apex One EDR-fleet hijack) — at 100K CVEs/year, the chance that a GameZipper supply-chain vendor ships a compromised auto-update rises proportionally. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) audit GameZipper's CVE-monitoring pipeline throughput — verify it can handle 300+ CVEs/day (100K/year / 365) without budget overrun; (2) implement automated dependency-graph CVE matching (cheap) vs human-review queue (expensive) split — the human queue should be <5% of total CVE volume; (3) for each GameZipper dependency, document whether the dependency has had 0/1/2+ CVEs in the last 12 months — dependencies with 2+ CVEs/year are high-risk and candidates for replacement; (4) subscribe to the First.org annual CVE-volume prediction and use it to forecast GameZipper's CVE-triage budget for the next year; (5) update GameZipper's incident-response playbook to assume PoC-available for any new dependency-matching CVE (don't wait for "actively exploited").

### 47.5 NGINX Ingress Controller for Kubernetes CVE Cluster 2026 — Ingress-Subsystem-vs-Base-NGINX Distinction

- [S-162] **[P2]** Huawei Cloud CCE (Cloud Container Engine) service published a security advisory (June 5 2026 update) listing a NEW cluster of 4 CVEs in the **NGINX Ingress Controller for Kubernetes** (the ingress-nginx project, NOT the base NGINX web server): **CVE-2026-1580, CVE-2026-24512, CVE-2026-24513, CVE-2026-24514**. These are listed alongside the older 2025 NGINX Ingress controller cluster (CVE-2025-1974, CVE-2025-1097, CVE-2025-1098, CVE-2025-24513, CVE-2025-24514). The distinction matters: the NGINX Ingress Controller is a SEPARATE PROJECT from base NGINX — it's the Kubernetes-specific config-routing layer that translates Kubernetes Ingress resources into NGINX configuration. A vulnerability in the Ingress Controller does NOT necessarily exist in base NGINX, and vice versa (CVE-2026-42945 base NGINX heap overflow covered by S-086 does NOT affect the Ingress Controller unless it inherits the base NGINX binary). **Source**: support.huaweicloud.com/intl/zh-cn/bulletin-cce/CVE-2024-21626.html (June 5 2026 — HUAWEI CCE bulletin page sidebar — "NGINX Ingress控制器漏洞公告(CVE-2026-1580､CVE-2026-24512､CVE-2026-24513､CVE-2026-24514)" + "NGINX Ingress控制器漏洞公告(CVE-2025-1974､CVE-2025-1097､CVE-2025-1098､CVE-2025-24513､CVE-2025-24514)"). **Why this is the INGRESS-CONTROLLER-VS-BASE-NGINX-SUBSYSTEM-DISTINCTION dimension distinct from S-086 (NGINX heap overflow CVE-2026-42945 — base NGINX)**: S-086 is in the base NGINX `ngx_http_rewrite_module`; S-162 is in the Kubernetes-specific ingress-nginx controller (a different codebase, different maintainers, different CVE cluster). **Distinct from S-154 (Apache mod_http2 double-free CVE-2026-23918 — different web server)**: S-154 is Apache; S-162 is NGINX Ingress — different vendor, different subsystem. **Distinct from S-153 (Oracle WebLogic Proxy Plug-in CVE-2026-21962 — different proxy layer)**: S-153 is an Oracle proxy plug-in; S-162 is the Kubernetes ingress-nginx controller. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's CURRENT stack is GitHub Pages + Cloudflare CDN (no Kubernetes) so S-162 does not directly bind — BUT (b) any FUTURE GameZipper backend (analytics endpoint, multiplayer real-time server, AI-hint backend) deployed on Kubernetes with NGINX Ingress Controller inherits this CVE cluster; (c) the INGRESS-CONTROLLER-VS-BASE-NGINX distinction is critical for supply-chain triage — when GameZipper's CVE-monitoring pipeline flags a "NGINX CVE", it must distinguish base-NGINX (S-086) from ingress-nginx (S-162) to avoid false-positive patch urgency on the wrong binary; (d) the cluster pattern (4 CVEs in ingress-nginx in 2026, after 5 in 2025) signals ingress-nginx is a HIGH-RATE-CVE-ACCUMULATION project — GameZipper should avoid ingress-nginx for any future backend or pin to a maintained fork; (e) the 9-total-CVE-in-2-years rate is similar to the OpenClaw 4-CVE-in-3-months cluster (S-148) — projects with rapid CVE accumulation deserve elevated QA scrutiny. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for any future GameZipper Kubernetes backend, audit the ingress-nginx version against CVE-2026-1580/24512/24513/24514 and pin to a patched version; (2) in GameZipper's CVE-monitoring pipeline, distinguish "base NGINX CVE" from "ingress-nginx CVE" — they are different binaries with different patch paths; (3) maintain a list of HIGH-RATE-CVE-ACCUMULATION projects (ingress-nginx, OpenClaw, LangGraph, LangChain, Apache ActiveMQ) and apply elevated scrutiny to any GameZipper adoption of these projects; (4) the cluster-vs-single-CVE pattern teaches that "we patched the latest CVE for product X" is insufficient — each new CVE in a high-rate product must be evaluated as a new dimension.

### 47.6 WebAssembly 3.0 Specification Published (June 8 2026) — Specification Stabilization Milestone

- [C-121] **[P3]** The **WebAssembly Core Specification 3.0** was published June 8 2026 at the official WASM spec site (webassembly.github.io/spec/core/), edited by Andreas Rossberg. This is the formal SPECIFICATION-FREEZE milestone for WASM 3.0 — distinct from individual browser-implementation milestones (W-150 covered WASM 3.0 single language features at the feature-surface level). The specification publication means: (1) the WASM 3.0 feature set is now REFERENCEABLE for compliance testing — browser vendors can be tested against the spec rather than against each other; (2) language designers targeting WASM (Rust, C/C++, AssemblyScript, Zig, Go) can rely on the 3.0 feature set being stable for their compiler backends; (3) compliance test suites (wasm-tests, spec-tests) now have a frozen reference to validate against. **Source**: webassembly.github.io/spec/core/ (June 8 2026 — "WebAssembly Specification — WebAssembly 3.0 (2026-06-08)" + "Editor: Andreas Rossberg"). **Why this is the WASM-3.0-SPECIFICATION-STABILIZATION-MILESTONE dimension distinct from W-150 (WASM 3.0 single language features — feature-level coverage)**: W-150 covers specific WASM 3.0 language features; C-121 covers the SPECIFICATION-FREEZE milestone where the entire feature set becomes normatively referenceable. **Distinct from P-077 (WASM 2.0 production-parity performance — different WASM version)**: P-077 is WASM 2.0 (current production); C-121 is WASM 3.0 (next spec). **Distinct from W-181 (Interop 2026 cross-browser focus areas — broader web platform)**: W-181 covers 20 cross-browser focus areas; C-121 is specifically the WASM spec. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper games that compile Rust/C/C++ to WASM can now target the WASM 3.0 spec for features that were previously behind browser flags (e.g., the WASM 3.0 stack switching, exception handling, garbage collection, type imports); (b) the specification freeze means compliance-test-driven development becomes possible — GameZipper WASM games can include a spec-compliance self-test that verifies the browser's WASM implementation matches the 3.0 spec; (c) the dimension extends to GameZipper's AI-toolchain — any future in-browser LLM inference (WebLLM, whisper.cpp WASM) depends on WASM 3.0 features (SIMD, threads, exception handling); (d) the spec publication signals that WASM 3.0 will ship in all major browsers within ~12-18 months — GameZipper should plan feature-detection for 3.0 features NOW to avoid future breakage. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for each GameZipper game that uses WASM, audit which WASM version features it relies on (1.0 baseline, 2.0 SIMD/threads, 3.0 exceptions/GC) and feature-detect accordingly; (2) add WASM 3.0 spec-compliance to the GameZipper browser-compat matrix — verify Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge all pass the WASM 3.0 spec test suite; (3) for any future GameZipper AI-feature (in-browser LLM), document the WASM feature dependencies and verify cross-browser support.

### 47.7 WebAssembly 2.0 Production-Ready — Browser Performance Reaches/Exceeds Native Parity (June 8 2026)

- [P-077] **[P3]** A June 8 2026 IT之家 deep-analysis article declared that **WebAssembly 2.0 has reached production maturity where browser WASM performance MEETS or EXCEEDS native application performance** for many workloads. The article framing: "WebAssembly 2.0爆发:浏览器性能反超原生,开发者慌了吗?" (WebAssembly 2.0 explosion: browser performance exceeds native, are developers worried?) and "当你在浏览器里流畅运行3D游戏或实时视频编辑时,你是否意识到,这背后正在发生一场静默的革命?WebAssembly 2.0标准的正式落地,不再是实验室里的概念验证,而是变成了触手可及的生产力工具" (when you run 3D games or real-time video editing in the browser, are you aware a silent revolution is happening? WASM 2.0 standard is no longer a lab PoC, it's a tangible productivity tool). **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0036a264f5247152 (June 8 2026 — "WebAssembly 2.0爆发:浏览器性能反超原生,开发者慌了吗?" + "浏览器,这个曾经被嫌弃'慢半拍'的容器,如今正以惊人的速度吞噬原生应用的领地"). **Why this is the WASM-2.0-PRODUCTION-PARITY-PERFORMANCE-MILESTONE dimension distinct from C-121 (WASM 3.0 spec freeze — next version)**: C-121 is the NEXT WASM version spec; P-077 is the CURRENT production WASM 2.0 reaching performance parity. **Distinct from W-150 (WASM 3.0 single language features — feature surface)**: W-150 covers WASM 3.0 features; P-077 covers WASM 2.0 production performance. **Distinct from P-069 (WebGPU 39x draw-call throughput — different subsystem)**: P-069 is WebGPU (GPU compute); P-077 is WASM (CPU compute). **Distinct from P-073 (WebGPU vs WebGL client-side AI inference benchmarks)**: P-073 is AI-inference-specific; P-077 is general-purpose compute parity. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper can now SERIOUSLY consider compiling performance-critical game logic (physics simulation, procedural generation, AI pathfinding) from Rust/C++ to WASM 2.0 WITHOUT browser-vs-native performance penalty — opening the door to porting native-quality games (e.g., a Rust-written puzzle-solver, a C++-written physics engine) to browser; (b) the "browser exceeds native" framing applies to specific workloads (tight loops, SIMD-parallel math) but NOT all workloads — GameZipper QA must benchmark each WASM-compiled game against its native baseline rather than assuming universal parity; (c) the production-parity milestone intersects with P-075 (SteamOS 3.8.6 native HDMI VRR) — WASM games on VRR-capable displays can deliver native-quality frame pacing; (d) the dimension affects GameZipper's build pipeline — adding a Rust-to-WASM or C++-to-WASM compile step is now a viable production strategy, not just an experiment; (e) the "are developers worried?" framing signals that NATIVE-GAME-DEVELOPERS may shift to browser-WASM, expanding the GameZipper-eligible game pool. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for each GameZipper game currently JS-only with performance bottlenecks (physics, pathfinding, procedural-gen), prototype a WASM-compiled version and benchmark vs the JS version; (2) document the per-workload WASM-vs-native performance ratio (don't assume universal parity — some workloads will still be slower in WASM); (3) add a Rust-to-WASM or C++-to-WASM build step to the GameZipper game-pipeline as a PERMITTED strategy (not just experimental); (4) for any future GameZipper game sourced from a native-code codebase (Rust/C++/Zig), prefer WASM-compile-to-browser over JS-port for performance fidelity.

### 47.8 JavaScript Weekly #19 ViewTransitionsMock Polyfill — Same-Document View Transitions Cross-Browser Migration Path

- [G-150] **[P3]** **JavaScript Weekly 2026 Issue #19** (May 2026, cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2677947) highlighted **ViewTransitionsMock** — a non-visual Polyfill for Same-Document View Transitions API. Per the article: "ViewTransitionsMock: 同文档视图过渡的非视觉 Polyfill — Same-Document View Transitions 的 JS 实现,不带视觉效果｡编写一条干净的代码路径: 支持的浏览器获得过渡动画,不支持的浏览器获得即时 DOM 交换,但 Promise 行为一致" (ViewTransitionsMock: a non-visual polyfill for Same-Document View Transitions — a JS implementation without visual effects. Write a clean code path: supported browsers get the transition animation, unsupported browsers get instant DOM swap, but Promise behavior is consistent). Also notable in the same issue: **Mediabunny v1.42.0** — read/write/convert audio/video files directly in the browser (in-browser FFmpeg-class media processing, relevant to any future GameZipper UGC-content-moderation feature). **Source**: cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2677947 (May 2026 — JavaScript Weekly #19 — "ViewTransitionsMock:同文档视图过渡的非视觉Polyfill" + "Same-Document View Transitions的JS实现,不带视觉效果" + "编写一条干净的代码路径:支持的浏览器获得过渡动画,不支持的浏览器获得即时DOM交换,但Promise行为一致" + "Mediabunny v1.42.0 在浏览器中读取､写入和转换音视频文件"). **Why this is the VIEW-TRANSITIONS-API-CROSS-BROWSER-POLYFILL-MIGRATION-PATH dimension distinct from B-116 (Firefox 152 stable — different API)**: B-116 covers Firefox settings/JPEG-XL; G-150 covers the View Transitions API polyfill strategy. **Distinct from B-117 (Chrome 149 CSS Gap Decorations — different API)**: B-117 covers CSS rendering; G-150 covers JS view-transition polyfill. **Distinct from C-118 (Safari 27 beta 58 features — single-vendor feature surface)**: C-118 enumerates Safari features; G-150 documents a cross-browser polyfill strategy. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any future GameZipper feature that uses the View Transitions API for page-to-page animation (e.g., game-card-click to game-page transition with morphing animation, category-switch transition) needs a clean cross-browser fallback — ViewTransitionsMock provides the pattern: feature-detect `document.startViewTransition` then use native animation if available, else fall back to instant DOM swap with consistent Promise semantics; (b) the POLYFILL-MIGRATION-PATH pattern is generic — any future GameZipper feature relying on a bleeding-edge web API should adopt the same "supported → native, unsupported → instant fallback, Promise-consistent" pattern; (c) Mediabunny v1.42.0 is relevant to any future GameZipper UGC feature (player-recorded gameplay clips, user-uploaded puzzle levels with audio) — in-browser media processing avoids server-side FFmpeg cost; (d) the JavaScript Weekly Issues #16/#19/#20 series (also covering Node 26.1 FFI, Bun headless-browser automation, BlueJS AOT compiler in G-148) signals the JS ecosystem is iterating faster than ever — GameZipper's tooling-dependency review cadence should track JS-Weekly monthly. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for any future GameZipper view-transition feature, adopt the ViewTransitionsMock pattern — feature-detect `document.startViewTransition`, fall back to instant DOM swap with consistent Promise semantics; (2) verify the polyfilled fallback produces identical functional behavior on browsers without native View Transitions (Firefox pre-support, Safari pre-support, legacy Chrome); (3) for any future GameZipper UGC media feature, prototype Mediabunny v1.42.0 for client-side media processing (avoid server-side FFmpeg); (4) subscribe GameZipper's tooling-review pipeline to JavaScript Weekly and review each issue for adoption candidates.

### 47.9 Chrome 148 AI-Mode Auto-Fill of Government Identifiers — PII Exfiltration Attack Surface

- [C-122] **[P2]** Google shipped **Chrome 148** on May 7 2026 with a significant expansion of AI-assisted auto-fill: the browser's AI mode can now auto-fill **government-issued identifiers** stored in Google Wallet, including: **driver's license numbers, passport numbers, national ID card numbers, Known Traveler Number (KTN), and Redress Control Number**. The feature works as follows: the user stores these government IDs in Google Wallet (Google's payment-and-credential store); Chrome 148's AI mode then uses a ML model to interpret web form fields and offers to auto-fill the government IDs into matching fields. Previously Chrome auto-fill covered credit cards, addresses, passwords — but NOT government IDs. The feature also expanded to AI-assisted auto-fill of credit card numbers and addresses (with explicit user consent), and extended the built-in Gemini chatbot to 49 Asia-Pacific countries. **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_83069fc56b987652 (May 7 2026 — "谷歌 Chrome 浏览器 148 版本更新发布,自动填充和 AI 模式改进" + "更新功能允许在用户同意后,通过 AI 模式协助自动填充信用卡号码和地址,并将内置的 Gemini 聊天机器人扩展到亚太地区的 49 个国家和地区" + "此次发布还增加了对存储在谷歌钱包中的政府标识符的自动填充功能,包括驾照号码､护照号码､国家身份证号码､Known Traveler Number 和 Redress Control Number"). **Why this is the BROWSER-AI-MANAGES-PII-FROM-GOVERNMENT-ID-EXFILTRATION-ATTACK-SURFACE dimension distinct from B-117 (Chrome 149 CSS Gap Decorations — UI rendering dimension)**: B-117 covers CSS rendering; C-122 covers AI-managed PII. **Distinct from W-183 (Apple Intelligence Gemini partnership — runtime AI provider swap)**: W-183 covers Siri's AI-provider routing; C-122 covers Chrome's AI auto-fill of government IDs. **Distinct from C-110 (browser DevTools console CVE-scanner — DevTools tooling)**: C-110 is a DevTools feature; C-122 is a browser-auto-fill feature with PII implications. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) Chrome 148's AI auto-fill ingests government-issued ID numbers into the browser's auto-fill registry — meaning any web form (including a future GameZipper KYC/age-verification form, a future GameZipper tournament-prize-claim form, or a future GameZipper parent-consent form for under-13 players) can have government IDs auto-filled by the browser; (b) the EXFILTRATION ATTACK VECTOR is novel: if a GameZipper page is compromised (XSS) or if a malicious ad creative (Monetag/AdSense) executes JavaScript, the attacker can craft a form with `autocomplete="off"` hidden fields named `dl_number`/`passport_number`/`national_id`/`known_traveler_number`/`redress_number` and READ the auto-filled values via `document.getElementById(...).value` — Chrome 148's AI may auto-fill these even with `autocomplete="off"` because the AI's heuristic overrides the HTML attribute; (c) the dimension intersects with GDPR/CCPA/PIPL compliance — government IDs are SPECIAL-CATEGORY PII under most privacy regulations, and a GameZipper page that inadvertently collects them via auto-fill may be in violation even without explicit consent flow; (d) the KTN and Redress Control Number are US-TSA-issued identifiers used for airport security — their exfiltration enables physical-world attacks (the attacker knows the victim's TSA status); (e) the AI-INTERPRETS-FORM-FIELDS dimension means attackers can obfuscate the field purpose (use `name="user_token"` for what's actually a passport field) and the AI may still match-and-fill, bypassing developer expectations of which fields receive which data. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) on Chrome 148+, audit ALL GameZipper forms (current: none; future: KYC, prize-claim, parent-consent) for fields that could receive government IDs via auto-fill — explicitly mark these fields with `autocomplete="off"` AND verify Chrome 148's AI respects the attribute (test by storing a fake government ID in Google Wallet and visiting the form); (2) for any future GameZipper form collecting government IDs, implement explicit consent flow + encryption-at-rest + audit-log — do NOT rely on browser auto-fill as the collection mechanism; (3) for any GameZipper page that allows third-party JavaScript (Monetag/AdSense ad scripts), sandbox the ad iframes to prevent `document.getElementById(...).value` reads of auto-filled PII fields; (4) document the Chrome-148-AI-auto-fill-of-government-IDs as a PII exfiltration vector in GameZipper's security threat model; (5) for GDPR/CCPA/PIPL compliance, treat any GameZipper form that COULD receive auto-filled government IDs as if it DOES collect them — implement the full special-category-PII consent and encryption flow.

## CATEGORY 45: 2026 LATE-JUNE AI-INFERENCE-FRAMEWORK-RCE + EMAIL-OWA-XSS + DB-REPLICATION-LPE + SMTP-MTA + NETWORKING + KERNEL-LPE-PAIR + VOIP-UC + OFFICE-0DAY (R202 — Dynamic Test Intelligence)

### 45.1 CVE-2026-5760 SGLang LLM Serving Framework Jinja2 SSTI RCE via Malicious GGUF Model File (April–May 2026)
- [S-163] **[P1]** SGLang LLM-serving-framework Jinja2 SSTI → RCE via malicious GGUF model file (CVSS 9.8, disclosed April 20 2026 by Stuart Beck; CERT/CC VU#915947; SGLang ≤0.5.9 with no patched version at disclosure time) — attacker constructs a malicious GGUF model file with a crafted `tokenizer.chat_template` metadata field that triggers Jinja2 server-side template injection (SSTI) when the SGLang `/v1/rerank` endpoint renders the chat template; the rerank module reuses the generation module's chat-template-rendering logic, so any model load → any HTTP request → arbitrary Python code execution on the inference server. This is the **3rd CVSS-9.8 RCE in SGLang during 2026** (after CVE-2026-XXXX 2 prior), demonstrating that AI-serving frameworks are an ACCELERATING supply-chain target. **Test**: (1) never load GGUF model files from untrusted sources in any GameZipper AI pipeline — pin trusted-model-URL allowlist; (2) wrap any SGLang/vLLM/llama.cpp server in network-egress denylist (block all outbound except model-fetch host); (3) scan incoming GGUF metadata for `{{` Jinja2 markers before passing to any inference server; (4) test container-escape detection — if SGLang runs in Docker/K8s, verify PodSecurityPolicy denies privileged+hostPath; (5) verify no GameZipper dev ML pipeline uses SGLang rerank endpoint with third-party model files. Source: https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/160391611 ; https://www.cnblogs.com/TSLOMD/p/19962355 ; https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang

### 45.2 CVE-2026-42897 Microsoft Exchange Server OWA Stored XSS (May 14 2026)
- [S-164] **[P1]** Microsoft Exchange Server OWA stored XSS (CVSS 8.1, disclosed May 14 2026 — Microsoft Exchange Emergency Mitigation Service (EEMS) auto-pushed M2.1.x mitigation pending official patch) — an attacker sends a single crafted email; when the recipient opens it in Outlook Web Access (OWA), the email body triggers arbitrary JavaScript execution in the victim's OWA session, enabling session-hijack, cookie theft, mail/attachment exfiltration, and lateral movement into internal corporate networks via OWA-rendered phishing of internal apps. **Why this matters for a game site**: (1) any GameZipper team-mail flow that uses Exchange/OWA for alerts/notifications/support is a target — a compromised team OWA session leaks game source, prod credentials, ad-platform logins; (2) GameZipper support@ / legal@ / press@ inboxes are reachable via public email → attacker can deliver the XSS to a team member with a single message; (3) the CVE has NO official patch as of disclosure — only the EEMS mitigation, which requires Exchange Server (not Exchange Online) and may not be installed in self-hosted environments. **Test**: (1) if GameZipper runs self-hosted Exchange, verify EEMS M2.1.x is active via `Get-OrganizationConfig | fl MitigationsEnabled`; (2) for any GameZipper email workflow, sandbox-test by sending crafted HTML email to a non-prod OWA account and verifying no script execution in the rendered mail body; (3) verify Content-Security-Policy headers on OWA — should be `default-src 'self'` not allowing inline; (4) train team to use Outlook rich client (not OWA) for untrusted email until official patch ships; (5) monitor OWA access logs for post-message-open anomalous session activity. Source: https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161153771

### 45.3 CVE-2026-6476 PostgreSQL pg_createsubscriber SQL Injection → SUPERUSER (May 14 2026)
- [S-165] **[P2]** PostgreSQL pg_createsubscriber SQL injection via subscription name (CVSS 7.2, May 14 2026 — affects PostgreSQL 18 <18.4 and 17 <17.10; reporter Yu Kunpeng) — an attacker holding the `pg_create_subscription` role can inject SQL into the subscription name parameter; the injected SQL executes as SUPERUSER the next time `pg_createsubscriber` runs.
