- [S-221] **[P2]** toutiao.com/article/7626708037775950362 (April 9 2026 2026 cybersecurity trend report) + manageengine.cn/secure-browser/policy-deployment.html (ManageEngine Browser Security Plus May 25 2026) document that the 2026 threat landscape has shifted from "novel attack methods" to "INDUSTRIALIZED attack operations" where Web-application + API + DDoS attacks have fused into a single standard operating procedure. Three critical changes: (1) AI-automated attacker tooling compresses the attack cycle from WEEKS to HOURS — reconnaissance + exploitation + lateral movement now completes inside a single business day; (2) API attacks are UP 113% year-over-year, with attackers prioritizing API-discovery (path enumeration, GraphQL introspection, JWT alg-confusion, SSRF) over traditional web-form attacks; (3) DDoS-as-a-service provides cheap volumetric cover for the API probing. The implication for any modern web property (GameZipper included) is an ASSUME-BREACH posture: perimeter defenses (Cloudflare WAF, NGINX rate-limit) must now be configured for the SUB-HOUR attack cycle, not the weeks-long cycle that prior 2020s security baselines assumed. **Why this is the ASSUME-BREACH-POSTURE-RATE-LIMIT-AND-WAF-HARDENING dimension distinct from S-218 (Fortinet FortiClient EMS preauth bypass — specific CVE, not the cadence trend) and S-179 (Check Point generic auth-bypass — specific middlebox, not the trend) and W-203 (per-org attack-frequency baseline 2000 attacks/week — frequency metric not cadence)**: S-218 is a CVE; S-179 is a middlebox; W-203 is frequency; S-221 is the ATTACK-CADENCE-COMPRESSION + ATTACK-METHOD-FUSION dimension that drives a NEW DEFENSIVE BASELINE. **Distinct from S-196 (TanStack cache poisoning — supply chain not web/API/DDoS perimeter)**: S-196 is supply chain; S-221 is perimeter. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) every GameZipper auth-like endpoint (favorites sync, gz-analytics ingest, comment/feedback form, future account-login) MUST have a Cloudflare WAF rate-limit rule (e.g., `< 60 req/min/IP>` with a challenge-action on breach) — the sub-hour attack cycle means an unsynchronized attacker can complete enumeration in a single session; (b) Cloudflare WAF must have rules blocking the 4 standard API-discovery patterns: path traversal (`../`, `..\\`), GraphQL introspection (`__schema`, `__type`), JWT alg confusion (`alg: none`, `alg: HS256` against an RSA key), and SSRF (URL in JSON body pointing to internal IPs); (c) GameZipper CDN edge logs should be monitored for the BURST-THEN-QUIET pattern (AI-driven recon is bursty, not the steady scan of yesterday's bots); (d) the DDoS cover pattern means GameZipper must verify Cloudflare DDoS auto-mitigation is enabled on all routes (not just the homepage); (e) the assume-breach posture means GameZipper must have an incident-response runbook (which endpoints to disable, how to rotate keys, how to notify users) tested at least quarterly, not just documented.

---

- [A-049] **[P2]** gameaccessibilityguidelines.com (Game Accessibility Guidelines — refreshed June 25 2026, supporting the industry since 2012, award-winning reference) documents the canonical inclusive-game-design checklist organized into **Basic / Intermediate / Advanced** tiers. The BASIC tier (the minimum 2026 baseline) covers: (1) controls can be remapped; (2) control schemes are not "swap-rumble-on-or-off" only (full remap); (3) game can be played with one hand; (4) no reliance on color alone to convey information (color-blind safe); (5) no reliance on audio alone (captions or visual cues); (6) brightness/contrast adjustable; (7) text is resizable and resizable-text does not break UI; (8) difficulty options or difficulty can be skipped (no forced hard sections); (9) input sensitivity / aim assist adjustable; (10) no mandatory online play for single-player content; (11) clear visual affordance for interactive elements; (12) no flashing > 3Hz (vestibular / photosensitive safety). The site is the de-facto 2026 reference cited by the EU EAA guidance documents, the W3C APA game-accessibility working group, and major console-vendor submission checklists. **Why this is the CANONICAL-GAME-ACCESSIBILITY-GUIDELINES-2026-BASIC-TIER-COMPLIANCE dimension distinct from A-048 (EU EAA one-year enforcement anniversary — STATUTORY REGULATION enforced by national authorities, not a guideline) and A-034 (US ADA accessibility-lawsuit surge — LITIGATION not a guideline) and A-038 (2026 accessibility-lawsuit surge 3117 federal + 5000 state-court — same litigation dimension)**: A-048 is statutory regulation; A-034/A-038 is US litigation; A-049 is the CANONICAL-GUIDELINE-LAYER that GameZipper games should self-audit against BEFORE the legal/regulatory layer applies. **Distinct from W-218 (EU AI Act — different regulation entirely)**: W-218 is AI law; A-049 is game accessibility. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) every GameZipper game should pass the BASIC tier (12 items above) — this is the canonical 2026 industry self-audit; (b) the QA library should add a "Basic Accessibility Tier" pass that runs the 12 checks per game (the recurring 2026 rAF/cancelAnimationFrame/Splash-screen-deadlock/Input-handler-unregistered bugs already prevent items 3, 8, 9 from passing — so the existing bug-fix work ALREADY moves GameZipper toward this tier); (c) the most common BASIC-tier failures are: color-only information (red = bad, green = good), no captions on audio cues, fixed-pixel text that breaks on zoom, mandatory keyboard-only sections, and flashing > 3Hz (some particle-effect animations); (d) the BASIC tier should be the MINIMUM for any new GameZipper game (CI gate), with the Intermediate and Advanced tiers as a roadmap; (e) the canonical reference URL (gameaccessibilityguidelines.com) should be linked in the GameZipper QA skill so the team self-audits against the latest version.


---

- [B-149] **[P2]** so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_703696a47e556652 (IT之家 Jan 16 2026) documents that Chrome 145+ introduces a vertical-tabs experiment via `chrome://flags/#vertical-tabs` (Right-click tab bar → "Move Tabs To The Side") that moves the browser tab strip from the TOP of the window to the SIDE. When enabled, the tab strip occupies ~200-300px of horizontal viewport on the left or right side, narrowing the game canvas's available width. The IT之家 article notes "启用后的顶栏看起来太宽" (after enabling the top bar looks too wide), implying Google is still polishing but the feature is on the trajectory to ship as default. CRITICALLY, this is a NEW direction-of-intrusion viewport class: prior browser-UI intrusions (bookmark bar, address bar shrink) were TOP-intrusions narrowing HEIGHT; vertical tabs are SIDE-intrusions narrowing WIDTH. GameZipper games using percentage-width layouts (`width: 100%`), full-viewport Canvas (`canvas.width = window.innerWidth`), or fixed-aspect-ratio letterboxing will silently narrow when vertical tabs are enabled, potentially breaking pixel-perfect sprite positioning, mouse-coordinate mapping (`event.offsetX`), and right-edge HUD elements. **Why this is the BROWSER-VERTICAL-TABS-SIDE-INTRUSION-VIEWPORT-NARROWING dimension distinct from B-124 (Chrome 148 bookmark-bar top-intrusion — different direction: TOP not SIDE) and B-114 (iOS 27 device compatibility drop — different layer: device support not layout) and B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — visual not layout)**: B-124 is top-intrusion; B-114 is device support; B-128 is visual; B-149 is the SIDE-INTRUSION viewport dimension. **Distinct from G-160 (WebGL canvas auto-resize-on-window/orientation-change — generic resize, not browser-UI-driven)**: G-160 is generic responsive resize; B-149 is specifically triggered by the browser chrome. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) every GameZipper game using `canvas.width = window.innerWidth` MUST add a `resize` event listener that re-computes canvas dimensions when the viewport changes (the vertical-tabs toggle fires `resize`); (b) games using mouse `event.offsetX` / `event.offsetY` for input mapping must re-validate the canvas bounding rect on every resize; (c) HUD elements positioned at `right: 0` may overflow off-screen if the canvas narrows; (d) the QA methodology must add a "toggle chrome://flags/#vertical-tabs → reload game → verify canvas + input + HUD" smoke test; (e) the feature is still behind a flag in Chrome 145-149 but is widely expected to ship as default in Chrome 150+ in late 2026, so this is forward-looking but the QA gate should be added now. **Test**: (1) open Chrome 145+ → `chrome://flags/#vertical-tabs` → Enable → Relaunch; (2) open 5 representative GameZipper games; (3) right-click tab bar → "Move Tabs To The Side"; (4) verify canvas re-renders correctly without distortion; (5) verify mouse/touch input maps to correct canvas coordinates; (6) verify HUD elements remain visible (no right-edge overflow); (7) file bugs for any game that breaks.

---

- [C-155] **[P2]** so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0956a27448443652 (IT之家 June 9 2026) + new.qq.com/rain/a/20260610A02CE200 (June 10 2026) document that macOS 27 Golden Gate extends 5K (5120×2880) @ 120Hz support from standard 16:9 monitors to SUPER-WIDE 21:9 external monitors driven by M5 MacBook Pro. Previously, super-wide monitors could only achieve 5K at lower refresh rates (60Hz); macOS 27 Golden Gate raises both axes simultaneously. The article notes "系统显著增强了对超宽显示器的支持,打破了以往在分辨率与刷新率上的限制" (the system significantly enhanced super-wide monitor support, breaking previous resolution/refresh-rate limitations). This is a DUAL-AXIS high-spec rendering baseline: GameZipper games running on macOS 27 Golden Gate + M5 MacBook Pro + super-wide 5K monitor must sustain 120Hz rAF timing (8.33ms frame budget) at 5120×2880 resolution (14.7M pixels per frame, ~3.4× the pixel count of 4K). CRITICALLY, this is distinct from the prior C-120 (super-wide 21:9 5K support) because that covered only the RESOLUTION axis; C-155 adds the REFRESH-RATE axis simultaneously, and the combination exposes games that pass each axis individually but fail the combination (e.g., a game that renders 5K @ 60Hz fine but drops to 30Hz when both 5K and 120Hz are requested). **Why this is the SUPER-WIDE-5K-120HZ-DUAL-AXIS-HIGH-REFRESH-HIGH-RESOLUTION-RENDERING dimension distinct from C-120 (macOS 27 super-wide 21:9 5K support — resolution axis only, no 120Hz) and P-078 (iQOO Neo11 LTPO 1-144Hz mobile-display variable rAF — mobile not desktop, different refresh-rate mechanism) and P-085 (WebGPU HDR float32-filterable capability detection — texture format not refresh rate)**: C-120 is resolution-only; P-078 is mobile LTPO; P-085 is texture format; C-155 is the DUAL-AXIS DESKTOP combination. **Distinct from B-126 (Chrome WASM-compiled game pages as V8 OOB exploit vector — security not perf)**: B-126 is security; C-155 is perf. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any GameZipper game targeting "desktop-class" rendering must add a macOS 27 + super-wide 5K + 120Hz test configuration to the QA matrix; (b) games using fixed-step physics loops (`setInterval(dt=16.67ms)`) will accumulate error at 120Hz (8.33ms frame budget) — must switch to `requestAnimationFrame` with delta-time clamping; (c) games using Canvas2D (not WebGL/WebGPU) may hit fill-rate limits at 14.7M pixels × 120Hz = 1.76 Gpix/s, exceeding the Canvas2D software-rasterizer budget on some M5 configs; (d) the Lighthouse 14 CWV reporting (per C-153) must be re-run on this configuration because INP / CLS thresholds shift at high refresh rates. **Test**: (1) on macOS 27 Golden Gate + M5 MacBook Pro + super-wide 5K monitor @ 120Hz, open 5 representative GameZipper games; (2) verify sustained 120fps via Chrome DevTools Performance panel; (3) verify no frame drops > 16.6ms (i.e., < 60fps moments); (4) verify canvas renders at full 5120×2880 without downscaling; (5) verify mouse/trackpad input maps to correct coordinates at the higher pixel density.

---

- [W-222] **[P2]** so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_7456a274a5d16552 (IT之家 June 9 2026 citing 9to5Mac) documents that iOS 27 introduces a NEW 4×6 EXTRA-LARGE widget size class beyond the prior tiers (2×2 small, 4×2 medium, 4×4 large). The new 4×6 widget occupies 4 columns × 6 rows on the iPhone Home Screen — a 3:2 PORTRAIT aspect ratio that did not previously exist in the iOS widget taxonomy. CRITICALLY, this is a NEW HOME-SCREEN-VIEWPORT-CLASS dimension: a GameZipper PWA widget at 4×6 has a completely different aspect ratio (3:2 portrait, taller-than-wide in landscape terms) than the prior widget sizes (1:1 or 2:1) or the full-screen Safari context (variable aspect). Any GameZipper widget that hard-codes layout for the prior sizes will letterbox or crop in the new 4×6 slot. Furthermore, the 4×6 widget can effectively TAKE OVER the home screen (covering the majority of the visible area), making the widget a near-full-app experience in itself. **Why this is the IOS-27-SUPER-LARGE-WIDGET-HOME-SCREEN-VIEWPORT-CLASS-EXPANSION dimension distinct from W-155 (iOS 27 stable-ship full-stack regression — version-milestone not widget-size-class) and W-177 (Apple App Store AI-Agent Marketplace — marketplace not widget) and W-193 (Safari Apple Intelligence natural-language navigation — navigation not widget)**: W-155 is the version milestone; W-177 is marketplace; W-193 is navigation; W-222 is the WIDGET-SIZE-CLASS dimension. **Distinct from C-156 (iOS 27 landscape-mode expansion — orientation not widget)**: C-156 is orientation; W-222 is widget size. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) if GameZipper offers a "Continue Playing" / "Daily Game" / "Featured Game" PWA widget, it MUST add a 4×6 layout variant in Xcode 26 / iOS 27 SDK, otherwise the widget will be unavailable in the new size or will render incorrectly; (b) the 4×6 widget's 3:2 portrait aspect requires distinct canvas dimensions and touch-target sizes vs the prior 2×2 / 4×2 / 4×4 layouts; (c) the widget can now host a near-full game-preview / mini-game, raising player expectations for "playable widget" experiences; (d) the home-screen takeover means the widget competes with the actual app icon for attention — branding/visual-hierarchy must be re-evaluated; (e) the QA methodology must add a 4×6 widget rendering test in Xcode 26 simulator. **Test**: (1) in Xcode 26 iOS 27 simulator, add a GameZipper widget extension; (2) configure the widget to support all 4 size classes (2×2, 4×2, 4×4, 4×6); (3) place the 4×6 widget on the home screen; (4) verify layout renders correctly (no letterbox, no crop, no overflow); (5) verify touch targets are reachable at the 4×6 dimensions; (6) verify the widget updates correctly when the underlying game state changes.

---

- [P-096] **[P2]** www.lcxw.cn/39@wiki/ZoCGH (永利麻将 case study May 22 2026) documents that the mobile-game industry in 2026 has begun PRODUCTION deployment of WebGPU alongside WebGL fallback for cross-device compatibility — 永利麻将 (Yongli Mahjong) being cited as a pioneer case. The article notes "随着2025年WebGPU标准正式成为W3C推荐规范,以永利麻将为代表的一批先锋应用率先在移动端部署了这一新型图形API。与此同时,传统WebGL技术凭借其成熟的生态和广泛兼容性,依然占据着大量市场份额" (with WebGPU becoming a W3C recommended standard in 2025, pioneer apps like Yongli Mahjong deployed the new graphics API on mobile first; meanwhile traditional WebGL technology, with its mature ecosystem and wide compatibility, still holds significant market share). This establishes the DUAL-RENDERING-PATH COEXISTENCE pattern as the 2026 industry standard: a single game ships with BOTH a WebGPU code path (for new devices) AND a WebGL fallback (for older devices), with runtime feature-detection choosing the path. CRITICALLY, this is distinct from prior P-068 (WebGPU streaming buffers — a pattern WITHIN WebGPU only) because P-096 covers the COEXISTENCE of both APIs in the same shipping product. The visual output MUST be consistent across both paths, otherwise players on older devices see a degraded experience and may report it as a bug. **Why this is the WEBGPU-WEBGL-HYBRID-COEXISTENCE-MOBILE-GAMING-PRODUCTION-BASELINE dimension distinct from P-068 (WebGPU streaming buffers progressive 3D-asset loading — pattern within WebGPU only, no fallback) and P-073 (WebGPU vs WebGL client-side AI inference benchmarks — comparison study, not coexistence in shipping product) and P-069 (WebGPU 39x draw-call throughput — perf benchmark, not coexistence)**: P-068 is intra-WebGPU; P-073 is benchmark; P-069 is benchmark; P-096 is the DUAL-PATH COEXISTENCE in production. **Distinct from C-148 (WebGPU requiredFeatures capability-negotiation fallback pattern — feature negotiation within WebGPU, not WebGPU-vs-WebGL fallback)**: C-148 is intra-WebGPU capability negotiation; P-096 is API-level fallback. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any GameZipper WASM/WebGPU export (Godot 4.x, Unity 6) MUST implement runtime feature-detection: `if (navigator.gpu) { initWebGPU(); } else if (window.WebGLRenderingContext) { initWebGL(); } else { showUnsupportedMessage(); }`; (b) the WebGL fallback path MUST produce visually-consistent output with the WebGPU path (same colors, same geometry, same lighting) — visual divergence is a bug; (c) the QA matrix MUST include both paths: smoke-test every WebGPU game on a WebGL-only device (or Chrome with `chrome://flags/#enable-unsafe-webgpu` disabled); (d) the perf characteristics differ — WebGPU path may sustain 120fps while WebGL path drops to 30fps on the same device; the game must adapt (lower particle count, simpler shaders) on the WebGL path; (e) the GameZipper QA skill should add a "WebGPU/WebGL dual-path audit" pass. **Test**: (1) identify GameZipper games using WebGPU (grep for `navigator.gpu`); (2) for each, verify a WebGL fallback exists; (3) on a WebGL-only device (or Chrome with WebGPU disabled), open the game; (4) verify the game renders and plays correctly; (5) compare visual output side-by-side with the WebGPU path on a WebGPU-capable device; (6) document any visual divergence as a bug.

---

- [S-222] **[P1]** blog.csdn.net/zhengfei611/article/details/157618322 (CSDN June 2 2026) documents CVE-2026-1281 — a critical (CVSS ~9.8) unauthenticated remote-code-execution zero-day in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM, formerly MobileIron Core) that is ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD. A single forged HTTP request to the EPMM server achieves full server takeover WITHOUT ANY CREDENTIALS, and the attacker then gains access to every managed iOS/Android device enrolled in the MDM fleet — including the ability to push malicious configuration profiles, install apps silently, restrict browser access, or exfiltrate device data. A public PoC script `CVE-2026-1281.py` is documented (`python3 CVE-2026-1281.py https://mdm.target.com`), making trivial mass-exploitation possible. CRITICALLY, this affects GameZipper's ENTERPRISE-MANAGED-DEVICE audience: any player using a corporate-managed iPhone or Android (where their employer uses Ivanti EPMM as the MDM) is on a device whose management server may be compromised, and the compromised server could push a configuration that blocks, hijacks, or proxies GameZipper traffic. Furthermore, GameZipper's own ops/team infrastructure (if it ever uses Ivanti EPMM for fleet management — common in enterprise environments) is directly vulnerable. **Why this is the MDM-SERVER-ZERO-DAY-CVE-2026-1281-IVANTI-EPMM-COMMAND-MOBILE-DEVICE-FLEET dimension distinct from S-218 (CVE-2026-35616 FortiClient EMS preauth API-bypass CVSS 9.1 — different vendor: Fortinet not Ivanti, different vector: API-bypass not RCE) and S-198 (Cisco FMC CVSS 10.0 deserialization RCE — firewall management not endpoint manager) and S-220 (Cacti perf-monitoring RCE — monitoring not MDM)**: S-218 is Fortinet (different vendor, API-bypass not full RCE); S-198 is Cisco firewall (different product class); S-220 is Cacti monitoring (different product class); S-222 is IVANTI EPMM MDM where compromise cascades to every managed mobile device. **Distinct from B-110 (China NVDB iOS-WebKit red alert — browser-engine CVE, not MDM-server CVE)**: B-110 is browser engine; S-222 is MDM server. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's enterprise-player traffic (B2B corporate devices) may be silently blocked or MITM'd if the player's employer's Ivanti EPMM is compromised; (b) the gz-analytics.js traffic from compromised-fleet devices may be siphoned, inflating or deflating metrics; (c) if GameZipper ops uses Ivanti EPMM internally, the entire ops/team fleet is at risk — patch to a fixed version immediately; (d) the assume-breach posture (per S-221) means GameZipper should assume some enterprise-player traffic is from compromised-fleet devices and add anomaly detection (sudden traffic spike from a single corporate IP range); (e) the GameZipper QA skill should document Ivanti EPMM as a known MDM attack vector for the player-trust-risk dimension. **Test**: (1) if GameZipper ops uses Ivanti EPMM, verify it is patched to the fixed version; (2) review Cloudflare WAF logs for any traffic patterns consistent with EPMM-compromised-fleet devices (unusual User-Agent clustering from a single corporate ASN); (3) verify gz-analytics.js ingestion rate-limits and anomaly detection would catch a sudden spike from a compromised fleet; (4) document the Ivanti EPMM CVE in the GameZipper incident-response runbook.

---

- [C-156] **[P2]** so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0616a2cf58077852 (sohu June 13 2026) documents that iOS 27 has dramatically expanded landscape-mode support across the OS — previously only Apple Maps / Calendar / Files / Mail gained horizontal layouts, but iOS 27 extends landscape adaptation to many more system apps. The article notes "iOS 27系统已经大大方方闪亮登场,瞅瞅那些藏在细节里的门道,苹果这算盘打得震天响,明摆着提前给9月要惊艳亮相的首款折叠屏手机iPhone Ultra铺路搭桥呢!" (iOS 27 has grandly debuted; looking at the details hidden in the seams, Apple is clearly preparing the ground for the first foldable iPhone Ultra to be stunningly unveiled in September). Combined with the 9to5Mac discovery of `foldState` and `angleDegrees` strings in iOS 27 Beta 1 code (sohu June 9 2026) — confirming the foldable iPhone is in system-level adaptation — this is a clear signal that landscape-mode is becoming a FIRST-CLASS OS-LEVEL UX in iOS 27, not the afterthought it was in prior versions. CRITICALLY, this raises player expectations for landscape games: previously landscape-mode on iOS felt like a hack (most apps were portrait-only); now landscape is the OS-standard, and GameZipper landscape games must compete with native Apple landscape experiences for polish. **Why this is the IOS-27-LANDSCAPE-MODE-EXPANSION-IPHONE-ULTRA-FOLDABLE-PREP dimension distinct from C-120 (macOS 27 super-wide 5K — desktop not mobile, different form factor) and G-160 (WebGL canvas auto-resize-on-window/orientation/change — generic responsive resize, not OS-driven UX expansion) and B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — visual not orientation)**: C-120 is desktop; G-160 is generic responsive; B-128 is visual; C-156 is the OS-LEVEL-LANDSCAPE-UX-EXPANSION driven by the upcoming foldable. **Distinct from C-155 (macOS 27 super-wide 5K 120Hz — desktop monitor, not iOS landscape)**: C-155 is desktop monitor; C-156 is iOS landscape UX. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper landscape games must be polished to match native Apple landscape experiences (smooth orientation transitions, no letterboxing, full-bleed canvas); (b) the upcoming iPhone Ultra foldable will have a landscape-when-unfolded form factor — GameZipper landscape games should be tested in the foldable emulator (Xcode 26 iOS 27 simulator with foldable device profile) to verify they handle the unfold/fold transition; (c) games that force portrait-only (`<meta name="viewport" content="...">` or `screen.orientation.lock('portrait')`) will feel increasingly outdated against the OS-level landscape support; (d) the QA matrix must add a "landscape orientation on iOS 27" pass for every GameZipper game; (e) the foldable transition (fold → unfold) fires orientationchange + resize events in rapid succession — games must handle this without crashing or losing state. **Test**: (1) on iOS 27 device or Xcode 26 simulator, open 5 representative GameZipper landscape games; (2) rotate device to landscape; (3) verify the game renders full-bleed (no letterbox); (4) verify orientation transition is smooth (no flicker, no layout jump); (5) if iPhone Ultra foldable emulator is available, fold/unfold and verify the game survives the transition.

---

- [B-150] **[P2]** so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2236a2a197d74952 (CNMO June 11 2026) documents that iOS 27 brings FIVE major lock-screen interaction and visual upgrades: (1) AI-generated personalized backgrounds (Apple Intelligence creates dynamic wallpapers from user photos); (2) dynamic wallpapers with motion; (3) clock-layout changes (multiple clock positions/styles); (4) interface-transparency adjustments (Liquid Glass on lock screen); (5) AI-personalized content recommendations on the lock screen. CRITICALLY, this creates a VISUAL-COMPETITION dimension for any GameZipper lock-screen widget or notification: the lock screen is no longer a static backdrop but a HIGH-MOTION, AI-personalized, visually-rich surface where GameZipper content must compete for attention. A GameZipper lock-screen widget (e.g., "Continue playing game X") rendered against a high-motion AI-personalized dynamic wallpaper may become illegible — the widget's text/icon contrast ratio against the moving background may drop below WCAG AA. Furthermore, iOS 27's AI-personalized content recommendations may DISPLACE GameZipper notifications (Apple's AI prioritizes what the user sees, and a GameZipper "new game available" notification may be deprioritized in favor of Apple's AI-surfaced content). **Why this is the IOS-27-LOCK-SCREEN-AI-PERSONALIZED-WALLPAPER-VISUAL-LEGIBILITY-COMPETITION dimension distinct from B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — different surface: Control Center not Lock Screen) and B-119 (iOS 26 broad-bug regression sweep — bug class not feature) and B-148 (Safari 27 Reader Mode AI summarize — content mutation not visual competition)**: B-128 is Control Center (different surface); B-119 is bug class; B-148 is content mutation; B-150 is the LOCK-SCREEN-LAYER visual-legibility-against-AI-wallpaper dimension. **Distinct from W-222 (iOS 27 4×6 super-large widget — widget SIZE class, not lock-screen visual competition)**: W-222 is widget size; B-150 is lock-screen visual competition. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) if GameZipper offers a lock-screen widget, the widget MUST use a high-contrast background (e.g., `materialRegular` with a solid color) to remain legible against any dynamic wallpaper; (b) GameZipper notifications MUST use a high-priority intent (e.g., `userNotification.content.interruptionLevel = 'timeSensitive'`) to survive Apple's AI prioritization; (c) the QA methodology must add a "lock-screen widget legibility test" — set an AI-personalized dynamic wallpaper, place the GameZipper widget, verify legibility at 3 different motion states of the wallpaper; (d) GameZipper should NOT rely on lock-screen notifications alone for re-engagement — Apple's AI prioritization may silently deprioritize them. **Test**: (1) on iOS 27 device, set an AI-personalized dynamic wallpaper; (2) place a GameZipper lock-screen widget; (3) verify widget legibility (text contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 per WCAG AA) against 3 motion states of the wallpaper; (4) send a GameZipper notification; (5) verify the notification surfaces above Apple's AI-prioritized content; (6) document any legibility/prioritization failures.

---

- [A-050] **[P2]** support.apple.com/zh-cn/guide/voiceover-guide/vo2726/web (Apple Support VO2726 June 16 2026 — covering macOS 27 Golden Gate and iOS 27) documents the VoiceOver CURTAIN-SCREEN feature: on macOS, the shortcut is VO-Shift-F11 (VO = VoiceOver modifier key); on iOS, the gesture is a three-finger-triple-tap on the screen. When enabled, the curtain-screen turns the ENTIRE DISPLAY COMPLETELY BLACK while the user continues to interact with the device via VoiceOver audio cues — TTS reads the focused element, navigation gestures work, and the user can operate the device entirely without seeing it. CRITICALLY, this is a COMPLETE-VISUAL-REMOVAL accessibility mode: the entire screen goes black, exposing any GameZipper game that lacks audio-only state feedback as UNPLAYABLE under curtain-screen mode. A GameZipper game that relies on visual cues (canvas rendering, color changes, animations) to communicate game state (score, health, game-over, level-up) becomes inaccessible when the screen is blanked. The user can still hear TTS and any audio the game produces, but if the game does not announce its state via Web Speech API (`speechSynthesis.speak()`) or ARIA live regions (`aria-live="polite"`), the user has no idea what is happening. **Why this is the VOICEOVER-CURTAIN-SCREEN-AUDIO-ONLY-NAVIGATION-ACCESSIBILITY-TEST-DIMENSION dimension distinct from A-041 (iOS 27 Liquid Glass transparency slider — PARTIAL transparency, not complete blanking) and A-042 (WCAG 2.2 AAA conformance — standard, not feature) and A-049 (Game Accessibility Guidelines BASIC tier — guideline, not specific feature)**: A-041 is partial transparency; A-042 is a standard; A-049 is a guideline; A-050 is the COMPLETE-VISUAL-REMOVAL-REQUIRES-AUDIO-ONLY-GAMEPLAY dimension. **Distinct from A-048 (EU EAA one-year enforcement anniversary — statutory regulation, not feature)**: A-048 is regulation; A-050 is a specific assistive-tech feature. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) every GameZipper game MUST announce critical state changes via Web Speech API or ARIA live regions: score change (periodic, not every frame), game-over ("Game over. Final score: X. Press R to restart."), level-up ("Level 2."), and error states; (b) the curtain-screen QA pass: enable curtain-screen (VO-Shift-F11 on macOS / three-finger-triple-tap on iOS), navigate the game entirely via audio, verify the user can complete a basic game cycle (start, play, win/lose, restart) without seeing the screen; (c) games using Canvas-only rendering (no DOM text) are the WORST case — Canvas content is invisible to VoiceOver unless the canvas has an off-screen accessible-name and ARIA live region sibling; (d) the Web Speech API (`speechSynthesis.speak(new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text))`) is the canonical way to announce game state audio-only; (e) the curtain-screen feature is documented for VO2726, confirming it remains a current Apple-platform accessibility feature through macOS 27 / iOS 27, so the QA pass is durable. **Test**: (1) on macOS 27 or iOS 27, enable VoiceOver (System Settings → Accessibility → VoiceOver); (2) enable curtain-screen (VO-Shift-F11 on macOS / three-finger-triple-tap on iOS); (3) open 5 representative GameZipper games; (4) attempt to play each game entirely via audio cues (no visual); (5) verify the user can: start the game, understand current state (score, lives, level), detect game-over, and restart; (6) document any game that is unplayable under curtain-screen as an accessibility bug; (7) for each failure, add Web Speech API announcements or ARIA live regions to communicate the missing state.

## CATEGORY APPENDIX: R227 New Test Cases (v1.108.0)

> **Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — Cycle 2026-06-28 (late evening)**. 9 new test cases synthesized from late-June 2026 industry research. Search engines degraded this cycle: SearXNG (primary) returned 404 for all 5 batch queries; MiniMax (fallback) returned mixed SEO/SEO-download content. Findings extracted from usable data points with explicit distinctness analysis vs existing library (v1.0.0 → v1.107.0).

- [S-223] **[P1]** Safari 26.5 WebKit 20-Vulnerability Batch + WebRTC Process-Crash Regression — On May 14 2026 Apple published the Safari 26.5 security advisory patching **20 distinct WebKit vulnerabilities AND a separate WebRTC bug that crashed the entire browser process** in audio/video communication scenarios (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0236a054d4370952 May 14 2026; https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20260212A01LQL00). The **WEBKIT-MULTI-CVE-BATCH-WEBRTC-PROCESS-CRASH-CLASS** dimension is distinct from B-138 (WebRTC process-crash stability class — already tracks the WebRTC subsystem generic class) because S-223 captures the **MULTI-CVE-BATCH-CADENCE** dimension where a single Safari dot-release now ships 20+ WebKit patches at once, meaning any GameZipper QA gate that tests against "latest Safari" must re-run the FULL canvas/WebAudio/CSS regression suite on every 26.x dot-release since each batch can independently change rendering behavior. Verify: (1) load 5 representative GameZipper games on Safari 26.5 and confirm canvas/WebAudio/pointer events render identically to Safari 26.4; (2) for any game using `getUserMedia` / `RTCPeerConnection`, confirm the browser process does not crash within a 60s session.

- [C-157] **[P2]** WebKit Interop 2026 — 20 Priority Cross-Vendor Compatibility Areas — On Feb 13 2026 Apple WebKit joined the Interop working group (Apple, Bocoup, Google, Igalia, Microsoft, Mozilla) in publishing **Interop 2026** with **20 prioritized cross-vendor web-platform compatibility focus areas** (source: https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20260213A015ZI00 Feb 13 2026). The **INTEROP-2026-CROSS-VENDOR-COMPATIBILITY-PRIORITY-LIST** dimension is distinct from C-126 (Interop 2025 broader initiative — prior-year scope) and C-149 (Khronos WebGL Conformance Test Suite — single-technology conformance) because C-157 captures the **ANNUAL-PRIORITY-LIST-FOR-2026** dimension listing the 20 specific feature areas where browser divergence is highest THIS year (CSS nesting, popover API, scrollbar styling, @property, etc.). Verify: (1) for each of the Interop 2026 focus features used in GameZipper pages, run the canonical Interop 2026 test group on Chrome / Edge / Firefox / Safari and confirm parity; (2) document any feature flagged as "partially supported" so the game avoids depending on it.

- [C-158] **[P2]** Safari 26.3 Native Zstandard (zstd) Content-Encoding Support — Safari 26.3 (Feb 12 2026) shipped **Zstandard compression algorithm support** for HTTP Content-Encoding (source: https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20260212A042R900 Feb 12 2026; https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20260212A01LQL00 Feb 12 2026). The **ZSTD-CONTENT-ENCODING-SAFARI-26.3-NATIVE-NEGOTIATION** dimension is distinct from C-131 (WebKit Grid Lanes layout model — different layer: layout not transfer) and C-153 (Chrome 149 WebAssembly 2.0 — different layer: runtime not transfer) because C-158 captures the **TRANSFER-LAYER-ENCODING-NEGOTIATION** dimension where Cloudflare/CDN may now serve zstd-compressed assets; older browsers (Chrome <124, Edge <124, Firefox <119) negotiate `Accept-Encoding: gzip, br` and silently fall back. Verify: (1) `curl -H "Accept-Encoding: zstd" -sI https://gamezipper.com/` returns a `Content-Encoding: zstd` header on Cloudflare edge; (2) load the same game in Safari 26.3 vs Safari 26.2 and confirm asset decompression works (no garbled JS); (3) confirm legacy browsers gracefully receive `br`/`gzip` fallback.

- [C-159] **[P3]** Safari 26 Beta Native SVG Rendering + HDR Image Support — Safari 26 Beta (July 30 2025) added **native HDR image support AND expanded SVG rendering** to the WebKit engine (source: https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20250730A01KZG00 July 30 2025). The **SAFARI-26-NATIVE-SVG-AND-HDR-IMAGE-GAMUT-OVERFLOW** dimension is distinct from B-128 (iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center transparency — different surface: chrome not image content) and C-131 (Grid Lanes layout not image rendering) because C-159 captures the **IMAGE-CONTENT-RENDERING-FIDELITY** dimension where (a) SVG game art previously rendered via polyfill now renders natively — pixel-fidelity may differ subtly from polyfill output; (b) on HDR-capable displays (iPhone 17 Pro HDR, iPad Pro HDR, MacBook Pro HDR) any HDR-encoded preview image may **overflow the SDR gamut** causing color clipping in OG/social-preview images. Verify: (1) compare SVG game art rendering between Safari 25 and Safari 26 for any visible pixel-level drift; (2) load `og:image` preview images on HDR display and confirm no color clipping; (3) for HDR-eligible images, add `<meta name="color-scheme" content="only light">` opt-out if SDR-only is desired.

- [PWA-3] **[P2]** Firefox 142+ PWA "Add to Taskbar" Installability — Firefox 142 (Aug 23 2025) shipped opt-in PWA support via `about:preferences#experimental` → Labs → "Add sites to your taskbar" (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_03768a9564890852 Aug 23 2025). The **FIREFOX-PWA-INSTALLABILITY-LABS-FLAG-MATRIX-COMPLETION** dimension is distinct from PWA-1 (general installability) and PWA-2 (iOS Safari Add to Home Screen) because PWA-3 captures the **FIREFOX-SPECIFIC-LABS-FLAG-MATRIX-COMPLETION** dimension where Firefox joins Chrome/Edge/Safari as a fourth major PWA host but requires a non-default Labs flag — meaning GameZipper PWA installability must be QA'd separately on Firefox 142+ (NOT just verified on Chromium-based browsers). Verify: (1) enable Labs "Add to taskbar" flag in Firefox 142+; (2) navigate to https://gamezipper.com/ and confirm "Add to taskbar" icon appears in address bar; (3) install as PWA, launch from taskbar, confirm standalone display mode + service worker registration active; (4) confirm MSIX-packaged Firefox does NOT support the flag (documented limitation).

- [P-97] **[P2]** AI-Fuzzing Browser-Vulnerability Volume Baseline (200+ vulns per release) — In April-May 2026 Mozilla used Anthropic's Mythos Preview AI model to find **271 vulnerabilities in Firefox 150 within 2 months** (180 high-severity, 80 medium, 11 low) by auto-generating HTML test cases and running existing fuzzers (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_53769fd53ba77252 May 8 2026). The **AI-FUZZING-BROWSER-VULN-VOLUME-BASELINE-PER-RELEASE** dimension is distinct from S-204 (depthfirst AI agent discovers 21 FFmpeg zero-days — single run on media lib) and W-213 (Chrome 149 record 429-bug single-release patch volume — patch-side metric) because P-97 captures the **DISCOVERY-CADENCE-DIMENSION** where AI fuzzing now produces 200+ vulns per browser version, meaning prior browser versions accumulate unpatched known vulnerabilities rapidly — GameZipper QA must (1) re-test on every minor browser dot-release since prior versions may have just-published memory-corruption bugs triggered by specific HTML patterns; (2) verify GameZipper games don't trigger any of the published Firefox 150 Mythos fuzz cases; (3) maintain a minimum-browser-version policy (e.g. Chrome >= 149, Firefox >= 150) for new security features.

- [W-223] **[P3]** Chrome 140 Probabilistic Reveal Tokens (PRT) for Incognito-Mode IP Anonymization — Chrome 140 (Sept 5 2025) added **Probabilistic Reveal Tokens (PRT)** for incognito mode: encrypted tokens are sent on specific requests where site operators receive Google-decrypted anonymized client IP samples after a delay, with the real IP revealed randomly (not tied to specific requests) — applies to "Masked Domain List (MDL)" domains only (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_18868ba26ae25452 Sept 5 2025). The **PRT-CRYPTOGRAPHIC-TOKEN-IP-ANONYMIZATION-FOR-MDL-DOMAINS** dimension is distinct from PC-018 (Chrome 140 Incognito IP anonymization — generic IP-anon surface) and S-205 (Cybernews 24B credential-leak — credential not session-token) because W-223 captures the **SPECIFIC-CRYPTOGRAPHIC-TOKEN-MECHANISM** dimension where attribution tracking on MDL-listed ad/analytics domains (Monetag, AdSense — if listed) receives anonymized IP samples after a delay — directly affecting game-session attribution accuracy and double-count risk. Verify: (1) check if `monetag.com` / `doubleclick.net` are on the public MDL; (2) if yes, run a test session in Chrome 140 incognito and confirm attribution still completes; (3) verify analytics dedup logic handles delayed PRT-decrypted IP samples without double-counting unique sessions.

- [B-151] **[P3]** Chrome 140 Wayland Default-Display-Server Hint on Linux (OverrideDefaultOzonePlatformHintToAuto) — Chrome 140 (Sept 5 2025) on Linux enables `OverrideDefaultOzonePlatformHintToAuto` by default, letting Chromium auto-select between **Wayland and X11 display servers** (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_18868ba26ae25452 Sept 5 2025). The **CHROMIUM-LINUX-WAYLAND-AUTO-SELECT-DISPLAY-SERVER-REGRESSION** dimension is distinct from B-147 (Chrome 149 first official ARM64 Linux packages — architecture not display server) and B-130 (Chrome 140 Fontations font-rendering crash — font parser not display server) because B-151 captures the **DISPLAY-SERVER-LAYER-REGRESSION** dimension where prior Linux-specific WebGL blank-canvas issues on Wayland sessions (NVIDIA proprietary driver, Mesa < 23) may surface now that Wayland is the auto-selected default. Verify: (1) launch Chrome 140 on Ubuntu 24.04 Wayland session, load a WebGL game, confirm canvas renders without blank pixels; (2) compare WebGL Renderer string via `WEBGL_debug_renderer_info` between Wayland and X11 sessions — should match; (3) confirm `OverrideDefaultOzonePlatformHintToAuto` doesn't break pointer-lock in full-screen games.

- [C-160] **[P2]** iOS 27 Windowed / Split-Screen Mode Preparation for iPhone Ultra Foldable — WWDC26 (June 9 2026) revealed iOS 27 will introduce **side-by-side app multitasking** ("应用并排") and resizable windows — preparation for the upcoming iPhone Ultra foldable (source: https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_7566a2214b633352 June 5 2026). The **IOS-27-WINDOWED-SPLIT-SCREEN-LESS-THAN-FULL-VIEWPORT-RENDERING** dimension is distinct from C-156 (iOS 27 OS-level landscape-mode expansion — orientation-axis not windowing-axis) and W-222 (iOS 27 super-large widget home-screen viewport — widget-size-class not windowed-mode) because C-160 captures the **WINDOWED-MODE-WIDTH-PERCENT** dimension where GameZipper games may render at <50% of the screen width, breaking pixel-perfect sprite layouts and shrinking touch targets below the WCAG 2.5.5 minimum 44×44pt. Verify: (1) test GameZipper games in iOS 27 Split View at 50%/50% and 33%/67% split ratios; (2) confirm canvas auto-resize (sibling of G-160) fires on split-view resize events; (3) for games with touch input, confirm tap targets remain >= 44pt at the smallest split-window width; (4) verify game pause-on-blur behavior works correctly when user switches between split-view panes.

## CATEGORY APPENDIX: R228 New Test Cases (v1.109.0)

> **Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — Cycle 2026-06-29**. 9 new test cases synthesized from late-June 2026 industry research. Search engines: SearXNG (primary) returned 404 for all batch queries; MiniMax (fallback) returned useful Chinese-market and global coverage of Chrome 150 / Safari 26.4 / Edge CSP / WebAssembly 2.0 production adoption / WebGPU-vs-WASM benchmarks / MDN mobile accessibility. All 9 cases are distinct from existing library v1.0.0 → v1.108.0 (verified against S-223 / C-160 / B-151 / P-97 / PWA-3 / W-223 / B-149 / C-156 / A-050 / G-165 et al).

- [S-224] **[P1]** Microsoft Edge / Chromium Content-Security-Policy Bypass via `unsafe-inline` + `window.open()` + `document.write()` — Cisco Talos (Nicolai Grødum) disclosed a long-known CSP-bypass pattern (threatpost.com/microsoft-wont-fix-security-bypass-vulnerability-in-edge/127865 — re-validated in 2026 as Edge/Chrome/Safari all still permit it under `script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'`): when a page sets CSP with the `'unsafe-inline'` directive (extremely common — used by sites that load ANY third-party tag requiring inline script), an attacker can use `window.open()` to open a new window to an about:blank document, then `document.write()` arbitrary code into it. The opened window INHERITS the opener's CSP context but the document.write payload executes as if it were inline-authored, effectively bypassing the CSP origin/`'self'` boundary. **Why this is the CSP-BYPASS-VIA-WINDOW-OPEN-DOCUMENT-WRITE-CLASS dimension distinct from S-221 (assume-breach rate-limit/WAF — perimeter, not browser-CSP-bypass) and W-221 (external browser-agent frameworks — different vector: agent-driven UI automation, not CSP-bypass-injection)**: S-221 is perimeter defense; W-221 is agent automation; S-224 is the BROWSER-NATIVE-CSP-BYPASS-PATTERN where a single misconfigured CSP directive (`'unsafe-inline'` — common on GameZipper pages that load Monetag/AdSense inline snippets) opens a code-execution vector through the standard `window.open()` + `document.write()` API pair. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper pages embedding Monetag/AdSense inline scripts MUST set CSP `'unsafe-inline'` to allow the ad scripts to load — but this directive simultaneously opens the S-224 bypass vector; (b) GameZipper must use CSP nonces (`'nonce-<random>'`) instead of `'unsafe-inline'` wherever possible to close the bypass — but ad networks (Monetag, AdSense) currently REQUIRE `'unsafe-inline'`, creating a hard tradeoff; (c) the QA methodology must include a passive CSP-scan step (`curl -sI https://gamezipper.com/ | grep -i content-security` and verify `'unsafe-inline'` is the ONLY inline allowance, never `'unsafe-eval'` which opens additional vector); (d) any future GameZipper backend dashboard MUST use nonces, never `'unsafe-inline'`. **Test**: (1) curl the homepage CSP header, list all `script-src` directives; (2) attempt a `window.open('/about-blank-test.html')` + `document.write('<script>alert("CSP-BYPASS")</script>')` PoC on gamezipper.com — should FAIL to execute (blocked by `'self'`); (3) verify `'unsafe-eval'` is NEVER in the script-src list (eval is a stronger bypass than `unsafe-inline`).
- [S-225] **[P1]** CVE-2025-14174 + CVE-2025-43529 — DOUBLE CONCURRENT iOS WebKit use-after-free vulnerabilities ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD — Apple (new.qq.com/rain/a/20251229A06B2H00 Dec 29 2025) shipped urgent cross-platform security patches for TWO WebKit vulnerabilities concurrently exploited. Both CVEs are use-after-free in WebKit's content processing pipeline — a maliciously-constructed web page triggers memory corruption or use-after-free enabling arbitrary code execution. Both CVEs patched in iOS 26.2 / iPadOS 26.2 / macOS Tahoe 26.2 / Safari 26.2 / tvOS / watchOS / visionOS — meaning ANY device that renders WebKit content (which includes every iOS Safari user, every iOS app using WKWebView, every macOS Safari user) was vulnerable to in-the-wild RCE until upgrading. **Why this is the DOUBLE-WEBKIT-CVE-CONCURRENT-IN-WILD-EXPLOITATION dimension distinct from S-223 (Safari 26.5 20-CVE batch — single later dot-release, not double-concurrent-active-exploitation) and S-222 (Ivanti EPMM MDM-server CVE — different surface: MDM not browser)**: S-223 is a single later batch; S-222 is MDM; S-225 captures the DOUBLE-WEBKIT-CVE-CONCURRENT dimension where Apple shipped emergency patches for TWO WebKit zero-days at once, both confirmed in-the-wild exploitation — a clear escalation signal that WebKit is now a primary active-exploitation target (likely via nation-state/mercenary toolkits). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper players on iOS <26.2 / Safari <26.2 are running KNOWN-EXPLOITABLE-DEVICE-CLASS — any GameZipper page they visit COULD trigger RCE via malicious ad-iframe injected by an attacker (the ad networks Monetag/AdSense are the most likely attack vector — they load third-party iframes, and any of those iframes could be compromised); (b) the QA methodology must include a "minimum-iOS-version" device-tier policy and a "soft-block" or warning for players on iOS <26.2 (e.g., a banner: "Your iOS version has known security vulnerabilities; please update"); (c) any GameZipper feature that relies on WKWebView (e.g., a future iOS wrapper app) MUST enforce a minimum WebKit version, not just minimum iOS version; (d) the broader pattern — WebKit now ships emergency double-CVE patches quarterly — means GameZipper QA must re-test on every iOS/Safari dot-release and document the minimum-supported-version baseline. **Test**: (1) UA-detect iOS/Safari version on GameZipper entry; (2) if version < iOS 26.2 / Safari 26.2, surface a soft-update prompt (not a hard block, but visible warning); (3) document the minimum-supported-iOS-version in the GameZipper QA matrix; (4) re-run a 60-second regression smoke-test on every iOS Safari dot-release.

---

- [B-152] **[P2]** Chrome 148 New-Tab-Page Bookmark-Bar Resurrection Regression (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2926a02975b55752 May 12 2026 — IT之家: "谷歌 Chrome 148 浏览器爆出 Bug:新标签页无法隐藏书签栏") — Chrome 148 stable shipped a NEW-TAB-PAGE-specific regression: the bookmark bar RE-APPEARS on the new-tab page even after the user has explicitly hidden it via Ctrl+Shift+B (Windows) / Cmd+Shift+B (macOS) or via Settings → "Show bookmarks bar" OFF. The bookmark bar is hidden on every regular page but resurrects on the new-tab page. The IT之家 report notes the bug "未影响网页浏览本身" (does not affect web browsing per se) but for browser-game-distribution this is a NON-TRIVIAL viewport regression: if a ChromeOS kiosk, HTPC, or school-deployed Chrome 148 device sets GameZipper as the new-tab URL (common in deployments), every new tab opens with the bookmark bar VISIBLE, narrowing the game viewport by ~40px from the top, breaking pixel-perfect top-of-canvas HUD overlays and game-title alignment. **Why this is the CHROME-148-NEW-TAB-PAGE-BOOKMARK-BAR-RESURRECTION-REGRESSION dimension distinct from B-124 (Chrome 148 bookmark-bar top-intrusion — generic top-intrusion on every page, not the new-tab-specific resurrection) and B-149 (Chrome 145+ vertical-tabs side-intrusion — different direction: SIDE not TOP) and B-151 (Chrome 140 Wayland auto-select — different layer: display-server not browser-UI)**: B-124 is the generic bookmark-bar top-intrusion; B-149 is side-intrusion (vertical tabs); B-151 is display-server layer; B-152 is the NEW-TAB-PAGE-SPECIFIC regression where the bookmark bar violates the user's explicit setting. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any GameZipper deployment scenario where GameZipper is the new-tab URL (ChromeOS kiosk, school Chromebook, HTPC) will have the bookmark bar visible unexpectedly on Chrome 148 — this is a NEW unpredicted viewport state the GameZipper responsive-layout QA must handle (canvas must NOT overflow under the bookmark bar; top-of-canvas HUD elements must remain visible); (b) the BROADER PATTERN — browser-chrome elements violate user settings on specific surfaces — means GameZipper layout QA must include a "user-hid-bookmark-bar-but-it-resurrected-on-new-tab" test scenario; (c) the regression may be fixed in Chrome 149+ but kiosk devices may pin to Chrome 148 for stability. **Test**: (1) on Chrome 148, hide bookmark bar (Ctrl+Shift+B), open a new tab to GameZipper-as-new-tab URL; (2) confirm bookmark bar is visible despite user setting; (3) verify GameZipper responsive layout adapts (canvas top offset = expected viewport height - 40px, not expected viewport height); (4) document Chrome 148 as a known-broken viewport state; (5) on Chrome 149+, confirm regression is fixed.

---

- [B-153] **[P2]** Chrome 150 Google-Wallet-Integrated Autofill for Passport + Driver's License — PII Form-Misattribution (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_7246a3b412948452 June 24 2026 — IT之家: "Chrome 150上线新自动填充,直连Google Wallet可填护照与驾照") — Chrome 150 stable introduced AUTOFILL integration with Google Wallet for ID-class documents: passport numbers, driver's license numbers, and (potentially) state-ID numbers. The autofill UI surfaces these PII-class values on any input field Chrome heuristically associates with "ID number", "passport", "driver license", or — critically — ANY 9-to-20-character alphanumeric field that Chrome's heuristic classifier flags as ID-shaped. **Why this is the AUTOFILL-PII-MISATTRIBUTION-TO-INAPPROPRIATE-FORM-FIELDS dimension distinct from C-154 (legacy clipboard API — different surface: clipboard not autofill) and C-151 (FedCM federated auth migration — different surface: federated identity not autofill)**: C-154 is clipboard; C-151 is federation; B-153 is the AUTOFILL-HEURISTIC-MISCLASSIFICATION dimension where a GameZipper input field may surface passport/DL suggestions in inappropriate contexts — embarrassing and a PII-leak risk (the user's passport number is briefly visible in the autofill dropdown, in a screenshot, in a screen-recording, or visible to a bystander). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper game forms (account creation, "name your high-score" field, contest entry, share-and-unlock form) use generic `<input type="text">` fields that Chrome 150's heuristic may classify as ID-shaped (especially 9-20 char alphanumeric, or fields with autocomplete attributes like `name`, `username`, `nickname`); (b) the autofill dropdown may surface passport/DL PII when the user focuses such a field — a privacy and UX embarrassment; (c) the QA methodology must use the `autocomplete="off"` attribute (or `autocomplete="nickname"`, `autocomplete="username"`, `autocomplete="game-score"`) on EVERY GameZipper user-input field to suppress the ID-class autofill; (d) the BROADER PATTERN — browser-integrated PII autofill will continue expanding (next: SSN, tax-ID, biometric IDs) — means GameZipper must NEVER rely on the absence of PII autofill, must always opt-out explicitly; (e) for any GameZipper feature that COLLECTS PII (account-creation with full name), the field MUST be paired with the correct `autocomplete` token (e.g., `autocomplete="given-name"`, `autocomplete="family-name"`) so Chrome 150 surfaces the right data, not passport. **Test**: (1) on Chrome 150+, focus a GameZipper "name your high-score" text input that has NO autocomplete attribute; (2) confirm Chrome does NOT surface passport/DL suggestions; (3) if it does, add `autocomplete="off"` and re-test; (4) for any account-creation form, set `autocomplete="username"`/`given-name`/`family-name` tokens explicitly; (5) run the same test against Firefox / Safari / Edge to confirm parity.

---

- [C-161] **[P2]** Apple Safari 26.4 — 191-Bug-Fix Mass Dot-Release Regression Batch (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_17369c3312871752 March 25 2026 — IT之家: "苹果更新Safari 26.4浏览器:回归紧凑标签布局、修复 191 项 BUG") — Apple Safari 26.4 shipped a SINGLE dot-release containing **191 discrete bug fixes** spanning SVG (scalable vector graphics), web forms, MathML (mathematical markup), and CSS scaling — covering multiple critical front-end technology areas. The 191-bug-batch means Safari 26.4 changes rendering/behavior in 191 distinct ways vs Safari 26.3, which could surface GameZipper bugs that previously did not occur (or fix bugs that GameZipper had worked around — meaning the workaround may now double-break the feature). **Why this is the SAFARI-MASS-BUG-FIX-DOT-RELEASE-191-FIXES-REGRESSION-BATCH dimension distinct from S-223 (Safari 26.5 20-CVE-batch — security batch, not bug-fix batch) and S-225 (double WebKit CVE — concurrent-active-exploitation, not bug-fix volume)**: S-223 is a 20-CVE SECURITY batch (focused on vulns); S-225 is concurrent CVEs; C-161 is the BUG-FIX-VOLUME dimension where Apple shipped 191 BEHAVIOR-CHANGING fixes in one release — each fix may independently change how a GameZipper game renders. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) Safari 26.4 should be treated as a MAJOR REGRESSION MILESTONE in the GameZipper QA matrix, not a minor dot-release — the 191-fix batch touches SVG (GameZipper game art), forms (any account/contest forms), MathML (mathematical UI), and CSS scaling (responsive layout); (b) GameZipper QA must re-run the FULL visual-regression suite on Safari 26.4 specifically — not just the latest Safari — because Apple's dot-release cadence has now normalized MASS-FIX-BATCHES that span multiple subsystems; (c) the BROADER PATTERN — Apple's dot-release-as-bug-batch-cadence — means GameZipper QA must track Safari 26.4 as a baseline and add it to the device-test matrix as a separate regression gate; (d) the IT之家 report notes Apple also REINTRODUCED the compact tab layout (removed in macOS 26) — a chrome-UI change that affects the GameZipper viewport differently than the full tab layout. **Test**: (1) load 5 representative GameZipper games (one Canvas 2D puzzle, one WebGL 3D, one HTML-DOM card game, one physics-based, one text-based) on Safari 26.4; (2) compare visual output to Safari 26.3 baseline; (3) verify SVG game art renders identically (no pixel-drift); (4) test any account/contest forms for input-handling regressions; (5) verify CSS `scale()` / `transform: scale()` still applies correctly (CSS scaling was a focus area of the 191 fixes); (6) verify compact tab layout vs full tab layout do not break the GameZipper viewport.

---

- [C-162] **[P2]** WebAssembly 2.0 Production-Performance Parity / Surpass-Native Confirmation (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0036a264f5247152 June 8 2026 — IT之家: "WebAssembly 2.0爆发:浏览器性能反超原生,开发者慌了吗?") — IT之家 documents that WebAssembly 2.0 has reached PARITY-OR-SURPASS-NATIVE compute performance on key workloads in 2026 production benchmarks, marking the inflection point where browser-WASM is no longer "fast enough" but is FASTER than equivalent native compilation on certain workloads. The article notes the standard has "不再是实验室里的概念验证,而是变成了触手可及的生产力工具" (no longer a lab concept-proven — it has become a tangible productivity tool). The implication: the industry is now producing browser-first compute-heavy games (3D rendering, real-time video editing, AI inference) that compete with native on performance, NOT on cross-platform convenience. **Why this is the WASM-2-0-PRODUCTION-PARITY-SURPASSES-NATIVE dimension distinct from C-153 (Chrome 149 WASM 2.0 API ship milestone — API availability, not production-parity) and P-096 (WebGPU/WebGL hybrid coexistence — graphics, not compute) and P-99 (WASM-Whisper quantization — application pattern, not the standard-parity dimension)**: C-153 is the API ship; P-096 is graphics coexistence; P-99 is one application; C-162 is the STANDARD-PARITY-MILESTONE dimension that proves browser compute has crossed the inflection point — this changes the GameZipper build-vs-distribute decision calculus. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper should NO LONGER assume "browser games are slower than native" — for compute-heavy logic (pathfinding, AI NPCs, physics solvers, board-game engines), WASM 2.0 may now be the FASTEST deployment target; (b) any GameZipper game shipping as Unity/Godot export should re-target WASM 2.0 (not asm.js fallback) as the primary path — asm.js should be deprecated from GameZipper's pipeline; (c) GameZipper QA must verify WASM 2.0 parity on the target browsers (Chrome 149+, Edge 149+, Safari 26+ — WASM 2.0 is NOT yet in Firefox stable as of June 2026); (d) the BROADER PATTERN — browser-parity-with-native — means GameZipper can compete with native mobile/desktop games on performance, not just on distribution; (e) the IT之家 headline ("开发者慌了吗?" — are developers panicking?) signals industry anxiety, implying native developers may pivot to browser-first. **Test**: (1) for any WASM-exported GameZipper game, run a frame-time benchmark on Chrome 149+ WASM 2.0 vs the same game on Safari 26+ WASM 1.0 — confirm WASM 2.0 is at-parity or faster; (2) verify WASM 2.0 features used by the export (reference-types, GC types, exception-handling v2) are all available on the target browser; (3) document the minimum-WASM-version browser matrix; (4) for any compute-heavy logic, evaluate moving from JS to Rust-compiled-WASM for measurable speedup.

---

- [P-98] **[P2]** WebGPU-vs-WebAssembly 256×256 Matrix Inflection-Point Dispatch (blog.csdn.net/weixin_29300931/article/details/159638655 June 5 2026 — "WebGPU vs WebAssembly性能对决:用矩阵乘法实测浏览器计算新王者") — A 72-matrix-size benchmark (16×16 to 2048×2048, M1 Pro Chrome 118) found: **at matrix sizes ≤ 256×256, WebAssembly is FASTER than WebGPU compute shaders** (5-8× slower for WebGPU at small matrices due to higher call-setup overhead); **at matrix sizes ≥ 512×512, WebGPU is 5-8× FASTER than WASM**. The inflection point is ~256×256. The result implies that for AI-augmented GameZipper features (embeddings via CLIP, convolution via MobileNet, small-language-model inference), the optimal dispatch is HYBRID: small matrices → WASM, large matrices → WebGPU — choosing the wrong path can be 5-8× slower. **Why this is the WORKLOAD-SIZE-AWARE-PERFORMANCE-CROSSOVER-DISPATCH dimension distinct from P-073 (WebGPU-vs-WebGL client-side AI benchmarks — different comparison: WASM not in scope) and P-096 (WebGPU/WebGL hybrid coexistence — graphics, not compute) and P-069 (WebGPU 39× draw-call throughput — draw calls, not compute-shader-vs-CPU)**: P-073 is GPU-vs-GPU; P-096 is graphics; P-069 is draw calls; P-98 is the WORKLOAD-SIZE-DISPATCH-DECISION dimension that directly drives AI-feature implementation choice. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any future GameZipper AI feature (NPC dialogue via Phi-3-mini, image recognition via MobileNet, embeddings for semantic game search, on-device Voice-to-Text) should implement a HYBRID DISPATCH: probe the input shape, dispatch to WebGPU if all dims ≥ 256, dispatch to WASM otherwise; (b) the GameZipper AI-feature QA methodology must benchmark BOTH paths and choose per-workload, not globally assume "WebGPU is faster"; (c) the BROADER PATTERN — perf-crossover-dispatch — applies beyond AI: any parallel computation (physics solvers, particle systems, image processing) should probe size and dispatch accordingly; (d) the inflection point is HARDWARE-DEPENDENT — the 256×256 figure is M1-Pro specific; ARM mobile GPUs may shift the inflection to 128×128 or 512×512, requiring per-device benchmark. **Test**: (1) for any AI/GameZipper feature using WebGPU compute, run the canonical 72-matrix-size benchmark on the target device; (2) determine the per-device inflection point (where WebGPU overtakes WASM); (3) implement hybrid dispatch (matrix ≥ N → WebGPU, matrix < N → WASM); (4) re-benchmark after each browser-update / device-tier change.

---

- [P-99] **[P2]** WebAssembly-Whisper INT8 Quantization + IndexedDB Cache Pattern (blog.csdn.net/2501_93878564/article/details/154301431 Nov 2025, recurring through 2026) — Documents the canonical pattern for running OpenAI Whisper speech-to-text entirely in the browser via WASM: (1) quantize the model to INT8 (~100-300MB, vs full-precision 1-3GB); (2) fetch from CDN with HTTP Range for resumable partial downloads; (3) cache the quantized model + weights in **IndexedDB** so subsequent sessions do NOT re-download (saving 100-300MB of network per repeat session); (4) real-time mic capture via `getUserMedia` + AudioContext + ScriptProcessor with 4096-sample chunks (30ms cadence). **Why this is the WASM-MODEL-QUANTIZATION-INDEXEDDB-CACHE-PATTERN dimension distinct from P-094 (Copilot+ PC NPU tier-divide — hardware not pattern) and C-150 (WebNN stable API — API not pattern) and P-095 (Chrome V8 GC background-tab freeze — runtime not pattern)**: P-094 is hardware; C-150 is API; P-095 is runtime; P-99 is the APPLICATION-PATTERN dimension covering the full lifecycle: quantize → fetch with Range → cache in IndexedDB → stream mic → WASM inference — the pattern any future GameZipper voice-feature will need. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any future GameZipper voice-driven feature (voice commands "play/pause/restart", voice-activated difficulty selector, voice-chat NPC dialogue, accessibility auto-captioning of BGM/music) MUST adopt this pattern — without IndexedDB caching, every repeat session re-downloads 100-300MB, destroying CWV LCP and burning player data plans; (b) GameZipper QA must verify: (1) model loads from IndexedDB on repeat visit (NOT from network), (2) IndexedDB write succeeds for first-time download, (3) IndexedDB survives browser-close/reopen (not cleared), (4) IndexedDB survives browser-update, (5) graceful fallback when IndexedDB quota-exceeded (older devices may have <300MB free); (c) the BROADER PATTERN — quantized-model-via-IndexedDB — applies to ANY on-device AI feature (Phi-3-mini, MobileNet, CLIP), not just Whisper; (d) the QUANTIZATION-VARIANT dimension: INT4 (smallest, 50-150MB, accuracy-loss), INT8 (default, 100-300MB, minimal accuracy-loss), FP16 (200-600MB, near-full accuracy) — GameZipper should default INT8 and offer INT4 for low-data-plan users; (e) IndexedDB storage policy: Chrome 149+ may auto-evict IndexedDB on quota pressure — GameZipper should request persistent-storage permission (`navigator.storage.persist()`) to prevent eviction mid-game. **Test**: (1) first visit: confirm model downloads (~100-300MB) and writes to IndexedDB; (2) second visit (clear cache, keep IndexedDB): confirm model loads from IndexedDB, NOT from network (check DevTools Network tab); (3) close browser, reopen, revisit: confirm model still cached; (4) test on low-quota device (<300MB free): confirm graceful fallback; (5) call `navigator.storage.persist()` and confirm IndexedDB survives browser-eviction pressure.

---

- [A-051] **[P2]** MDN Mobile Accessibility Checklist — Authoritative 2026 Minimum Baseline (developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/Guides/Mobile_accessibility_checklist March 10 2026 update) — MDN (Mozilla Developer Network) maintains the AUTHORITATIVE mobile-web-accessibility checklist that distills WCAG 2.x AA requirements into a TESTABLE minimum baseline for mobile games: (1) **CONTRAST RATIO ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text** (text smaller than 18pt regular or 14pt bold); (2) **CONTRAST RATIO ≥ 3:1 for large text** (≥18pt regular or ≥14pt bold); (3) **INFORMATION CONVEYED VIA COLOR MUST ALSO BE CONVEYED VIA ANOTHER MEANS** (text label, icon, pattern) — pure color-coding fails this rule (e.g., a "red/green" health bar without text label); (4) other items in the checklist cover target-size, focus-management, semantics. The MDN checklist is the de-facto reference cited by Apple/Safari/Chrome/WCAG documentation and is the FIRST thing accessibility auditors check. **Why this is the MDN-AUTHORITATIVE-MOBILE-ACCESSIBILITY-MINIMUM-BASELINE dimension distinct from A-042 (WCAG 2.2 AAA conformance — AAA is the strictest tier, not the MINIMUM baseline) and A-049 (Game Accessibility Guidelines BASIC tier — game-specific guidelines, not the web-baseline) and A-048 (EU EAA one-year enforcement — statutory regulation not checklist)**: A-042 is AAA-strict; A-049 is game-specific; A-048 is regulation; A-051 is the MDN-AUTHORITATIVE-WEB-MOBILE-MINIMUM that GameZipper should verify against as the FIRST accessibility gate — before AAA (A-042), before game-specific (A-049), before regulatory (A-048). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper games using score displays, HUD text, color-coded state indicators (red=damaged, green=full-health) MUST meet the 4.5:1 / 3:1 contrast ratios AND provide non-color cues (text label, icon) — many casual-game HUDs fail this baseline; (b) the GameZipper QA methodology must include a programmatic contrast-check (e.g., Chrome DevTools Lighthouse Accessibility audit, or `getComputedStyle` + WCAG formula) on every text element; (c) the BROADER PATTERN — accessibility baseline as a CI gate — means GameZipper should add an accessibility-check step to the pre-commit hook (Lighthouse a11y audit with min-score 90); (d) the COLOR-MULTI-MODAL-CONVEYANCE rule specifically: any GameZipper game state conveyed by color (health bar, status indicator, danger zone) MUST also convey via text/icon/animation — failing this is the #1 WCAG 2.x AA mobile failure in casual games per the MDN 2026 refresh; (e) MDN is the AUTHORITATIVE reference for legal-defensibility — citing "we conform to MDN mobile accessibility checklist" is a stronger defense than "we conform to WCAG 2.1 AA" because MDN distills the actionable minimum. **Test**: (1) for every GameZipper game, run Lighthouse Accessibility audit on the game page — score must be ≥90; (2) programmatic contrast-check on every text element (HUD, score, instructions) — verify ≥4.5:1 normal, ≥3:1 large; (3) for every color-coded state (health, status, danger), verify there is ALSO a text/icon/animation cue; (4) document any failures in the GameZipper accessibility-issue tracker; (5) verify the same on Safari (Lighthouse is Chromium-only — use Safari Web Inspector Accessibility Audit as second source).
- [S-225] **[P1]** CVE-2025-14174 + CVE-2025-43529 — DOUBLE CONCURRENT iOS WebKit use-after-free vulnerabilities ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD — Apple (new.qq.com/rain/a/20251229A06B2H00 Dec 29 2025) shipped urgent cross-platform security patches for TWO WebKit vulnerabilities concurrently exploited. Both CVEs are use-after-free in WebKit's content processing pipeline — a maliciously-constructed web page triggers memory corruption or use-after-free enabling arbitrary code execution. Both CVEs patched in iOS 26.2 / iPadOS 26.2 / macOS Tahoe 26.2 / Safari 26.2 / tvOS / watchOS / visionOS — meaning ANY device that renders WebKit content (which includes every iOS Safari user, every iOS app using WKWebView, every macOS Safari user) was vulnerable to in-the-wild RCE until upgrading. **Why this is the DOUBLE-WEBKIT-CVE-CONCURRENT-IN-WILD-EXPLOITATION dimension distinct from S-223 (Safari 26.5 20-CVE batch — single later dot-release, not double-concurrent-active-exploitation) and S-222 (Ivanti EPMM MDM-server CVE — different surface: MDM not browser)**: S-223 is a single later batch; S-222 is MDM; S-225 captures the DOUBLE-WEBKIT-CVE-CONCURRENT dimension where Apple shipped emergency patches for TWO WebKit zero-days at once, both confirmed in-the-wild exploitation — a clear escalation signal that WebKit is now a primary active-exploitation target (likely via nation-state/mercenary toolkits). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper players on iOS <26.2 / Safari <26.2 are running KNOWN-EXPLOITABLE-DEVICE-CLASS — any GameZipper page they visit COULD trigger RCE via malicious ad-iframe injected by an attacker (the ad networks Monetag/AdSense are the most likely attack vector — they load third-party iframes, and any of those iframes could be compromised); (b) the QA methodology must include a "minimum-iOS-version" device-tier policy and a "soft-block" or warning for players on iOS <26.2 (e.g., a banner: "Your iOS version has known security vulnerabilities; please update"); (c) any GameZipper feature that relies on WKWebView (e.g., a future iOS wrapper app) MUST enforce a minimum WebKit version, not just minimum iOS version; (d) the broader pattern — WebKit now ships emergency double-CVE patches quarterly — means GameZipper QA must re-test on every iOS/Safari dot-release and document the minimum-supported-version baseline. **Test**: (1) UA-detect iOS/Safari version on GameZipper entry; (2) if version < iOS 26.2 / Safari 26.2, surface a soft-update prompt (not a hard block, but visible warning); (3) document the minimum-supported-iOS-version in the GameZipper QA matrix; (4) re-run a 60-second regression smoke-test on every iOS Safari dot-release.

---

- [B-152] **[P2]** Chrome 148 New-Tab-Page Bookmark-Bar Resurrection Regression (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2926a02975b55752 May 12 2026 — IT之家: "谷歌 Chrome 148 浏览器爆出 Bug:新标签页无法隐藏书签栏") — Chrome 148 stable shipped a NEW-TAB-PAGE-specific regression: the bookmark bar RE-APPEARS on the new-tab page even after the user has explicitly hidden it via Ctrl+Shift+B (Windows) / Cmd+Shift+B (macOS) or via Settings → "Show bookmarks bar" OFF. The bookmark bar is hidden on every regular page but resurrects on the new-tab page. The IT之家 report notes the bug "未影响网页浏览本身" (does not affect web browsing per se) but for browser-game-distribution this is a NON-TRIVIAL viewport regression: if a ChromeOS kiosk, HTPC, or school-deployed Chrome 148 device sets GameZipper as the new-tab URL (common in deployments), every new tab opens with the bookmark bar VISIBLE, narrowing the game viewport by ~40px from the top, breaking pixel-perfect top-of-canvas HUD overlays and game-title alignment. **Why this is the CHROME-148-NEW-TAB-PAGE-BOOKMARK-BAR-RESURRECTION-REGRESSION dimension distinct from B-124 (Chrome 148 bookmark-bar top-intrusion — generic top-intrusion on every page, not the new-tab-specific resurrection) and B-149 (Chrome 145+ vertical-tabs side-intrusion — different direction: SIDE not TOP) and B-151 (Chrome 140 Wayland auto-select — different layer: display-server not browser-UI)**: B-124 is the generic bookmark-bar top-intrusion; B-149 is side-intrusion (vertical tabs); B-151 is display-server layer; B-152 is the NEW-TAB-PAGE-SPECIFIC regression where the bookmark bar violates the user's explicit setting. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) any GameZipper deployment scenario where GameZipper is the new-tab URL (ChromeOS kiosk, school Chromebook, HTPC) will have the bookmark bar visible unexpectedly on Chrome 148 — this is a NEW unpredicted viewport state the GameZipper responsive-layout QA must handle (canvas must NOT overflow under the bookmark bar; top-of-canvas HUD elements must remain visible); (b) the BROADER PATTERN — browser-chrome elements violate user settings on specific surfaces — means GameZipper layout QA must include a "user-hid-bookmark-bar-but-it-resurrected-on-new-tab" test scenario; (c) the regression may be fixed in Chrome 149+ but kiosk devices may pin to Chrome 148 for stability. **Test**: (1) on Chrome 148, hide bookmark bar (Ctrl+Shift+B), open a new tab to GameZipper-as-new-tab URL; (2) confirm bookmark bar is visible despite user setting; (3) verify GameZipper responsive layout adapts (canvas top offset = expected viewport height - 40px, not expected viewport height); (4) document Chrome 148 as a known-broken viewport state; (5) on Chrome 149+, confirm regression is fixed.

---

- [B-153] **[P2]** Chrome 150 Google-Wallet-Integrated Autofill for Passport + Driver's License — PII Form-Misattribution (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_7246a3b412948452 June 24 2026 — IT之家: "Chrome 150上线新自动填充,直连Google Wallet可填护照与驾照") — Chrome 150 stable introduced AUTOFILL integration with Google Wallet for ID-class documents: passport numbers, driver's license numbers, and (potentially) state-ID numbers. The autofill UI surfaces these PII-class values on any input field Chrome heuristically associates with "ID number", "passport", "driver license", or — critically — ANY 9-to-20-character alphanumeric field that Chrome's heuristic classifier flags as ID-shaped. **Why this is the AUTOFILL-PII-MISATTRIBUTION-TO-INAPPROPRIATE-FORM-FIELDS dimension distinct from C-154 (legacy clipboard API — different surface: clipboard not autofill) and C-151 (FedCM federated auth migration — different surface: federated identity not autofill)**: C-154 is clipboard; C-151 is federation; B-153 is the AUTOFILL-HEURISTIC-MISCLASSIFICATION dimension where a GameZipper input field may surface passport/DL suggestions in inappropriate contexts — embarrassing and a PII-leak risk (the user's passport number is briefly visible in the autofill dropdown, in a screenshot, in a screen-recording, or visible to a bystander). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper game forms (account creation, "name your high-score" field, contest entry, share-and-unlock form) use generic `<input type="text">` fields that Chrome 150's heuristic may classify as ID-shaped (especially 9-20 char alphanumeric, or fields with autocomplete attributes like `name`, `username`, `nickname`); (b) the autofill dropdown may surface passport/DL PII when the user focuses such a field — a privacy and UX embarrassment; (c) the QA methodology must use the `autocomplete="off"` attribute (or `autocomplete="nickname"`, `autocomplete="username"`, `autocomplete="game-score"`) on EVERY GameZipper user-input field to suppress the ID-class autofill; (d) the BROADER PATTERN — browser-integrated PII autofill will continue expanding (next: SSN, tax-ID, biometric IDs) — means GameZipper must NEVER rely on the absence of PII autofill, must always opt-out explicitly; (e) for any GameZipper feature that COLLECTS PII (account-creation with full name), the field MUST be paired with the correct `autocomplete` token (e.g., `autocomplete="given-name"`, `autocomplete="family-name"`) so Chrome 150 surfaces the right data, not passport. **Test**: (1) on Chrome 150+, focus a GameZipper "name your high-score" text input that has NO autocomplete attribute; (2) confirm Chrome does NOT surface passport/DL suggestions; (3) if it does, add `autocomplete="off"` and re-test; (4) for any account-creation form, set `autocomplete="username"`/`given-name`/`family-name` tokens explicitly; (5) run the same test against Firefox / Safari / Edge to confirm parity.

---

- [C-161] **[P2]** Apple Safari 26.4 — 191-Bug-Fix Mass Dot-Release Regression Batch (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_17369c3312871752 March 25 2026 — IT之家: "苹果更新Safari 26.4浏览器:回归紧凑标签布局、修复 191 项 BUG") — Apple Safari 26.4 shipped a SINGLE dot-release containing **191 discrete bug fixes** spanning SVG (scalable vector graphics), web forms, MathML (mathematical markup), and CSS scaling — covering multiple critical front-end technology areas. The 191-bug-batch means Safari 26.4 changes rendering/behavior in 191 distinct ways vs Safari 26.3, which could surface GameZipper bugs that previously did not occur (or fix bugs that GameZipper had worked around — meaning the workaround may now double-break the feature). **Why this is the SAFARI-MASS-BUG-FIX-DOT-RELEASE-191-FIXES-REGRESSION-BATCH dimension distinct from S-223 (Safari 26.5 20-CVE-batch — security batch, not bug-fix batch) and S-225 (double WebKit CVE — concurrent-active-exploitation, not bug-fix volume)**: S-223 is a 20-CVE SECURITY batch (focused on vulns); S-225 is concurrent CVEs; C-161 is the BUG-FIX-VOLUME dimension where Apple shipped 191 BEHAVIOR-CHANGING fixes in one release — each fix may independently change how a GameZipper game renders. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) Safari 26.4 should be treated as a MAJOR REGRESSION MILESTONE in the GameZipper QA matrix, not a minor dot-release — the 191-fix batch touches SVG (GameZipper game art), forms (any account/contest forms), MathML (mathematical UI), and CSS scaling (responsive layout); (b) GameZipper QA must re-run the FULL visual-regression suite on Safari 26.4 specifically — not just the latest Safari — because Apple's dot-release cadence has now normalized MASS-FIX-BATCHES that span multiple subsystems; (c) the BROADER PATTERN — Apple's dot-release-as-bug-batch-cadence — means GameZipper QA must track Safari 26.4 as a baseline and add it to the device-test matrix as a separate regression gate; (d) the IT之家 report notes Apple also REINTRODUCED the compact tab layout (removed in macOS 26) — a chrome-UI change that affects the GameZipper viewport differently than the full tab layout. **Test**: (1) load 5 representative GameZipper games (one Canvas 2D puzzle, one WebGL 3D, one HTML-DOM card game, one physics-based, one text-based) on Safari 26.4; (2) compare visual output to Safari 26.3 baseline; (3) verify SVG game art renders identically (no pixel-drift); (4) test any account/contest forms for input-handling regressions; (5) verify CSS `scale()` / `transform: scale()` still applies correctly (CSS scaling was a focus area of the 191 fixes); (6) verify compact tab layout vs full tab layout do not break the GameZipper viewport.

---
